publishedfiledetails.title: [B42/41] Fix BlowTorch & PropaneTank
publishedfiledetails.description:
[hr][/hr]
[h1]Supports Version 42 SP+MP and Version 41 SP+MP[/h1]

[hr][/hr]

Propane Torch and Propane Tank Fix!

Increases the propane torch capacity from 10 to 100.
Adjusts the propane tank so it can refill the propane torch 16 times.

Originally, a propane torch with a capacity of 10 could be refilled 16 times,
but when the capacity was increased to 100, it could only be refilled 1.6 times.
This mod fixes that issue.

Since the item stats of the propane tank itself are not modified,
this mod is compatible with other mods that change propane tank stats,
such as Real Metalworking.


Workshop ID: 3044705007
Mod ID: FixBlowTorchPropaneTank

publishedfiledetails.title: True Music Jukebox B42
publishedfiledetails.description:
This version of True Music Jukebox is exclusively for Build 42. If you are still playing Build 41 for the better gamepad support and multiplayer, [b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3118990099]please follow this link to True Music Jukebox B41.[/url][/b]

Are you tired of swapping cassettes and vinyls after every track? Is the effort to find batteries doing more damage to your sanity than the zombie apocalypse, itself? Well, now you can load hundreds of cassettes and vinyls from the infamous True Music into a handy multipurpose jukebox and dance until you're undead! Unfortunately, Build 42 does not yet have multiplayer, but we will look into that in the future.

1. [i]First[/i] [b]remove[/b] [i]and then[/i] [b]unsubscribe from[/b] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3059476842]the old version of True Music Jukebox[/url]! (!!!)
2. Get [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2613146550]True Music[/url] (and it's prerequisites)
3. Subscribe to some good True Music track packs.
4. Find or build yourself an epic jukebox (but don't forget electricity)!
5. Turn it on, fill it with cassettes and vinyls, and crank it up to ELEVEN! *

[h2]How to Use Jukeboxes[/h2]

To use a jukebox, simply interact with it once after finding or placing it in the world and turn it on. This will convert the jukebox into a container object. After that, you can move (or "load") True Music cassettes and vinyls into the jukebox. Once tracks have been loaded, you can play them using either any loaded track's inventory menu or the jukebox interaction menu. Powered jukeboxes will play continuously. You can create a queue of specific tracks that will play next in the loaded playlist, and you can lock the queue if you want to loop through a subset of loaded tracks.

Jukeboxes can hold ELEVENTY arbitrary Zomboid weight units (110 AZWU) of your finest tapes and vinyl records. There is no track limit.

By default, zombies will be attracted to the sound of the jukebox up to 50 squares away. The range at which zombies can hear a jukebox at full volume can be set using sandbox options.

* Jukebox sound is controlled by Ambient Volume in sound settings.

[h2]Jukebox Therapy and Nutrition[/h2]

Jukeboxes can be given the power to improve the moods and health of players who hear them in many ways; they can even quench your thirst if you want them to be that magical. These features are off by default; enable them in sandbox options if they are up your alley!

[h2]Jukebox Icons[/h2]

This mod features icons to help people navigate its menus nonverbally. All buttons still do what their button text says they will do; buttons that say "play", "enable", or "turn on" do exactly that. However, the icon for buttons that toggle things on and off will reflect the CURRENT state of that option, [b]not[/b] the state it will be in after it's pressed. We made this decision because we wanted the icons to appear "on" when the associated toggle is "on" - e.g., the Jukebox icon is green when it's on. This pattern can be inverted on our game options page.

[h2]Portable Jukebox Keys[/h2]

Want to remotely access a jukebox's playlist and queue? Simply enable portable jukebox keys via sandbox options, add a jukebox to your key ring via the jukebox's main menu, and open the context menu on your (or any) key ring to control the box at any range. You may rename jukeboxes to remember locations more easily. Controller input is supported via the in-game keyboard.

[h2]Range Options[/h2]

In sandbox options, you can determine the audible sound range of the jukebox (a special setting exists for zombies); note, however, that our option for players will only work if you use our custom sound mode (the default sound mode of this mod). If you are using Project Zomboid's 3D sound effects, however, be aware that we are not able to control the sound range; it will drop off in volume according to whatever is deemed realistic by Project Zomboid.

[h2]Jukebox Hero[/h2]

Want to get a jukebox as fast as possible? Just choose the Jukebox Hero trait at character creation, and you will spawn with a Jukebox Starter Kit for building your very own jam station! You will also learn a recipe for making jukeboxes out of scrap.

[h2]3D Audio Effects[/h2]

True Music boomboxes and cassette players use 3D audio effects that many people find annoying because they have excessive bias toward one ear, and that ear is often the wrong ear (it's based on player position, not head orientation). We do not like the sound of that audio, so we replaced it with something we like much better. [b]However[/b], you may enable that form of 3D audio using both game options (for the individual level) and sandbox options (if you want to force your entire server to use the same 3D sound as True Music). Just be aware that we do not control the audible range of that sound mode.

[h2]Multiplayer Sync[/h2]

We have spent many hours confirming that you will hear the same songs as other people, and tracks will change at [i]almost[/i] the same time. Feel free to contact us if you believe you have ideas that can significantly improve this aspect of the mod!

We have not seen any desync in testing lately; however, if for some unexpected reason a jukebox does become desynced for one or more players, there are two things that will likely fix this. If the data is only wrong clientside, reconnecting should fix the issue; if the jukebox data is somehow corrupted serverside, then picking up a jukebox and putting it back down will reset its data.

[h2]Game Options[/h2]

True Music Jukebox now has its own page in the Game Options menu, where players can control a wide range of settings from jukebox volume to hiding menu options.

[h2]Translations[/h2]

We want True Music Jukebox to be available to every player in every language! Please learn more via [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3118990099/4035852798885773535/]our permanent discussion about language support[/url].

[h2]Update Schedule[/h2]

We will push feature updates on [b]Sundays at 10 PM EST[/b] whenever those updates are ready.

[h2]Discord Server[/h2]

Come join [url=https://discord.gg/3a3dWFB6yw]the True Music Jukebox community[/url] on Discord! In the future, we plan to warn people there if we need to do an unscheduled update for any reason (such as bug fixes that we consider urgent). We are also able to respond to usage questions, bug reports, translation requests, and feature suggestions faster on Discord. Please join us!

[h2]Compatibility[/h2]

This mod was written and will continue to be improved with compatibility as a high priority. It was tested alongside hundreds of mods, and it was fully compatible with all of them. It also integrates smoothly with Lifestyle: Hobbies. If you have any information regarding a potential incompatibility, please visit us on Discord and ping us!

You can add this midsave. Starter kits and manuals will not spawn in places that the server has already initialized with items.

*** may have compatibility issues with other b42 mods as not everything has been tested together ***

[h2]Modpacks[/h2]

Please [b]read[/b] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3118990099/4139439021818677235/]our Modpacks discussion[/url].

[h2]Other Mods by Norby007[/h2]

See my other mods for mixed insanity:

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3042138819]FunctionalAppliances[/url] - Now with More Brews!

[h2]Other Mods by Burryaga[/h2]

Burryaga is a Wookiee. Check out [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/zendachi/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]his Steam Workshop[/url] for a variety of useful mods!

Workshop ID: 3398874593
Mod ID: TrueMusicJukebox

Workshop ID: 3398874593
Mod ID: TrueMusicJukebox

publishedfiledetails.title: ISyncYouSyncWeAllSyncForDeSync
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]ISyncYouSyncWeAllSyncForDeSync[/h1]
[i]Build 42 multiplayer desync fix — active position sync, dead reckoning, combat guard, ghost zombie remediation, vehicle animation sync, and threat learning. B42-first. SP + MP.[/i]

[h2]What It Does[/h2]
This mod goes beyond detection — it actively fights desync at every layer Lua can reach.

[h3]Active Position Sync (Dead Reckoning)[/h3]
[list]
[*]Server broadcasts authoritative positions for players, zombies, and vehicles
[*]Clients predict movement between updates using velocity extrapolation with exponential decay
[*]Position corrections are blended smoothly instead of teleporting (snap threshold configurable)
[*]Zombies within melee range (3 tiles) of any player get immediate priority sync every 2 ticks
[*]Closest zombies to players are prioritized — hard cap at configurable max (default 150)
[*]Combat exclusion zone: zombies within 4 tiles are NOT repositioned during active combat, preventing hit registration failures
[*]Vehicle angle interpolation using shortest-arc math for smooth rotation sync
[*]Vehicle light state sync (headlights, stoplights, lightbar mode)
[*]State delta detection — only broadcasts entities whose position/health/direction actually changed
[*]UDP chunking at 50 entities per packet to prevent buffer overflow
[/list]

[h3]Combat Guard[/h3]
[list]
[*]Detects ghost swings — melee attacks that don't connect due to zombie position desync
[*]After 3+ ghost swings in 8 seconds, requests the server to scan and kill ghost zombies
[*]Refunds stamina lost to swinging at desynced air
[*]Automatically recovers when hits start connecting again
[/list]

[h3]Zombie Remediation (Server-Side)[/h3]
[list]
[*]Server scans nearby zombies when combat failure is reported
[*]Kills ghost zombies (health <= 0 but not marked dead — still attacking you)
[*]Preemptive cull: removes ghost zombies in areas flagged by the threat memory system
[*]Rate-limited to prevent abuse (10s global, 15s per-player cooldown)
[/list]

[h3]Vehicle Remediation[/h3]
[list]
[*]Captures vehicle part condition snapshots before entering desync corridors
[*]Server can roll back vehicle damage that occurred during confirmed desync windows
[*]Snapshots auto-expire after 60 seconds to prevent stale rollbacks
[/list]

[h3]Vehicle Animation Sync[/h3]
[list]
[*]Syncs door and window animations between all clients
[*]Doors play instantly via playPartAnim
[*]Windows use predicted interpolation over time via setOpenDelta — smooth, no per-tick streaming needed
[*]Compatible with modded vehicles (Vanilla Vehicles Animated, Filibuster Rhymes, etc.)
[/list]

[h3]Threat Memory & Preemption Engine[/h3]
[list]
[*]Learns WHERE and WHEN desync happens — patterns persist across server restarts via ModData
[*]Records trigger conditions: zombie density, chunk transitions, player count, ping spikes
[*]Confidence scores grow with repeated incidents, decay when an area stays clean
[*]Preemption Engine checks every 3 seconds and acts BEFORE desync recurs in known problem areas
[*]Zombie threats: requests preemptive server cull or sends early warnings
[*]Vehicle threats: warns the driver and captures defensive snapshots
[*]Admin command to dump or clear the threat memory
[/list]

[h3]Detection & Monitoring[/h3]
[list]
[*]Zombie combat watchdog and vehicle travel watchdog
[*]Chunk-based hotspot tracker with decay and severity scoring
[*]Server health monitor with configurable sampling
[*]Position validator detects rubberbanding (3+ jumps > 3 tiles in 30 seconds)
[*]Chunk pre-warming: pre-loads chunks ahead of vehicle travel direction
[/list]

[h3]Sync Shield & Mitigation[/h3]
[list]
[*]Defers risky vehicle interactions while chunk streaming or sync health is unstable
[*]Vehicle queue guard clears queued timed actions during dangerous mitigation windows
[*]Rate-limited player and admin warnings when sync risk rises
[/list]

[h3]Admin Tools[/h3]
[list]
[*]Optional lightweight status HUD for current area risk and recent incidents
[*]Context menu: dump risk state, events, hotspots, review summaries
[*]Tag incidents for fairness review, mark manual hotspots
[*]Generate test events, toggle debug logging
[*]Dump or clear threat memory patterns
[/list]

[h2]Sandbox Settings[/h2]
Everything is configurable from the sandbox options menu — no server restart needed:
[list]
[*]Toggle each feature independently (Position Sync, Combat Guard, Vehicle Anim Sync, etc.)
[*]Tune sync intervals (player/zombie/vehicle broadcast ticks)
[*]Set max synced zombies, sync distance, snap distance
[*]Configure mitigation cooldowns and warning thresholds
[/list]

[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[list]
[*]Project Zomboid Build 42
[*]Singleplayer and Multiplayer (dedicated servers, hosted co-op)
[*]Every Java API call is pcall-wrapped — mod cannot crash your server or client
[*]If errors occur, falls back to vanilla sync behavior silently
[/list]

[h2]Install[/h2]
[list]
[*]Subscribe on Workshop
[*]Add ISyncYouSyncWeAllSyncForDeSync to the Mods= line in your server .ini
[*]Add the Workshop ID to the WorkshopItems= line
[*]Clients just need to subscribe — no extra setup needed
[/list]

Workshop ID: 3705645576
Mod ID: ISyncYouSyncWeAllSyncForDeSync

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42] MandoDB - Old Moodles Icons + Redrawn
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]B42.2.0 SUPPORTED[/h1]

[h1]IF YOU NEED ANY QUESTION/ NEED HELP :[/h1]
[h1]https://discord.gg/YkSBKXgMBj[/h1]
[hr][/hr]
[h1]CHANGES LIST :[/h1]
- All icons are from B41 or created by artists.
- The border has been changed to match B41.
- All icons have been re-centered.
[hr][/hr]
Hello, I made sure in this mod that no images/icons were created using AI as I had done before. You can thank [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198273896062/]Comrade / AC-18[/url] and ricottakitten for creating these amazing icons!

publishedfiledetails.title: VorpallySauced - Weapon Mastery System
publishedfiledetails.description:
[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/vps-banner.png[/img]

Every swing leaves an impression. Use the same weapon long enough and something [i]stirs[/i] within. It remembers your grip. It settles into the motion. It [b]changes[/b].

This isn't an upgrade system. It's a [b]bonding ritual[/b].

Kill enough zombies with the same weapon, and it earns a [b]Mark[/b]. Keep killing, and it forms a [b]Bond[/b]. Push past 200 kills, and it becomes [b]Blooded[/b] — fused to your history, carrying the weight of everything you've survived together.

[i]"Something stirred within it. I felt it before I saw it."[/i]

[hr][/hr]
[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/vps-tiers.png[/img]

Three tiers of awakening. Each upgrade fully repairs the weapon. The bond sustains it.

[i]"It stopped being a separate thing. It's just my arm now."[/i]

[hr][/hr]
[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/vps-header-traits.png[/img]

Your weapon develops a [b]character[/b] based on how you use it — nine stages from Dormant to Undying. A [b]modifier[/b] reflects your recent behavior: Nocturnal if you fight at night, Ravenous if you kill fast and constantly, Patient if you're slow and selective, Pristine if you never let it touch a whetstone. Change how you play, and the personality shifts over days.

Your tooltip reads: [b]Proven. Nocturnal. Hunting.[/b] That last word is the [b]mood[/b] — a live state that responds to time, weather, your blood, your stress.

[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/vps-traits.png[/img]

Modifiers aren't just flavor. A Nocturnal weapon fades your panic at night. A Patient weapon grants crit chance. A Scarred weapon gains durability on repair. Effects grow as the Stir deepens.

[hr][/hr]
[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/vps-header-journal.png[/img]

A worn notebook that tracks everything you've witnessed — every Mark, Bond, and Manifestation you've discovered, a persistent history of the weapons you've bonded with, and field observations from the Compendium volumes you've studied. Eight narrators. One phenomenon. A story that builds from curiosity to horror.

[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/vps-journal.png[/img]

[i]"The pages are mostly blank. A few nervous notes. Morrison wasn't sure what he was writing down."[/i]

[hr][/hr]
[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/vps-header-reforge.png[/img]

Blooded weapons survive the [b]reforge[/b]. Dismantle your Fire Axe, craft a nail-bat, saw off a shotgun — the soul lives in the blade. Reassemble or transform, and everything carries through: kills, Marks, Bonds, the weight of every night.

[b]Blooded-only.[/b] Lower impressions live in the vessel and are unmade when the form changes. The Bond must run deeper than the weapon's shape for the Stir to leap forms.

[hr][/hr]
[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/vps-header-firearms.png[/img]

Pistols, rifles, shotguns, SMGs — every firearm follows the same path. Your M9 becomes a [b]Steady M9 of the Arsenal[/b]. Your shotgun becomes [b]Blooded Savage Shotgun of Destruction[/b]. Fire rate, accuracy, magazine capacity, reload speed, recoil, noise, jam resistance, stagger — all shaped by the bond.

Works automatically with any weapon mod. Rain's Firearms, Brita's, whatever you're running — if PZ recognizes it as a weapon, VorpallySauced will bond with it.

[hr][/hr]
[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/vps-header-shards.png[/img]

[i]Something fell from the weapon. Sharp. Warm.[/i]

Found rarely in the world, Vorpal Shards reshape a weapon's marks and bonds. Three uses per shard. Old affix history is preserved in your journal.

[hr][/hr]
[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/vps-stats.png[/img]

Eight Compendium volumes scattered across Knox County document the phenomenon. Find them, study them, fill in Morrison's pages.

[b]Full sandbox control:[/b] kill thresholds, tier weights, trait notifications, feature toggles, global disable. Safe to add or remove mid-save.

[hr][/hr]
[h2]Compatibility[/h2]

Build 42.15.0+. Multiplayer server-authoritative and fully synced. Works with any weapon mod. TooltipLib recommended for the best tooltip experience.

[hr][/hr]
[h2]Pour One Out[/h2]

This mod is a love letter to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=647041145]Vorpal Weapons[/url] by [b]electroglyph[/b]. Back in the day it was [i]the[/i] mod for weapon progression. Then he caught a case, went away, and never came back. The mod died with Build 41.

This one is for you, electroglyph. The spirit lives on.

[i]Free my boy. His only crime was making bangers.[/i]

[hr][/hr]
[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/mod-tiles.png[/img]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]The Pact[/h2]

[i]Blood taught me your name.[/i]
[i]I practiced it in the dark.[/i]
[i]You slept. I whispered.[/i]

If you see electroglyph out there, tell him Dark Sauce says thanks.

Workshop ID: 3645781559

publishedfiledetails.title: Tooltiplib - Tooltip Framework for Modders
publishedfiledetails.description:
[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/tooltiplib-banner.png[/img]

Half the Workshop independently hooks ISToolTipInv.render to add content to item tooltips. Every one of them saves the original function, replaces it with their own, crosses its fingers, and ships. Load two together and it's a coin flip whose content shows up.

TooltipLib fixes this. One managed hook per surface. Mods register as providers instead of fighting over who owns the render function. Priority ordering, error isolation, automatic column alignment, two-tier caching. Install it and every tooltip mod works together — aligned, organized, conflict-free.

[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/tooltiplib-flow.png[/img]

[hr][/hr]

[b]For Players[/b]

Install it. If a mod lists TooltipLib as a dependency, install TooltipLib. No configuration. No load order fiddling. No compatibility patches.

Safe to add mid-save. Safe to remove mid-save. No persistent state. Writes nothing to your save file.

[hr][/hr]

[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/header-surfaces.png[/img]

[b]Item[/b] — Inventory tooltips. The one everyone fights over. Not anymore.
[b]Item Slot[/b] — Crafting panel slots. Item providers auto-apply here.
[b]World Object[/b] — Generators, fridges, rain collectors, containers — anything in the world.
[b]Skill[/b] — Character skill bars. Rich text.
[b]Vehicle[/b] — Mechanic overlay part tooltips. Rich text.
[b]Recipe[/b] — Build 42 crafting recipe side panel.

All six share the same context method interface. Write a provider once, point it at any surface.

[hr][/hr]

[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/header-formodders.png[/img]

One call. No hooking, no metatable juggling, no layout lifecycle.

[code]
require "TooltipLib/Core"

TooltipLib.registerProvider({
    id          = "MyMod_WeaponStats",
    target      = "item",
    enabled     = TooltipLib.Filters.weapon,
    cacheable   = true,
    description = "Weapon damage stats",
    callback    = function(ctx)
        ctx:addKeyValue("Damage:", "+15%", nil, TooltipLib.Colors.GREEN)
        ctx:addProgress("Mastery:", 0.75, nil, TooltipLib.Colors.GOLD)
    end,
})
[/code]

The framework handles the hook, the layout, the error handling, and the caching. Your callback adds content.

[b]Error isolation.[/b] Every callback runs inside pcall. Provider A fails, providers B through Z keep rendering. Circuit breaker benches repeat offenders after 10 consecutive errors. Cleanup phase runs unconditionally — if your preTooltip modified item state and your callback divided by zero, cleanup still fires.

[b]Filtering.[/b] 14 item filters, 3 world object filters, factory functions for custom cases, combinators (allOf, anyOf, negate). Every filter is pcall-hardened.

[b]Context methods.[/b] Ten common methods for all surfaces:
[code]addLabel  addKeyValue  addProgress  addInteger  addFloat
addPercentage  addSpacer  addHeader  addDivider  addText[/code]
Layout surfaces (item, itemSlot, object) add texture rendering. Rich text surfaces (skill, vehicle) add line-level formatting.

[b]5-phase lifecycle.[/b] preTooltip → callback → textures → postRender → cleanup. In order. Every time.

[b]Documentation Bundled w/ Modfiles[/b]

[hr][/hr]

[b]Multiplayer[/b]

Item, skill, vehicle, and recipe surfaces are fully client-side. Zero server traffic.

World objects use a request-response pipeline on dedicated servers. Client sends one request per object. Server reads whitelisted methods only — getters and boolean checks. No mutations, no arbitrary invocation. Responses cached client-side. Sandbox option EnableMPSync lets admins disable the pipeline.

Singleplayer and listen servers read directly. No network overhead.

[hr][/hr]

[b]Compatibility[/b]

Works alongside mods that hook ISToolTipInv.render directly — deferred mode detects the foreign hook and renders provider content below it. Works with Inventory Tetris, Equipment UI, and other UI-replacing mods. Multiple mods can bundle TooltipLib — version guard ensures newest copy loads, older ones skip silently.

[hr][/hr]

[b]Requirements[/b]
[list]
[*] Build 42.13.1+
[*] Safe to add or remove mid-save
[*] No persistent state, no save writes
[/list]

[b]Bug reports:[/b]
[code]
TooltipLib Version:
Game Version:
Mode: [SP / MP Host / Dedicated Server]
What happened:
Other tooltip mods installed:
[/code]

[img]https://pz-mod-images.us-southeast-1.linodeobjects.com/mod-tiles.png[/img]

Mod ID: TooltipLib

publishedfiledetails.title: Force Sprinters [42.16 Multiplayer]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]This mod forces sprinters in multiplayer[/h1]
Right now Sprinters are disabled in MP on purpose (not a bug). When forced they feel overall the same as it was in B41 (where sprinters also weren't officially supported in MP) which many will consider playable hence this mod exists.

Please report bugs in bugs discussion and not on the mod page itself. Updates happen on 5am or 5pm CST just as with my other multiplayer mods.

[h3]Important[/h3]
Vanilla game [b]Random Sprinter Amount[/b] Sandbox Option has to be set to [b]None[/b] (ZombieLore.SprinterPercentage=0)

[h3]Commong Sandbox Options for all modules[/h3]
[list]
[*]Choose whether zombies sprint the whole day or only during certain hours (default 24h)
[*]Choose if sprinters become shamblers during fog or rain (based on intensity, default never)
[/list]

[h3]"Force Sprinters" module info[/h3]
[list]
[*]5% by default, can be changed in Sandbox Options
[*]Certain outfits of zombies can be set to always be sprinters (i.e. "Spiffo" or modded zombies)
[/list]

[h3]"Force Sprinters - Locations" module info[/h3]
[list]
[*]Add-on for the main module which allows to choose sprinter % per location through Sandbox Options
[*]Has to be loaded after the main module
[/list]

[h3]"Force Sprinters - 100" module info[/h3]
[list]
[*]Enable only this module if you want 100% sprinters
[/list]

[h3]Notes[/h3]
[list]
[*]Bug/Feature: sometimes sprinters might sound like shamblers
[*]Sandbox setting change requires server restart to update existing zombies
[*]Sprinters in multiplayer can become very janky if they are targeting other players. This is probably the reason why they are disabled at the moment
[*]Special thanks to [b]@Girth[/b] and [b]@lect[/b] for help
[*]Can be added/removed to/from existing saves/servers
[*]If you want something more predictable give a try to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3665657529]Not Random Sprinters[/url]
[/list]

[h3]To modders[/h3]
[list]
[*]There's easy to override [b]SWFS_isSprinter(zombie)[/b] global function for add-ons support like the Locations module
[/list]

A thumbs up helps with the mod's discoverability, thank you if you left one
Check [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/somewhatfrog/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]my other mods[/url], maybe you'd like some of them

[code]Modpacking or reuploading is not allowed as long as the original version is present.[/code]

[url=https://discord.gg/SReMnbV4V7]PZ Modding Community Discord[/url]

Workshop ID: 3664955167
Mod ID: ForceSprinters
Mod ID: ForceSprinters100
Mod ID: ForceSprintersLocations

publishedfiledetails.title: PhunCure 2
publishedfiledetails.description:
Someone was on to a cure, now you just got to find it! A Build 42 single and multiplayer mod that enables zeds to drop a cure for the Knox infection. Inspired by the awesome They Knew.

[H2]Highlights[/H2]
[list]
[*]Designed for Multiplayer, fully compatible with Single Player
[*]Fully configurable drop rates
[*]Optional spoilage system, keep cures refrigerated to extend life
[*]Optional integration with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3532685233]PhunSprinters 2[/url] for different sprinter vs normal drop rates
[*]Optional integration with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3388867450]PhunZones 2[/url] for zone-specific drop rates (e.g. higher rates in Louisville)
[/list]

[H2]What’s New in PhunCure 2[/H2]
[list]
[*]Full support for PhunZones 2
[*]Rebuilt PhunSprinters 2 integration
[*]PhunLib components integrated — no longer required as a dependency
[*]Reworked chance and distance systems
[*]Performance optimisations and cleanup for Build 42+
[/list]

[H2]Global Settings[/H2]
[table]
[tr][th]Setting[/th][th]Description[/th][/tr]
[tr][td][b]Default Drop Rate[/b][/td][td]Chance out of 100 that a zed is carrying a cure. (10 = 10%, 5.5 = 5.5%)[/td][/tr]
[tr][td][b]Default Sprinter Drop Rate[/b][/td][td]Chance out of 100 that a sprinter is carrying a cure.[/td][/tr]
[tr][td][b]Expired Cure Chance[/b][/td][td]Chance (out of 100) that the cure is already expired. Further increases rarity. Has no effect if spoilage is disabled (Days to Rot = 0).[/td][/tr]
[tr][td][b]Days to Rot[/b][/td][td]Number of days the cure remains usable without refrigeration. Set to 0 or a very high value to disable spoilage. Applies only after restart and to newly created items.[/td][/tr]
[tr][td][b]Days Fresh[/b][/td][td]Number of days the cure is considered fresh (currently cosmetic only). Applies only after restart and to newly created items.[/td][/tr]
[tr][td][b]Cure Virus[/b][/td][td]Injecting a valid cure removes the Knox infection.[/td][/tr]
[tr][td][b]Heal Wound[/b][/td][td]Injecting a valid cure heals infected wounds (separate from the Knox virus).[/td][/tr]
[tr][td][b]Heal Bite[/b][/td][td]Injecting a valid cure heals bites.[/td][/tr]
[tr][td][b]Heal Scratch[/b][/td][td]Injecting a valid cure heals scratches.[/td][/tr]
[tr][td][b]Debug[/b][/td][td]Enables debug logging for troubleshooting and balancing.[/td][/tr]
[/table]

For individual zone settings in PhunZones 2, edit the zone (start with Default Zone) and configure drop rates there. If left empty, sandbox values are used.

[img]https://i.ibb.co/DZ7Vffn/Thumbs-Up.png[/img]

[h2]More Phun Stuff[/h2]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3689006725][img]https://i.ibb.co/4ZLgMZ4m/preview.png[/img][/url] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3676252660][img]https://i.ibb.co/3mQpB8ST/preview.png[/img][/url] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3676252110][img]https://i.ibb.co/d4y0BhnF/preview.png[/img][/url]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3623897097][img]https://i.ibb.co/mCv5kDgy/preview.png[/img][/url] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3676250472][img]https://i.ibb.co/svW9sVvK/preview.png[/img][/url]

Coded with love for our awesome [url=https://discord.gg/v2USyAtP6q]PhunZoid[/url] community. [url=https://github.com/PhunZoider/PhunCure]GitHub repo and more info[/url]

publishedfiledetails.title: Beathoven's Night Vision Framework
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Night Vision Framework[/h1]
A framework for adding realistic night vision functionality to Project Zomboid items.
Includes a working example item: [b]Night Vision Goggles[/b] that can be found in military loot.

[hr][/hr]
[h2]Known supported Mods[/h2]
[list]
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3470491629]Night Vision Googles[/url]

[/list]
[hr][/hr]
[h2]How to Use[/h2]
[olist]
[*]Find or spawn the example Night Vision Goggles.
[*][b]Right-click[/b] the goggles in your inventory and select [b]Insert Battery[/b]. This is only available while they are in inventory and not worn.
[*][b]Wear[/b] the goggles.
[*]Press [b]V[/b] to toggle night vision on or off. If your item supports exact [b]_ON / _OFF[/b] or [b]_DOWN / _UP[/b] state tokens, the visor model flips automatically.
[/olist]

[h2]Features[/h2]
[list]
[*][b]Multiple NV modes[/b] — shipped with Custom, Green2, White Phosphor, and Vanilla modes, with room for add-on modes from other mods. The Debug authoring mode is exposed only in debug-enabled singleplayer.
[*][b]Custom framework overlay[/b] — tint, grain, vignette, flicker, and climate tinting driven by per-mode profiles.
[*][b]Dynamic directional lighting[/b] — raycasted light cone with opening-aware LOS, low-cover exemptions, curtain-aware windows and see-through openings, plus reduced lightpost churn for better performance.
[*][b]Debug authoring mode[/b] — built-in live editor with instant preset import for registered non-vanilla modes plus direct directional-light controls, available only in debug-enabled singleplayer.
[*][b]Green2 glare[/b] — bright-room glare effect is available on Green2 only.
[*][b]Vanilla helper light[/b] — built-in Zomboid NV mode can still receive a framework indoor helper light in very dark rooms.
[*][b]Singleplayer helper sandbox toggle[/b] — the sandbox can choose whether singleplayer uses the TorchCone-backed directional helper or the lamppost-style fallback helper.
[*][b]Battery system[/b] — install/remove batteries via right-click. Each battery lasts 12 in-game hours. Charge bar visible on the item tooltip and updates live while charge drains. Low battery triggers a flicker warning below 20%.
[*][b]Multiplayer sync[/b] — battery state, live charge-bar updates, battery install/remove actions, and named visor state variants are synchronized through shared/server framework code.
[*][b]Sound support[/b] — on/off clicks plus a subtle ambient hum while NV is active.
[*][b]Fully localized[/b] — 27 language translations included.
[/list]

[h2]Mod Options[/h2]
Open [b]Options → Mod Options → Night Vision Framework[/b] to configure:
[list]
[*][b]Night Vision Mode[/b] — choose between the shipped modes or registered add-on modes. The debug authoring mode only appears in debug-enabled singleplayer.
[*][b]Legacy light cone sampling[/b] — defaults to on for the denser classic cone look; disable it to switch to the lighter sparse sampler on weaker PCs.
[*][b]Toggle key[/b] — rebind the NV toggle key (default: V).
[*][b]Debug Overlay Editor key[/b] — open the live editor while the [b]debug[/b] mode is active (default: N). This row only appears in debug-enabled singleplayer.
[*][b]Enable ambient NV hum[/b] — toggle the looping ambient sound (default: on).
[*][b]Ambient hum volume[/b] — slider 0–100% (default: 30%). Changes apply live.
[/list]

[b]Shipped mode notes:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Custom[/b] — classic green phosphor look with the restored wide light cone and no glare.
[*]Directional shipped modes default to [b]legacy[/b] cone sampling, but legacy rebuild checks are throttled and players can switch to [b]sparse[/b] sampling at runtime in Mod Options.
[*]Directional LOS now treats fences, counters, and half walls as low cover instead of solid walls, and supported windows or see-through openings follow curtain state.
[*][b]Debug[/b] — live overlay and directional-light authoring mode with instant preset import for registered non-vanilla modes, exposed only in debug-enabled singleplayer.
[*][b]Green2[/b] — stronger ReShade-inspired green mode with room-light glare.
[*][b]White Phosphor[/b] — cooler grayscale or blue-white profile.
[*][b]Vanilla[/b] — Zomboid's built-in night vision effect plus a tuned indoor helper light.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]For Modders[/h2]
Add [b]NightVision = true,[/b] to a clothing item script and the framework will handle the rest. [b]Tags = NVITEM,[/b] is still accepted as a legacy fallback. Full documentation lives in [b]README.md[/b].

[b]Features available to modders:[/b]
[list]
[*]Automatic NV detection via the [b]NightVision = true[/b] script key
[*]Battery-aware toggle flow with automatic shutdown on invalid gear or empty battery
[*]Exact state-token swapping for [b]_ON / _OFF[/b] and [b]_DOWN / _UP[/b]
[*]Dynamic directional-light helper or vanilla-style indoor helper reuse
[*]Texture-backed overlay noise via [b]noiseTexturePaths[/b] for filmed or sensor-style modes
[*]Built-in in-game debugger for live overlay and directional-light tuning in debug-enabled singleplayer
[*]External mode registration through [b]NV.RegisterNightVisionMode({ ... })[/b]
[*]Copyable dummy mode example with tuned overlay and light profiles
[*]Complete example item with models and textures
[/list]

[b]Model swap:[/b] To get the visor-up or visor-down visual, provide two item variants that use exact [b]_ON / _OFF[/b] or [b]_DOWN / _UP[/b] state tokens in the item name. The framework swaps only that exact token, so names like [b]..._UP-HeadStrap[/b] are handled safely.

[hr][/hr]
[b]Multiplayer note:[/b] Battery state, live battery-bar updates, battery install/remove, and named visor state variants use shared or server-authoritative synchronization. The screen effect itself remains local to the wearer, as intended.

publishedfiledetails.title: Maplewood [B42]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[quote] B42 UNSTABLE RELEASE
This is an unstable release. For any Maplewood related issues, please use the bug report forum section at the bottom of this page. [/quote]

[hr][/hr]
[h1] 🍁 Welcome to Maplewood! 🍁 [/h1]
Maplewood is a small rural town, located in central Knox Country, West of Muldraugh, and neighbours Fallas Lake. The town features 19th century American architecture, and offers a varied amount of essential loot to be found. Maplewood is surrounded by rural farmland and home to a tight knit community, making it perfect for those who seek to escape the cityscape.

[h2] ⭐ Features Include: [/h2]
[list]
    [*]Vanilla themed map
    [*]Basements
    [*]Wild Animals + Ranch Zones
    [*]In game map + Lootable Map
    [*]Street Names + World Map Annotation
    [*]Foraging + Zombie Zones
    [*]No additional Tile packs required
[/list]

[h1] 📌 Map location [/h1]
Maplewood is located in central Knox Country, West of Muldraugh, and neighbours Fallas Lake.

[b] Cells: 27x28 [/b]

[b] B42 Cells: 31x32, 31x33, 32x32, 32x33 [/b]

[hr][/hr]
Maplewood is a 1 cell map that I started several months ago, my mission with this map was simple:
[list]
    [*]Create a small and enjoyable map to explore
    [*]Capture the vanilla map theme
    [*]Incorporate B42's game features
    [*]Easy Installation, No additional Tile packs required
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

I will try limit the amount of updates I do and post in the comments prior to any updates. Beware that updates to both the B42 Unstable branch and the map itself, may break your saves, please backup your saves!
[b] I have no plans to do an official B41 backport for Maplewood as this map contains tiles and features designed with B42 in mind. [/b]

[b] Credits:[/b]
A huge thank you to members within the Official PZ and Unofficial Mapping Discord for all the help and mapping knowledge you shared with me with to complete this map, this would not have been possible without you. Also a big thank you to the play testers!

[h1] In memory of my father Alexander. [/h1]
[h1]Welcome back to the Muldraugh 1993 in Build 42![/h1]
[h2]It's been a long time since the release of B41 Muldraugh, but now it's back to replace even the B42 one![/h2]

[h2] What is Muldraugh 1993? [/h2]
[quote=Ktorr2]Muldraugh '1993' is a solo project, aimed to recreate the real Muldraugh from Kentucky, US. Every single building is unique and built from scratch, by powering imagination. The map will cover the vanilla Muldraugh, so it would be possible to visit from ex.West-Point or Rosewood. The project have been creating since 21.09.24 and have ended in 12.07.25, so it's 10 months old. [/quote]

publishedfiledetails.title: W900 Semi-Truck [B42]
publishedfiledetails.description:
Original Mod [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2759339330]W900 Semi-Truck[/url] by [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/MrLexx/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]Lexx[/url]

[h1]W900 Semi-Truck [B42][/h1]
Build 42 port and expansion with integrated RotatorsLib, multi-slot hauling, trailer logistics systems, cistern gameplay, and a powered freezer box-truck variant.

[h2]Current Features[/h2]
[list]
[*] W900 truck lineup: SemiTruck, SemiTruckBox, SemiTruckLite, SemiTruck_mil and SemiTruckBox_mil
[*] Trailer lineup: SemiTrailerLowBed, SemiTrailerCistern, SemiTrailerVanCattle and SemiTrailerVan_mil
[*] Multi-Slot Wrecker lowbed system for hauling up to 3 vehicles on the trailer
[*] Vehicle state preservation for transported vehicles, including parts, condition, keys, containers, and special item data
[*] Sleeper utility interior support with mattress, microwave, and fridge on supported truck layouts
[*] Box-truck freezer system with 4 auxiliary batteries and a craftable freezer upgrade
[*] Dedicated cistern gameplay with custom gasoline and water tank logic
[*] Hydrant filling support, post-shutoff reserve handling, and generator-aware water rules
[*] Military variants with dynamic firearm loot support
[*] 4-tire rear axle support with dedicated middle axle parts, wheelchains, and bogie sync fixes
[*] Payload damping and mass validation for more stable loaded-truck behavior (Boxtruck and Trailers with not RCP)
[*] Trailer armor, wheelchain, and mechanics UI naming cleanup across supported languages
[/list]

[h2]Compatibility Notes[/h2]
[list]
[*] RotatorsLib is already integrated as a sub-mod and is required for the full feature set
[*] Autotsar Trailers is no longer required for W900 tanker functionality
[*] If trailer attach options do not appear correctly, load W900 after mods that replace or heavily patch vehicle radial menus
[*] Realistic Car Physics is optional, but recommended if you want the alternate sound and handling setup (Beta Sandbox options)
[/list]

[h2]Recommended Mods[/h2]

publishedfiledetails.title: '79 Chevrolet Camaro
publishedfiledetails.description:
79 was the best year for Camaro sales, there were many variants and options and they all shared that iconic base. The pack also features one of the most iconic cars ever modified: The Ghost Camaro. In 1992 ex-Danish Special Forces operative Helge Meyer modified it with some help from his friends in US Army, US Airforce and Deutsche Bundeswehr to give it a chance to survive in an active warzone. His plan was to drive it deep into war-torn Bosnia to deliver toys and food for children, he did this for 3 years, he is considered a hero and the car is still running. Try to find one in the Knox and see how far it will take you!

[b]Main features:[/b]
[list]
[*]4 variants (Base, RS, Z28 and The ghost)
[*]3 Front bumpers and a rear bumper with 2 tiered protection
[*]Optional run flats that come standard with the ghost
[*]Fully animated hood, trunk, doors and windows
[*]Visible interior and character
[*]All parts have 3d objects you can remove and place in the world
[*]Extra parts to craft and find in the world to make the vehicle truly yours (bumpers, bullbars, window armor, roofracks, mudflaps, spoilers etc)
[*]Multiple texture variations
[*]Armor protection for windows, doors, engine, headlights etc
[*]4 seats
[/list]
[b][list][*]You can use it in your current save game, just enable the mod in the load menu
[*]It can tow and be towed
[*]Not recolorable
[/list][/b]

If you can't wait to find this mod in your game and want to test it right away, use [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2634426926]bikinitools[/url] to spawn it, hide the items that your character holds while in vehicle, or for all other good stuff that mod has, go get it!

[b]If you like my mods, please consider supporting me with a coffee[/b],
so I don't fall asleep while making new mods and supporting old ones :)

[url=https://ko-fi.com/ki5mods][img]https://i.imgur.com/2uTskWl.png[/img][/url]

Check out my vehicle mods:

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2490220997][img]https://i.imgur.com/1C4iQ6U.png[/img][/url]

[b]Credits:[/b]
[list]
[*]This mod is commission work by request of iron assassin90
[*]Thanks to The Indie Stone for making this awesome game and all this possible.
[*]Special thanks to bikinihorst for the huge help with code, optimization, and taking care of the multiplayer and part code.[/list]

[quote=KI5]

Ask for permission
This mod can only be added to and extended with the express permission from the original creator. If no permission is received you may not alter the mod, and it must be treated as a mod that is 'On Lockdown'. You are not allowed to repack this mod under any circumstances, ever. [/quote]

[b]Compatibility:[/b]

✅ B41 (b41.78.16) Single Player / Multiplayer fully supported
✅ B42 (b42.13+) Single Player / Multiplayer fully supported

publishedfiledetails.title: Vanilla Foods Expanded
publishedfiledetails.description:
[img]https://i.postimg.cc/yd9LWwgQ/DOESNOTCONFLICT.png[/img]

[img]https://i.postimg.cc/RVMdSqnS/Over1700.png[/img]
[b]Vanilla Foods Expanded[/b] adds over [b][u]1700[/u] new foods, recipes and evolved recipes[/b] that are similar in style and use to base game. You can find:
[list]
[*]Canned foods, such as meats, vegetables, fruits, soup, stew and bait
[*]Jars and new canning recipes
[*]Herbs, spices and seasonings
[*]Sauces, relishes and dressings
[*]Pantry staples, snacks, candy and breakfast foods
[*]Desserts, such as cake mixes and pudding
[*]Fresh fruit and vegetables
[*]Fresh meat and seafood
[*]Fridge and freezer foods
[*]Beverages
[*]New recipes and evolved recipes
[/list]
Vanilla spawn rates remain the same, there won’t be an overwhelming amount of food in the world, just a lot more variety. 3D models have been created for every item.

[hr][/hr]
[img]https://i.postimg.cc/MH65VwHP/3.png[/img]
[list]
[*]Discover thousands of new items across every imaginable category, all carefully designed to be similar in style to base game
[*]Everything comes in multiple flavors and varieties
[*]Find recipe books scattered throughout your world that unlock new cooking possibilities, letting you create authentic, home cooked meals
[*]Pour it into a bowl, or make it into a plate - a lot of recipes can be dished up into individual servings
[*]Baking! Start with convenient box mixes for a quick and tasty treat, then progress to learning recipes that allow you to bake everything from scratch
[*]Craft new evolved recipes, including casseroles, curry, lasagna, loaded baked potatoes, nachos, chili, ramen, milkshakes, fried rice and dozens more!
[*]Season your creations with an extensive collection of herbs, spices and seasoning blends, to perfect every dish
[*]Craft countless homemade versions of common items and base game items
[/list]

Can be added to an existing save, just be mindful that areas already explored won't have the new loot. As far as we've tested, it can be safely removed from a save, just be mindful you will lose all Vanilla Foods Expanded loot.

[url=https://jiizzy.notion.site/Vanilla-Foods-Expanded-1e12e5e910cb80819d70d4e928f370bc][img]https://i.postimg.cc/d1mnh3Qc/1.png[/img][/url]
[i]With thousands of items added, it's impossible to detail everything in just a description. So just load in, and start exploring for yourself. Or check out the wiki, it's a complete guide on everything added to Vanilla Foods Expanded, upcoming changes I'm currently working on, and more![/i]

[img]https://i.postimg.cc/vTYhfyDb/9.png[/img]
Vanilla Foods Expanded is an ever-growing project, and while there are thousands of items already, our plans haven't stopped here. If you have anything you'd like to see added, check out the discussions and let us know!

[hr][/hr]
[img]https://i.postimg.cc/wMq2LzM4/4.png[/img]
[b]French[/b] thanks to Aatheom!
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3578140897] [b]Russian[/b] [/url] thanks to Malevi4!

[hr][/hr]

[b]A very special thank you goes out to Aatheom, whose invaluable contributions helped bring this project to life. From creating models, timed actions, detailed hand props, French translation and so much more - his dedication and expertise made this possible. It is as much his creation, as it is my own.[/b]

[b]A further thank you is extended to the whole of the modding community, who have supported this entire journey. What began as a small project years ago, has grown into something far beyond what I imagined, thanks to their advice, support and encouragement.[/b]

publishedfiledetails.title: Zed's Universal Mod Unbork
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Zed's Universal Mod Unbork[/h1]

Patches renamed or changed game API so [b]old mods keep working on newer game builds[/b]. When the game updates and renames methods (e.g. getDirtyness → getDirtiness) or changes return types (e.g. getTypeString() → getItemType(), getTags() list → set), this mod adds compatibility shims so mods written for older versions still run.

[h1]What it patches[/h1]
[list]
[*][b]Clothing[/b] — getDirtiness/getDirtyness and setDirtiness/setDirtyness (whichever spelling the game uses, the other is aliased).
[*][b]Item script (getTypeString)[/b] — ScriptItem no longer has getTypeString() in 42.13+; we restore it by mapping getItemType() back to the old string (e.g. "Drainable", "Literature").
[*][b]Item getTags()[/b] — Old mods expect tags as a list with .get(index); when the game exposes a Set, we add .get() so tag access by index still works.
[*]other stuff
[/list]

Load this mod [b]before[/b] or with mods that target older game versions to reduce breakage after updates. No config required.

[b]Want another mod fixed?[/b] Open [b]one thread per mod[/b] in the Discussion tab (include the mod name and what breaks), so requests can be tracked and prioritized.

[h1]Mod load order[/h1]
[olist]
[*]zdk
[*]Zed's Universal Mod Unbork
[*]all other mods that target older game versions
[/olist]

publishedfiledetails.title: Realistic Car Physics [B42MP]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1] Realistic Car Physics:[/h1] 

[b]Requires manual Install! See install section for details![/b]

[H3]Now updated to B42.17SP/MP[/H3]

[H3]Note: Trailers misbehaving upon attachment is [url=https://theindiestone.com/forums/index.php?/topic/93713-42161-animal-trailers-flipping-attached-cars/]vanilla bug[/url][/H3]

[H3]New Update: Optional Engine sound, Horsepower, Vehicle weight and Trunk capacity overhaul![/H3]

[H3]New Update: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3692796577]KI5 compatibility patch[/url] for HP/weight/trunk/sound overhaul![/H3]


[b]Engine sound overhaul[/b] with 4 new engine sound sets applied to all vanilla vehicles. Hear the roar of the engines come to life with sounds that respond to your throttle.

[b]Horsepower and weight overhaul:[/b] No longer will all the cars drive just like each other, each car is given realistic horsepower and weight from the real life car its based off of. Some cars will drive crazy fast while others will lumber up to speed. Larger vehicles with more HP will consume more fuel with project summer car, while lighter cars will be more fuel efficient. Trailers are also heavier and can store more! Vehicles now range from [b]900 to 3100KG[/b] and [b]70hp to 400hp![/b]

[b]Trunk overhaul:[/b] Vehicles now have [b]100~1300[/b] sized trunks and you can scale that up or down as much as you wish in the sandbox options! The big slow vans have massive storage while the faster vehicles now have trunks that can at least store a couple backpacks worth of stuff. 

[b]Try these new options by enabling them in the sandbox menu! These are all beta options and by default disabled. only supports Vanilla vehicles[/b]

[H3]Dynamic traction![/H3]
Vehicle traction for steering, braking and acceleration is now all dynamic and changes depending on road surface, weather and tire condition and pressure!

Vehicle physics has been revamped to solve several vanilla bugs like heavy vehicles tires falling through the world and going out of control when driving over zombies and animals.

Towed vehicles now tow even easier and even steer slightly towards you. Drag and on/off road rolling resistance has been redone from scratch and is now fully adjustable in sandbox options.

Slowdown impulse from hitting zombies is now fully adjustable in sandbox options! Make it as easy or as hard to plow through hoards of zombies as you want!

[h3]Now with manual shifting mode![/h3]

[h2]A Car Physics Overhaul Mod. Features include:[/h2]

[b]Accurately simulated 3-4-5 speed auto transmission[/b] with torque converter and alternate transmissions up to 8 speeds available via [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3564950449]Project Summer Car as well as many other advanced car mechanic features![/url]

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3564950449][img]https://images.steamusercontent.com/ugc/14446766769677975445/16054438CE61A7F5DE985D75E338E8B51F398E46/[/img][/url]

[b]Different levels of traction[/b] when going offroad, when it rains and when the ground is covered in snow!

[b]Configurable steering[/b] with multiple settings to fine tune steering at low and high speed. Or switch back to vanilla steering if you wish!

[b]Towing is now 100% easier:[/b] Someone left the brakes on the towed vehicle! Towed vehicles glide with ease behind you.. and crash into you if you stop too suddenly. Tire pressure also matters a lot for towing speed. Optionally require the key or vehicle be hot-wired to tow them easily! 

[b]Different vehicles[/b] have different [b]RPM red lines[/b] and hit them in every gear before shifting if you floor it! Listen to your engine roar to life when you floor it!

[b]Tires now visually lockup[/b] during hand braking and [b]spin out[/b] if losing friction when accelerating. Tire friction based on tire type and condition limits accelerations. [b]Enhanced tire skidding noises[/b] when burning out, turning hard and locking up the hand brake with different sounds for on road vs off road skids.

[b]Tires matter![/b] Low condition tires will burnout when you try to accelerate, Full inflation pressure will reduce drag on the road while low inflation pressure will reduce drag off road and increase traction off road!

[b]Transmission[/b] automatically up shifts to save fuel when you apply partial throttle and down shifts when you apply full throttle just like a real automatic transmission! Or manual transmission mode if you prefer to shift yourself!

[b]Horsepower tweaks[/b] made by mods now properly affect acceleration.

[b]Per car adjustments[/b] of horsepower and top speed in sandbox settings. Make OP vehicles balanced! make underpowered vehicles overpowered! whatever you wanna do!

[b]Cruise Control[/b] has been improved and is now much easier and safer to use! 

[b]Gamepad[/b] support for analog throttle, brake and manual shifting!

[h2][b]Compatible with: [/b] KI5, Filibuster Rhymes and all other mods that add new cars to the game![/h2]
[b]Not compatible with: [/b] Build 41, Better Car Physics, Effortless towing. Common Sense Reborn (Due to Effortless Towing code)

[h3]Can be installed/removed at any time in a save game![/h3] 

[h3]Install instructions:[/h3]

Copy the [b]"zombie" folder AND ONLY THE ZOMBIE FOLDER[/b] located at: [code]C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\workshop\content\108600\3559765660\mods\Realistic Car Physics\B42.17_Manual_Install[/code]
 to 
[code]C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\ProjectZomboid[/code] 
If you can't find these folders or your steam is installed elsewhere, you can try right clicking Project zomboid in your steam library, going to properties and clicking 'browse files' 
Enable Realistic Car Physics mod in your save game. 

For linux, the folder will end up being in .steam/steam/steamapps/common/ProjectZomboid/projectzomboid/ You can try the following command to install it:
[code]cp -a ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/workshop/content/108600/3559765660/mods/Realistic\ Car\ Physics/zombie ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/ProjectZomboid/projectzomboid/[/code]

For mac, Move the files to within the zombie/core/physics folder to [code]/Library/Application Support/Steam/steamapps/common/ProjectZomboid/Project Zomboid.app/Contents/Java/zombie/core/physics[/code]

For dedicated servers, it must be installed to the \java\ folder alongside the ProjectZomboid.jar

[h3]Uninstall Instructions:[/h3]

Delete [code]C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\ProjectZomboid\Zombie[/code] folder
Disable Realistic Car Physics mod in your save game. 

Special Thanks to Albion and everyone else on the PZ modding discord for help with modding. 
Special Thanks to Filibuster Rhymes' Used Cars! for permission to use their cars for my logo image! 

If you like this mod, try my other mod, [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3543815008]Beefcake Food Overhaul![/url] It makes all food useful and more balanced!

[h2]Frequently asked questions:[/h2]
[b]Does everyone need to install it in multiplayer?[/b] Yes. 
[b]Clients and Server?[/b] Yes. 
[b]Does it still work for B42.16?[/b] Yes, use the zomboid folder in B42.16_Manual_Install folder.

[b]I don't have a zombie folder![/b]: Yes. That is expected in B42.17. you still copy the zombie folder in the mod as install instructions state to the correct location and it will work. It has been tested and works fine.
[b]Is it compatible with Remove Inventory Limits?[/b]: It has its own version of that included, use Realistic car physic's file to have the functionality of both mods.

[h1]Make sure to unsubscribe, resubscribe and delete the old Zombie folder in Project Zomboid for B42.14 update![/h1] 

Workshop ID: 3559765660
Mod ID: RealisticCarPhysics

publishedfiledetails.title: Moodle Descriptions Expanded - Patch B42.15+
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Build Support: Project Zomboid B42.15-B42.16+[/h1]

This is a compatibility patch for [b]Moodle Descriptions Expanded[/b] on [b]Project Zomboid Build 42.15+[/b].

After the build 42.15 update, Project Zomboid changed the translation file format used by the game. Because of that, some translated Moodle descriptions from the original mod may appear missing, broken, or fall back to English.

This patch updates and restores those translations so they display properly again on B42.15+, adds and expands language support while keeping the original mod's functionality unchanged.

[h2]Included languages[/h2]
[list]
[*][b]CH[/b] - Traditional Chinese
[*][b]CN[/b] - Simplified Chinese
[*][b]DA[/b] - Danish
[*][b]DE[/b] - German
[*][b]EN[/b] - English
[*][b]ES[/b] - Spanish
[*][b]FR[/b] - French
[*][b]HU[/b] - Hungarian
[*][b]IT[/b] - Italian
[*][b]JP[/b] - Japanese
[*][b]KO[/b] - Korean
[*][b]NL[/b] - Dutch
[*][b]PL[/b] - Polish
[*][b]PT[/b] - Portuguese
[*][b]PTBR[/b] - Brazilian Portuguese
[*][b]RU[/b] - Russian
[*][b]TH[/b] - Thai
[*][b]TR[/b] - Turkish
[*][b]UA[/b] - Ukrainian
[/list]

[h2]What this patch does[/h2]
[list]
[*]Updates translated Moodle descriptions for the B42.15+ translation system
[*]Restores missing or broken translated tooltips
[*]Fixes incorrect translation keys and tooltip formatting issues
[*]Expands language coverage
[*]Keeps the original gameplay and behavior unchanged
[/list]

[h2]What this patch does NOT do[/h2]
[list]
[*]It does not replace the original mod
[*]It does not add new gameplay features
[*]It does not change Moodle balance, effects, or mechanics
[*]It only updates and fixes translation compatibility for B42.15+
[/list]

[h2]Requirements[/h2]
[list]
[*][b]Moodle Descriptions Expanded[/b] is required
[*]This patch must be enabled together with the original mod
[/list]

[h2]Load order[/h2]
[list]
[*]Load [b]Moodle Descriptions Expanded[/b] first
[*]Load this patch [b]after[/b] it
[/list]

If you use a manual mod list, make sure this patch is placed after the main mod in your active load order.

[h2]Notes[/h2]
[list]
[*]This is a lightweight compatibility patch focused on translation support
[*]It is meant for players who want the original mod's expanded Moodle descriptions to display correctly on B42.15+
[*]It may become unnecessary if the original mod is updated with native B42.15+ translation support
[/list]

[h2]Credits[/h2]
[list]
[*]Full credit for [b]Moodle Descriptions Expanded[/b] goes to its original author: [b][BRE] Galand[/b]
[*]Compatibility patch for translation support: [b]Afyrmo[/b]
[/list]

Workshop ID: 3684807879
Mod ID: MDE_B42_15_Patch

publishedfiledetails.title: Unified Carry Weight Framework (UCWF)
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]If you're a user[/h1]
This doesnt do anything on it's own, the modders are supposed to use this to adjust players carry weight without conflicting with each other.

[h1]Mods using this framework[/h1]
And therefore, their weight-related functionalities should not clash.
[list]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails?id=2914075159]Evolving Traits World (ETW)[/url]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails?id=1299328280]More Traits[/url]
[/list]
If you have a mod that adjusts carry weight in any way and it conflicts with this framework (it will), just bully them into using the framework, it's the only way to have weight changes from different mods play well with each other. 

[h1]If you're a modder[/h1]
You are probably aware that majority of mods that modify carry weight conflict with each other and usually it's just "whichever mod applied their override last" sets the weight.

So I made this little thing.

- This allows multiple mods to modify carry weight without overwriting each other changes.
- Supports flat bonuses and multiplications for both base weight (before str applied) and total max weight (after str bonuses are applied)

So something like this is possible and very easy to do now: 

- Mod A has sandbox setting that sets base carry weight to 10 (from 8)
- Mod B has a trait that gives player 0.25 bonus to base carry weight per point of strength
- Mod C has a exoskeleton that when worn increases total carry weight by 50%.

There's a client and a server file with example on how to use the framework, in their respective folders.

[h1]GitHub and Discord[/h1]
If you want to submit translation files, make suggestions or report bugs, the easiest way to do it would be on Discord.
[url=https://discord.gg/8pBN5Ccm7z][img]https://cdn.iconscout.com/icon/free/png-256/discord-3691244-3073764.png[/img][/url][url=https://github.com/MusicManiac/UnifiedCarryWeightFramework][img]https://cdn.iconscout.com/icon/free/png-256/github-163-761603.png[/img][/url]

[h1]Credits and Translations[/h1]
Huge thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198042464272]Albion[/url] for assist.
Currently mod supports following languages:
[list]
[*]English
[/list]

[h1]Afterword and Permission[/h1]
If you like this mod, check out collection on top of page for other mods I made or collaborated on! Rate ups are appreciated, and if you wanna send me a coffee, that's a real homie move, and you can do it here:
[url=https://ko-fi.com/musicmaniac][img]https://uploads-ssl.webflow.com/5c14e387dab576fe667689cf/61e11d5cf697cee7ca5b65d9_Button-p-500.png[/img][/url]

publishedfiledetails.title: Skullys Lowerback Dufflebags, Chest Rigs + Fannypack, Container Rebalance [42.16 + .15]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[img]https://i.imgur.com/G3hHRBo.gif[/img]
[h2]3 Main functionalities[/h2]
[b]Each can be enabled through the mods menu, all optional, all separate but work well together:[/b]


[h3][u]Equip on Lowerback:[/u][/h3] Duffel Bags can now be equipped on the Lower Back together with a backpack, and Chest Rigs have been adjusted so they no longer conflict with Fanny Packs.

[h3][u]Container rebalancing:[/u][/h3] Rebalanced most if not all player worn containers such as backpacks, first aid kits, cases and etc. Small containers like School Bags, Fanny Packs, and Satchels are now restricted from carrying very heavy individual items. To compensate, they offer much higher Weight Reduction, making them ideal for carrying lightweight essentials at all times.

[h3][u]Minor Item rebalancing:[/u][/h3] Changed weight, and other parameters on a small amount of items to match with the rebalancing aspect of this mod.

Safe to add mid-game.


[hr][/hr]
[h3]Balance & Logic:[/h3]
[i]This mod focuses on refining container utility by rebalancing weight, capacity, and equipment options. The goal is to make early-game storage more viable while ensuring specialized containers feel distinct and logical, and to allow to player to carry more things overall but not make it too overpowered or unbalanced[/i]

[i]My thought process was that your satchel and your fanny is where you carry all your necessities and tools, whereas bags are used mostly for loot, hence those two have the highest weight reduction.

Dufflebags serve as a new way of expanding your entire inventory. They can serve as an extra loot bag that you can drop easily with excess loot when you are surrounded and need to run.[/i]

[h3]Planned Features:[/h3]
- keybind to quick drop down duffelbag worn on lowerback
- rebalancing of backpack values

[h1]FAQ:[/h1]
Is it compatible...? A:Probably is until someone finds a mod that is not. If it is not i'll write it down here in the description

B41? A: I didnt test it but i made it available for b41, i believe you can now enable it, please let me know if its working.

Multiplayer? A: According to the comments it seems to be, its a rather simple mod so i dont think it wouldnt be MP Compatible.


[h1]My mods:[/h1]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3702483515]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/ZacIqzM.png[/img][/url][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3702705968]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/6JYZvHG.png[/img][/url]

[hr][/hr]
[i]Bellow are mods i recommend using together with some of the modules from my mod, and you can adjust to your liking.[/i]
[h2]Recommended Mods:[/h2]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3419255610]RNG Backpack Capacity[/url] by N0wh3re

[h3][i][u]Feedback is welcome![/u][/i][/h3]

publishedfiledetails.title: Realistic Car Physics KI5 Patch
publishedfiledetails.description:
Extends [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3559765660]Realistic Car Physics[/url] beta overhaul features to KI5 modded vehicles and Filibuster Rhymes RVs.

Covers all KI5 vehicles found in [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=3652192243]KI5's temp B42.13+ MP collection[/url]

RCP's Horsepower & Weight, Sound, and Trunk overhaul betas only support vanilla vehicles. This patch adds support for approx. 385 KI5 vehicle variants covering approx. 75 real-world base models.

[h1]Features[/h1]
[list]
[*] [b]HP & Weight Overhaul[/b] - Researched real-world horsepower and curb weight for every supported vehicle. A '69 Mini with 34hp feels very different from a '69 Charger with 375hp.
[*] [b]Sound Overhaul[/b] - RCP's custom engine sounds applied to all supported vehicles.
[*] [b]Full KI5 Collection[/b] - All 71 KI5 car mods supported, from the '49 Dodge Power Wagon to the '04 VW Touran.
[*] [b]Filibuster Rhymes RVs[/b] - Fleetwood Bounder and Ford Econoline RV.
[*] [b]Extras[/b] - Lock-Mart M577 APC (from Aliens).
[/list]

[h1]Requirements[/h1]
[list]
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3559765660]Realistic Car Physics[/url]
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=3652192243]KI5's temp B42.13+ MP collection[/url]
[*] Enable "Horsepower and Weight Overhaul (Beta)", "Sound Overhaul (Beta)", "Trunk Overhaul (Beta)" in sandbox options
[/list]

[h1]How It Works[/h1]
Each vehicle is assigned realistic specs based on its real-world counterpart:
[list]
[*] Pre-1972 cars use SAE gross horsepower ratings (period-correct)
[*] Post-1972 cars use SAE net ratings
[*] Weights are real-world curb weights in kg
[*] Vehicles range from 34hp / 620kg (Mini) to 450hp / 18,600kg (Oshkosh P19A fire truck)
[/list]

The patch integrates directly with RCP's physics engine - it is not just setting engineForce and mass, it feeds horsePower data to RCP's transmission and torque simulation for proper vehicle behavior.

[h1]Compatibility[/h1]
[list]
[*] Build 42
[*] Singleplayer and Multiplayer (all players and server need the mod)
[*] Can be added or removed from a save at any time
[/list]

[h1]Install[/h1]
Subscribe and enable "RCP KI5 Patch" in your mod list. Load after RCP.

[h1]Credits[/h1]
[list]
[*] [b]Black Moons[/b] - Realistic Car Physics - and thanks for reaching out and showing me your intended way to extend your mod!
[*] [b]KI5[/b] - KI5 vehicle collection
[*] [b]Filibuster Rhymes[/b] - Used Cars / RVs
[/list]

Workshop ID: 3692796577
Mod ID: RCP_KI5_Patch

publishedfiledetails.title: Fireproof Firefighter Clothing
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Firefighter Clothing is now Functionally Fire-Resistant[/h1]
This mod will prevent the player from engulfing in flames when wearing the full Firefighter outfit. Each item protects a different part of the body from suffering burns. But wearing the entire outfit will keep the flames from sticking to you.

[code][b]Here are examples of the "full outfit" and the parts of the body each item protects from burns.[/b]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/h5uPpI7.png [/img] [img] https://i.imgur.com/x0zRgoh.png[/img] Any SCBA or Gas Mask (Protects Neck)
[img] https://i.imgur.com/G16wrdo.png[/img] Firefighter Helmet (Protects Head)
[img] https://i.imgur.com/JldhMBC.png[/img] Firefighter Jacket (Protects Torso, Arms)
[img]https://i.imgur.com/DA9fz0W.png[/img] Firefighter Trousers (Protects Legs, Groin)
[img] https://i.imgur.com/TAh0brF.png[/img] Any Gloves (Protects Hands)
[img]https://i.imgur.com/nXxvK7p.png [/img] Any Boots (Protects Feet)

Upon equipping one of these valid items, a notification will pop-up saying something like "3/6 Fire Protective Gear Equipped." If you are fully geared-up, and a clothing item breaks or falls off, another notification should say "Fire Protection Lost."
[b]Notifications can be enabled/disabled via Mod Options.[/b][/code]

Standing directly on flames still steadily lowers your health (a mod linked below helps with that). I didn't remove this aspect of fire damage because I don't want to completely negate the danger of fire. You should be running through that burning building, not shopping for groceries!

[h3] Technical Details [/h3]
In my script, I tried to make it so that this will be compatible with modded clothing. So any item with the tag "scba" or "gasmask" will work with my mod. For gloves and boots, any item will work as long as it has "gloves" or "boots" in it's name.

These non-firefighter clothing items will ONLY offer burn protection for their body parts when worn in conjunction with the proper firefighter gear (Masks work if a Helmet is also worn, Gloves work when combined with Jacket, and Boots with Trousers).

[b]Compatibility:[/b]
✅ Build 42.15+
✅ [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3671351750]FireFighter's Finery[/url]
✅ [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2863836591]Fire Resistance[/url]

Workshop ID: 3689973240
Mod ID: Bubbs_FRFC

publishedfiledetails.title: EZRoof [B42.15+ SP/MP]
publishedfiledetails.description:
Isn't it annoying to build stairs every single time just to put a roof on a rabbit hutch?
At least, it was annoying for me.

EZRoof is a very small quality-of-life mod that lets you place ceilings without building stairs first.

[h2]How to Use:[/h2]
[list]
[*] You have the materials needed to make a floor.
[*] You need a chair or stool. If you don't have these, the "Install Ceiling" option will not appear in the right-click menu.
[*] Be careful not to fall off the scaffolding while installing the ceiling.
[*] No more stairs needed in the rabbit hutch, yay!
[/list]

[h2]Features:[/h2]
[list]
[*] Adds a Place Ceiling option to the right-click menu
[*] Lets you choose between wooden and metal ceilings
[*] [b]Requires a chair or stool as temporary scaffold[/b](This can also be disabled using the sandbox option.)
[*] Works with both carried chairs/stools and nearby placed chairs/stools you can stand on
[*]The goal of this mod is to make roof and ceiling building a little more convenient without heavily breaking vanilla building rules.
[/list]

[h2]Supported version:[/h2]
[list]
[*] Confirmed working on Build 42.15
[/list]

[h2]Notes:[/h2]
[list]
[*] Placement depends on nearby walls, pillars, and existing floor connections
[*] Some moveable furniture may not be recognized as valid scaffold depending on its object data
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

ウサギ小屋の屋根を張るためだけに毎回階段作るの面倒じゃない？少なくとも自分は面倒でした。
EZRoofは階段を作らずに天井を張るためのとても小さなQOL改善MODです。

[h2]使用方法:[/h2]
[list]
[*] 床を作るのに必要な材料を持ちます。
[*] 椅子またはスツールが必要です。これらがない場合、右クリックメニューに「天井を設置する」オプションが表示されません。
[*] 足場から落ちないように気を付けて天井を張る
[*] もうウサギ小屋に階段は要らない、やった！
[/list]

[h2]主な特徴:[/h2]
[list]
[*] 「天井を張る」を右クリックメニューに追加
[*] 木製天井 / 金属製天井を選択可能
[*] [b]足場として椅子 / スツールがインベントリに必要[/b](サンドボックスオプションで不要にも設定可能)
[*] 手持ちの椅子だけでなく、近くに設置した椅子 / スツールの上に立っていても使用可能
[*] このMODの目的は、バニラの建築ルールを大きく壊さずに、屋根・天井作りだけを少し快適にすることです
[/list]

[h2]対応バージョン:[/h2]
[list]
[*] Build 42.15で動作確認
[/list]

[h2]注意:[/h2]
[list]
[*] 建築可能かどうかは、周囲の壁・柱・既存床との接続状況に依存します
[*] 一部の移動式家具は足場判定の対象外になる場合があります
[/list]

Workshop ID: 3688537535
Mod ID: EZRoof

publishedfiledetails.title: More Vehicle Options — Patch B42.15
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]For Build 42.15+ (tested on 42.15.2)[/h2]
This Workshop item provides [b]JSON translation compatibility patches[/b] for [i]More Vehicle Options[/i] on Build 42.15 and later, where the game no longer reads the old TXT translation files used by earlier builds. It includes [b]two alternative patch mods[/b]: one with texts closer to the original mod, and one with more descriptive and quantified texts for players who want clearer sandbox option descriptions.

[h2]This item includes 2 separate mods[/h2]
[list]
[*][b]More Vehicle Options Patch B42.15[/b]
Keeps the wording closer to the original mod translations, while making them compatible with Build 42.15+.
[*][b]More Vehicle Options Patch B42.15 — Descriptive[/b]
Uses more detailed and less ambiguous texts, including clarified descriptions for options that are unclear, misleading, quantified, or no longer have any effect in Build 42.
[/list]

[b]Important:[/b] activate [b]only one[/b] of these two patch mods at a time.
They are [b]mutually incompatible by design[/b].

[h2]Requirements[/h2]
[list]
[*]Requires the original mod [i]More Vehicle Options[/i]
[*]Load this patch [b]after[/b] the original mod
[*]Designed for [b]Build 42.15+[/b]
[/list]

[h2]What this patch does[/h2]
[list]
[*]Adds JSON translation files required by Build 42.15+
[*]Restores proper display of sandbox option texts from [i]More Vehicle Options[/i]
[*]Supports multiple languages
[*]Does [b]not[/b] change the gameplay logic of the original mod
[/list]

[h2]Available languages[/h2]
EN, ES, DE, FR, CH, CN, DA, HU, IT, JP, KO, NL, PL, PT, PTBR, RU, TH, TR, UA

[h2]Which version should I use?[/h2]
[list]
[*]Choose [b]More Vehicle Options Patch B42.15[/b] if you want texts that stay closer to the original mod.
[*]Choose [b]More Vehicle Options Patch B42.15 — Descriptive[/b] if you want clearer, more explicit descriptions of what the options actually do.
[/list]

[h2]Notes[/h2]
[list]
[*]These patches are intended for [b]Build 42.15 and later[/b].
[*]They exist because Project Zomboid now reads mod translations from [b]JSON[/b] instead of the older [b]TXT[/b] format.
[*]The descriptive version may intentionally explain some options more clearly than the original mod, especially where the original wording is ambiguous or where a setting behaves differently from what its name suggests.
[/list]

[h2]Credits[/h2]
* Base mod: [i]More Vehicle Options[/i] by [b]gzeta[/b].
* Patch B42.15: Afyrmo

Full credit for the original mod goes to [b]gzeta[/b]. This Workshop item only provides compatibility and translation patching for newer Build 42.15+ versions of Project Zomboid.

[b]gzeta is welcome to use any part of these translations if useful for the original mod.[/b]

[h2]More mods & feedback[/h2]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199133259679/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]You can find my other Project Zomboid mods here[/url]
[img]https://images.steamusercontent.com/ugc/819002974403344349/DD18664314A26EA83C62E9B0127FFCB35092B53A/[/img]
If you find [b]More Vehicle Options — Patch B42.15[/b] useful, please consider leaving a [b]thumbs up[/b] and adding it to your [b]favorites[/b] — it helps other players discover it.  
Feedback and suggestions are always welcome.
