publishedfiledetails.title: Vehicle Ground Guard [B42 SP + MP]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[b][h1]Vehicle Ground Guard (VGG)[/h1][/b]
Vehicle Ground Guard is an answer to a common bug of vehicles getting stuck in the ground and clipping, rendering them useless. With this mod, your game will now have a debug-free option for all players to free their vehicle whether its clipping face first into the ground, sinking into the concrete, or flipped upside down! The mod also features a self rescue option for players who fall through the map while enterting a clipped vehicle.

[b]Current version:[/b] 1.7.2
- Deep-burial respawn rescue now preserves inventory/container state more reliably.
- Part-state cloning keeps condition/fluid/door/tire/battery state while staying compatible with vanilla mechanics/cheat part editing.


[hr][/hr]


[h2][b]How to use[/b][/h2]

[u][b]Unstick Vehicle[/b][/u]
To use this mod, simply find the clipped vehicle in question and right click on it while near the vehicle. Use the 'Unstick Vehicle' in the drop down menu and the mod will do the rest!

[b][u]Resurface Vehicle[/u][/b]
VGG now features a "Return to Surface option for particularly stubborn vehicles completely submerged below the surface and uses an alternate code with a little help from Beathoven, the maker of the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3409472393&searchtext=W900]W900 Semi Truck.[/url]

[b][u]Self Rescue[/u][/b]
The self-rescue option is made specifically for the black screen glitch when entering a clipped vehicle. To use, hold down 'V' to bring up the vehicle radial menu and use 'Self Rescue'.

[u][b]NOTE:[/b][/u] As of version 1.6.0 of the mod, you may need to manually go into your workshop folder.

For typical installs, it will be:
~\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\workshop\content\108600

 Find and delete the folder [u][b]3690308807[/b][/u] and all its contents, the unsub + resub to VGG to do a fresh install of the mod.

[b][u]You only have do this one time, and only if you used a version of the mod before 1.6.0! (mod versions before the date 3/26/26)[/u][/b]

[hr][/hr]


[h2][b]Mod FAQ[/b][/h2]
[b][i]Does this mod work with modded vehicles?[/i][/b]
Yes! This mod will work with any ingame object marked as a vehicle for Build 42.13 onward. This means that KI5 vehicles not marked as ready for B42.13+ MP will *not* work. There isnt much I can do about that on my end.

[b][i]What versions of the game are supported for VGG?[/i][/b]
The *only* stable build I have for VGG is for Build 42.15+. It may work with earlier B42 builds, but I haven't tested it personally. I just don't have time to download, install, test, patch, uninstall and repeat for every single public build of the game. This mod is *NOT* for build 41.

[b][i]Is this mod safe to add midsave?[/i][/b]
Yes! This mod can be safely added/removed to existing saves!

[b][i]Does this mod work for Singleplayer / Multiplayer?[/i][/b]
Yes to both! The mod was specifically built for B42.15+ multiplayer in mind, but works just as well, if not better in singleplayer!

[b][i]Where should the mod be in the load order?[/i][/b]
I have only personally tested the mod to load after any mods that affect vehicles (add vehicles, changes vehicle mechanics) so I would advise to do the same. Load any vehicle physics mods AFTER VGG to avoid hard crashes.

[b][i]I have multiple mods activated that change vanilla vehicle behavior and your mod isnt working[/i][/b]
Check to make sure that VGG is the last vehicle mod in your load order, as other mods may overwrite my mod in some way. If this doesn't fix it, unfortunately you'll either have to drop VGG or some other mods, because I cannot ensure stability with every single vehicle mod in the game. It's just not realistic.

[hr][/hr]

[b][h2]Incompatible Mods[/h2][/b]
-Common Sense (Specifically the crowbar function on vehicles)
-Realistic Car Physics (Makes resurface + unstick act unpredictably)
-Better Car Physics (Makes resurface + unstick act unpredictably)
-Vanilla Vehicle Animations (Makes resurface + unstick act unpredictably, as well as crash upon loading VVA mod files)
-Project Summer Car (May conflict with part condition in Self Rescue)

[h2][b]Known Bugs[/b][/h2]
-Mod acts janky with mods that change vanilla vehicle physics
-Exact values of gas seems to change after self rescue
-Exact values of battery charged seems to change after self rescue

Workshop ID: 3690308807
Mod ID: VehicleGroundGuard

publishedfiledetails.title: Crossroads Checkpoint B42
publishedfiledetails.description:
*Crossroads Checkpoint*

A fortified military checkpoint and expanded crossroads hub between Muldraugh and West Point.
During the early days of the Knox Event, the military rushed to establish containment lines across Knox Country. One of the largest of these efforts appeared at the highway crossroads between Muldraugh and West Point, where a major road and rail line intersect.
What began as a simple roadblock quickly expanded into a full military screening checkpoint, supported by a National Guard supply yard, rail infrastructure, and a civilian quarantine camp for suspected infected.
This mod transforms the crossroads area into a major point of interest, adding new businesses, infrastructure, and a large military checkpoint that survivors must navigate if traveling between towns.

New Locations
Military Checkpoint
• Highway traffic funnel and vehicle inspection lanes
• Train car barricade blocking the rail crossing
• Guard towers and perimeter fencing
• Military screening area and inspection checkpoint
• Separate quarantine camp for suspected infected
• Holding area where civilians flagged as infected were pulled out of traffic

National Guard Logistics Depot
A military staging area supporting the checkpoint.
Includes:
• National Guard headquarters building
• Vehicle motor pool hangars
• Rail unloading warehouse
• Military storage areas
• helicopter landing pads
• underground bunker access

Civilian Rail Yard
A small industrial rail spur supporting local industry and military logistics.
• warehouse buildings
• rail loading infrastructure
• freight staging areas

Crossroads Businesses
The surrounding crossroads has been expanded with several new and updated locations.
• Refreshed Nolan’s Used Cars dealership
• New car dealership and repair shop
• Truck repair shop
• Beer Barn liquor store
• Farm and hunting supply store
• Welding and metalworking shop
Restaurants & Services

• Updated Spiffo’s with a corrected drive-thru layout
• Roadside café / pancake house
• Grocery store
• Doctor’s office / small clinic
• Video rental store
• Music store

Lodging
• Roadside hotel / motel for travelers passing through the crossroads
Gameplay

The crossroads area now acts as a dangerous choke point between major towns.
Survivors traveling between Muldraugh and West Point will have to pass through the remains of a large military checkpoint and quarantine zone.

Expect:
• dense zombie populations
• military loot opportunities
• abandoned civilian vehicles
• environmental storytelling tied to the Knox containment effort

The surrounding businesses provide additional places to explore and scavenge, turning the crossroads into a small roadside hub that feels like it could have existed in the base game.

Lore Friendly Design
All locations were designed to blend naturally with the vanilla map and fit within the timeline of the Knox Event.
The checkpoint, rail yard, and military staging area tell the story of a containment effort that rapidly escalated as the outbreak spread.

Build Compatibility
Built for Project Zomboid Build 42.
Should work with most map mods unless they modify the crossroads area between Muldraugh and West Point.

*I recommend a ladder climbing mod!*

If you enjoy exploring my maps, you can support future builds here ☕ One cup goes a long way!
https://ko-fi.com/freebird550

Check out my other maps!

Updated 4/13/25
Added 2 propane tanks
Changed loot in a few buildings

Workshop ID: 3686455777
Mod ID: Crossroads-Checkpoint
Map Folder: Crossroads-Checkpoint

publishedfiledetails.title: Vanilla Crowbars Expanded
publishedfiledetails.description:
When it comes to improvised weaponry why should baseball bats, planks of wood and long sticks have all the fun?  It's time to dress up your crowbar!

[img]https://images.steamusercontent.com/ugc/12905167294115738678/607AF34DF484FDE91FBB8BEF8274001F9EB3B878/[/img]


This mod includes:

[b]Crowbar with Rake Head[/b]
Not very useful for leaf removal, but very good at zombie removal.

[b]Crowbar with Railspike[/b]
Also usable as a pickaxe.

[b]Crowbar with Circular Sawblade[/b]
Used like an axe. It can also do all the usual axe things like cutting down trees.

[b]Crowbar with Axe Head[/b]
Used like a Crowbar with Circular Sawblade. But better.

[b]Crowbar with Hammer Head[/b]
Able to hammer things when crowbaring things gets boring.

[b]Crowbar with Sledehammer Head[/b]
If at first you don't succeed, get a bigger hammer.

[b]Crowbar with Metal Bolts[/b]
I don't have a catchy description for this one. Sorry.

[b]Sawn-off Crowbar[/b]
Everything you love about crowbars in a cute little package that fits on your belt.

[b]Spearbar[/b]
When you want to crowbar something from further away.

[b]Reinforced Crowbar[/b]
Sick and tired of crowbars breaking on you? Now there's a solution!

[b]Crowbar with More Crowbars[/b]
An experimental model pushing the limits of extreme durability.




[h3]Where can I find these weapons?[/h3]

While they can be found in the world, they are not common and it's far more likely that you will craft them.

[h3]What are the crafting requirements?[/h3]

Welding 2.  That's it.  You're taking two bits of metal and welding them to each other, this is not rocket science.  Plus if you're installing this mod it's because you want to play with new crowbars, not because you want to grind welding to 8 and long blunt to 6 while you hunt down a skill magazine that is rarer than a generator manual.

[h3]How do they compare with vanilla weapons?[/h3]

All these weapons (except the reinforced crowbar) are slightly less durable than a normal crowbar, which still makes them more far durable than nearly every other melee weapon.  Plus they can be fixed up with a blowtorch, a bit of  welding rod and some scrap metal. 

In general the combat stats are based on the modified baseball bats, taking into account the difference between crowbar and a baseball bat. This means a lower crit chance, a higher crit multiplier, slightly higher max damage, slightly lower min damage,  and much more durability.    

[h3]Do they still function as crowbars?[/h3]

Of course!  Except the spearbar.  


Workshop ID: 3684674703
Mod ID: NepCrowbars

publishedfiledetails.title: Staircast  | B41/B42
publishedfiledetails.description:
Lets you see upstairs in a normal way.

Compatible game versions: [b]41.78 and above[/b]; [b]42.13 and above[/b]

[h2]Installation[/h2]
Subscribe and make sure you have [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=3619862853]ZombieBuddy[/url] installed properly.

[h2]Q/A[/h2]
[h3]Why ZombieBuddy is required?[/h3]
This is a Java mod and ZombieBuddy is a Java mod loader. What this mod does is not achievable in a standard mod, and while it is possible to make a Java mod by replacing the game's class files, such mods tend to break even on minor game updates. The mod loader solves this problem by injecting code at runtime.
[h3]Can I verify what the mod does and build it myself?[/h3]
Yes, the source code and build instructions are on GitHub. See below.
[h3]Multiplayer support?[/h3]
Yes. [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=3619862853]ZombieBuddy[/url] must be installed manually before you join a server.
[h3]Is it safe to add to or remove from an existing save?[/h3]
Yes.
[h3]Does it work on my game version?[/h3]
Both Build 41 and Build 42 are supported. Build 41 requires version [b]41.78 or later[/b], and Build 42 requires version [b]42.13 or later[/b].
[h3]It doesn't work on my game version that is supported![/h3]
This is most likely because [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=3619862853]ZombieBuddy[/url] is not installed correctly/not working. Installation requires manual steps, please follow its instructions carefully. Another reason might be that you didn't enable the mod for your save. If that's not the case for you, please report it.
[h3]Mod does work!!![/h3]
Enjoy!

[h2]Source code[/h2]
[url=https://github.com/copiumsawsed/pz-Staircast]View GitHuh[/url]

Workshop ID: 3684713089
Mod ID: Staircast

publishedfiledetails.title: ZombieBuddy
publishedfiledetails.description:
A framework that enables advanced Java mods for Project Zomboid.

[b]What it does:[/b]
ZombieBuddy is required by some mods to work. If a mod tells you to install ZombieBuddy, follow the instructions below.

[h1]Installation (One-Time Setup)[/h1]

[b]Step 1:[/b] Subscribe to ZombieBuddy on Steam Workshop

[b]Step 2:[/b] Installation is required ONLY ONCE for ZombieBuddy itself.

[b]🪟 Windows:[/b]

[u]Option A: Automated Installer (Recommended)[/u]
[list]
[*] Close Steam completely (right-click Steam in system tray → Exit)
[*] Download the latest ZombieBuddyInstaller.exe from [url=https://github.com/zed-0xff/ZombieBuddy/releases/]GitHub Releases[/url]
[*] Run the installer - it will automatically detect your game folder, copy the files, and set up Steam launch options.
[*] Start Steam.
[/list]

[u]Option B: Manual Installation[/u]
[list]
[*] Copy ZombieBuddy.jar and zbNative.dll from:
[code]C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\workshop\content\108600\3619862853\mods\ZombieBuddy\libs\[/code]
to your Project Zomboid folder:
[code]C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\ProjectZomboid\[/code]
[*] Open Project Zomboid's launch options in Steam
[*] Add this launch option: [code]-agentlib:zbNative --[/code]
[*] [b]⚠️ IMPORTANT:[/b] The -- at the end is mandatory - do not omit it!
[/list]

[b]🍎 macOS / 🐧 Linux:[/b]
[list]
[*] Copy ZombieBuddy.jar from the mod's libs/ directory to:
[list]
[*] [b]macOS:[/b] ~/Library/Application Support/Steam/steamapps/common/ProjectZomboid/Project Zomboid.app/Contents/Java/
[*] [b]Linux:[/b] ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/ProjectZomboid/projectzomboid/
[/list]
[*] Open Project Zomboid's launch options in Steam
[*] Add this JVM argument: -javaagent:ZombieBuddy.jar --
[*] [b]⚠️ IMPORTANT:[/b] The -- at the end is mandatory - do not omit it!
[/list]

[b]Step 3:[/b] After this setup, all mods that require ZombieBuddy will work automatically!

[h1]Uninstall[/h1]
[list]
[*] [b]Disable the agent first:[/b] Open Project Zomboid → Properties → clear the Launch Options line
[*] Delete zbNative.dll and ZombieBuddy.jar from your Project Zomboid install directory
[*] Unsubscribe from ZombieBuddy on Steam
[/list]

[h1]⚠️ Security Warning ⚠️[/h1]
Java mods enabled through ZombieBuddy have unrestricted access to your system. Only install mods from sources you trust.

[b]No jar will sneak in.[/b] Whenever a Java mod ships a new or updated JAR, ZombieBuddy shows a native dialog with the mod id, file path, last-modified date, and SHA-256 fingerprint. Nothing is loaded until you click [b]Yes[/b]. You can choose to remember the decision (per-mod, per-hash) or keep it session-only. Decisions are stored in:
[list]
[*] [b]Windows:[/b] %USERPROFILE%\.zombie_buddy\java_mod_approvals.txt
[*] [b]macOS / Linux:[/b] ~/.zombie_buddy/java_mod_approvals.txt
[/list]
If a previously-approved JAR changes on disk (hash mismatch), you will be prompted again.

[h1]Java Mod Policy[/h1]
Controls what happens when an unknown/changed JAR is seen. Pass as a [b]policy=...[/b] argument:
[list]
[*] [b]prompt[/b] (default) - ask me each time via a native dialog.
[*] [b]deny-new[/b] - silently skip any JAR that isn't already approved.
[*] [b]allow-all[/b] - load every JAR without prompting. [b]Not recommended.[/b]
[/list]
Examples (block form, these go into Steam launch options):
[code]
-agentlib:zbNative=policy=deny-new --            (Windows)
-javaagent:ZombieBuddy.jar=policy=deny-new --    (macOS / Linux)
[/code]

[h1]FAQ[/h1]
[list]
    [*][b]Is it a virus?[/b]
    No.

    [*][b]How to start game in debug mode?[/b]
    [code]-agentlib:zbNative -- -debug[/code]

    [*][b]Do I need to repeat manual installation after I upgrade/downgrade/validate game?[/b]
    No.

    [*][b]Works on MP?[/b]
    Yes.

    [*][b]Save compatibility?[/b]
    Safe to add or remove on existing saves.

    [*][b]I clicked the wrong button in the approval dialog. How do I reset it?[/b]
    Open [code]java_mod_approvals.txt[/code] (path above), delete the offending [code]modId|sha256=yes|no[/code] line, and restart the game.

    [*][b]Can I see which Java mods were loaded or blocked?[/b]
    Yes - install [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3709229404]Zed's Better ModList[/url] and the status is shown next to each JAR path in the mod list.

    [*][b]Supported game versions?[/b]
    B41, B42.12, B42.13, B42.14, B42.15, B42.16
[/list]

[h1]Links[/h1]
[url=https://github.com/zed-0xff/ZombieBuddy]GitHub Repository[/url]

[h1]Support the Project[/h1]
If you find ZombieBuddy useful, consider supporting its development:
[url=https://ko-fi.com/zed_0xff][img]https://i.imgur.com/Utx6OIH.png[/img][/url]


Workshop ID: 3619862853
Mod ID: ZombieBuddy

publishedfiledetails.title: Reload Speed Fix [B42.15 - 42.16]
publishedfiledetails.description:
Fixes a vanilla bug where the Reloading perk has no effect on magazine loading and unloading speed.

When loading or unloading a magazine by hand, leveling up the Reloading perk does nothing — every cycle takes the same amount of time regardless of perk level. This mod fixes that.

[h1]Root Cause[/h1]
Magazine loading (ISLoadBulletsInMagazine) and unloading (ISUnloadBulletsFromMagazine) both drive their looped animations by manually accumulating the raw animation time delta each frame. Unlike every other reload action, which uses the standard animation speed variable (ReloadSpeed) to control playback, these actions bypass it entirely — so the perk multiplier is never applied.

[h1]What This Mod Does[/h1]
Overrides the time accumulation in both classes to scale it by the character's current ReloadSpeed. Magazine loading and unloading now correctly speed up with each Reloading perk level, matching the behavior already present in all other reload types.

[b]Speed by Reloading perk level (unchanged values, now actually applied):[/b]
[list]
[*] Level 0: 0.80x
[*] Level 3: 1.10x
[*] Level 5: 1.30x
[*] Level 10: 1.80x
[/list]

[h1]Scope[/h1]
Only affects ISLoadBulletsInMagazine and ISUnloadBulletsFromMagazine. All other reload types (single-round, rack, eject, unload from firearm) were already working correctly and are not touched.
Compatible with multiplayer.

[url=https://ko-fi.com/P5P21WPFHK][img]https://i.imgur.com/F5AkuRS.jpeg[/img][/url]

Workshop ID: 3682591755
Mod ID: ReloadSpeedFix

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42]RV Interior - Exit Fix
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1][B42]RV Interior - Exit Fix[/h1]

[b]Required Mod:[/b] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3543229299]Project RV Interior[/url]

[h2]Overview[/h2]
A comprehensive compatibility fix specifically designed for the B42 version of Project RV Interior. This mod resolves the critical issue where players occasionally get trapped inside vehicle interiors. This patch is shared for personal use.

[h2]Fixed Issues[/h2]
[b]Root Cause:[/b] When ModData is accidentally lost, the original mod's exit functions fail because they cannot retrieve correct persistence records. This patch resolves the issue by providing a data reconstruction layer.

[h2]Technical Fix Process[/h2]
This patch uses [b]Hooks[/b] to intercept functions and automatically reconstruct missing ModData before the original logic executes, without modifying any original mod files.

[b]ModData Reconstruction Steps:[/b]
[list]
[*] [b]Step 1-2:[/b] Ensures the player has a unique ID (PID) and verifies data integrity. If the data is healthy, the fix skips reconstruction.
[*] [b]Step 3 (VehicleId Search):[/b] Searches for the target vehicle using three prioritized fallback methods:
[list]
[*] [b]Method A:[/b] Intelligent proximity matching (within 15 tiles) based on the player's current physical coordinates against cached room data.
[*] [b]Method B:[/b] If no proximity match is found, it performs a forced read from the player's historical interior records.
[*] [b]Method C:[/b] Scans all room assignment tables for a full-text match and updates the cache.
[/list]
[*] [b]Step 4-5:[/b] Identifies the room type (bus, trailer, etc.) and populates all missing critical fields in the player's record.
[*] [b]Step 6 (Coordinate Failsafe):[/b] Updates vehicle world coordinates. If the vehicle is not loaded in the current cell, it uses the player's pre-entry location or a failsafe coordinate (10000, 10000).
[/list]

[h2]Special Notes[/h2]
[list]
[*][b]Exit Position:[/b] After fix, you can customize the [b]exit position[/b] (Outside, Driver's Seat, or Passenger Seat) via [b]Sandbox Options[/b].
[*] [b]Maintenance:[/b] Currently bug-free for personal use. We do not provide support for issues unrelated to this patch.
[/list]

[h2]Disclaimer[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]Personal Nature:[/b] Provided "as-is" based on personal gameplay needs.
[*] [b]Technology:[/b] Implementation logic and solutions assisted by AI technology.
[*] [b]Copyright:[/b] All original resources and code belong to the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3543229299]Project RV Interior[/url] owner.
[*] [b]Takedown:[/b] If the original author feels this project infringes on their rights, please contact me for immediate removal.
[/list]

Workshop ID: 3677265317
Mod ID: PRV_SafehouseExitFix

Yes, its another weapon slings mod. Can be worn in 4 positions (Right Shoulder, Left Shoulder, Neck, Back). Adds extra hotbar slot for any long weapon (supports weapon from mods too). You can craft or find it, called "Weapon Sling". Not compatible with other weapon sling mods. Works in B42 MP.

-you can choose between invisible or black textures (to do this, select your preferred mod when starting the world).

Huge thanks to Akyet for creating [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2644986930]this mod[/url] for build 41. I just updated it to build 42, edited textures and added translation support.

publishedfiledetails.title: '82 Oshkosh M911 + Military Semi-Trailers
publishedfiledetails.description:
So you want to move your whole base to another location, or you want to hoard all the gas in Knox County, maybe you just want to steal all the fridges and get them to your super secret base, you do you, I'm just here to help.

M911 was the workhorse of US Armed forces for decades, his last job was to transport M1 Abrams tanks to battlefield. It's huge, it's ugly and it's one of the most iconic semi-trucks ever made. It comes in normal, armored and burnt flavor, with many texture variations.

[b]All variants include:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Fully animated[/b] - They all come with 3d custom parts, animations and craftable armor
[*][b]Run-flat Tires[/b] - They will run even when damaged, but with reduced friction
[*][b]Central tire inflation system[/b] - Monitors tire pressure, if it gets too low, it pumps it back up just like in rl
[*][b]Lightbar[/b] - Make a distraction with some light and sound effects 
[*][b]Trunk[/b] is divided in 2 compartments (it's not huge, but you can organize your gear)
[*][b]Spare tire [/b]
[*][b]Gas tank[/b] is armored
[/list]

[b]Military Semi-Trailers[/b] (USMC Green, NATO Green, Desert Tan and Woodland camo)
[list]
[*][b]M126 Stake Semi-Trailer[/b]
[*][b]M127 Van Semi-Trailer[/b]
[*][b]M128 Van Semi-Trailer[/b]
[*][b]M747 Lowbed Semi-Trailer[/b]
[*][b]M967 Fuel Tanker Semi-Trailer[/b] (Requires [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=2392709985]Tsar's Common Library[/url] to have fuel functionality)

[/list]

[b]Trailers are NOT towable by normal vehicles, only M911, but the M911 can also tow normal trailers, M911 is not compatible with other semi mods, they use different connection method.

You attach the trailer by aligning the truck under the trailer just like in the pictures above, exit, press v and select + from radial menu just like with normal trailers.[/b]

[b][list]
[*]B41.78+ Single Player and Multiplayer Compatible
[*]New vehicles, not replacing any other
[*]You can use it in your current save game, just enable the mod in the load menu
[*]2 seats inside
[*]It can tow and be towed (only from the rear)
[*]Not recolorable
[/list][/b]

[b]If you like my mods, please consider supporting me with a coffee[/b],
so I don't fall asleep while making new mods and supporting old ones :)

[url=https://ko-fi.com/ki5mods][img]https://i.imgur.com/2uTskWl.png[/img][/url]

Check out my other mods:

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2490220997][img]https://i.imgur.com/1C4iQ6U.png[/img][/url]

Credits:

Fuel tanker functionality was made possible by iBrRus and the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=2392709985]Tsar's Common Library[/url]

Awesome maps in previews: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2522833452]Coyote County Araxas[/url], [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1516836158]Fort Redstone[/url] and [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2552627090]Fort Crosstown[/url]

Special thanks to bikinihorst for the initial idea, all help along the way and two months of internal alpha and beta testing, and German localization.

You can also get a water tanker variants as an addon for this mod here: 
[url=steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2719592131]Tread's Water Tank Trucks[/url] or [url=steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2732639855]Water Trailer[/url]

[quote=KI5]

Ask for permission
This mod can only be added to and extended with the express permission from the original creator. If no permission is received you may not alter the mod, and it must be treated as a mod that is 'On Lockdown'. You are not allowed to repack this mod under any circumstances, ever. [/quote]

publishedfiledetails.title: Neat Crafting & Neat Building – XP & Mod Display
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]Build Support: B42.12.0 to B42.16 (Build 42 only)[/h2]
[b]Neat Crafting & Neat Building – XP & Mod Display[/b] adds optional [b]XP gain[/b] and [b]source mod[/b] information to the recipe lists of [b]Neat Crafting[/b] and [b]Neat Building[/b].

It is a lightweight companion addon for Build 42, focused on [b]XP + Mod display[/b], optional filters, text scaling, and clean auto-sizing recipe lines.

[h2]📦 What this Workshop item includes[/h2]
This Workshop item contains [b]two separate addons[/b]. Enable only the ones you use.
[list]
[*] [b]Neat Crafting — XP & Mod Display[/b]
[list]
[*] Requires: [b]Neat Crafting[/b]
[*] Must be loaded [b]AFTER[/b] Neat Crafting
[/list]

[*] [b]Neat Building — XP & Mod Display[/b]
[list]
[*] Requires: [b]Neat Building[/b]
[*] Must be loaded [b]AFTER[/b] Neat Building
[/list]
[/list]

[h2]📌 Recommended Load Order[/h2]
[list]
[*] NeatUI Framework
[*] Neat Crafting
[*] Neat Building
[*] Neat Crafting — XP & Mod Display
[*] Neat Building — XP & Mod Display
[/list]

[h2]✨ Core Features[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]XP per recipe:[/b] shows how much XP you gain and [b]which skill[/b] receives it
[*] [b]Display modes:[/b] Base XP / Final XP / Multiplier / Full breakdown
[*] [b]Optional “Mod” line:[/b] shows which mod a recipe comes from
[*] [b]Auto-resize + text scaling:[/b] recipe boxes grow to avoid clipped text
[*] [b]Optional filters:[/b] [b]XP only[/b] / [b]Mod only[/b]
[/list]

[h2]🧰 Addon Details[/h2]
[h3]Neat Crafting — XP & Mod Display[/h3]
[list]
[*] XP and optional Mod source lines in the recipe list
[*] Optional [b]XP only[/b] / [b]Mod only[/b] filters
[*] Auto-sizing recipe entries to keep longer lines readable
[*] [b]Build 42.15.x:[/b] fully working again on recent builds
[/list]

[h3]Neat Building — XP & Mod Display[/h3]
[list]
[*] XP and optional Mod source lines in the recipe list
[*] Optional [b]XP only[/b] / [b]Mod only[/b] filters
[*] Text scaling for extra lines
[*] Correct recipe entry resizing for taller entries
[*] [b]Build 42.15.x:[/b] fully working again on recent builds, including:
[list]
[*] vanilla building recipes
[*] Neat Building recipes
[/list]
[/list]

[b]Important:[/b] the latest base versions of [b]Neat Crafting[/b] and [b]Neat Building[/b] already include many recent compatibility fixes and several UI improvements that used to be bundled here.

This Workshop item is now focused on [b]XP + Mod display features[/b] and their related filters.

[h2]🌍 Translations[/h2]
[list]
[*] CH, CN, DA, DE, EN, ES, FR, HU, IT, JP, KO, NL, PL, PT, PTBR, RU, TH, TR, UA
[/list]

[h2]⚠️ Multiplayer / Saves Note[/h2]
In Build 42 multiplayer, some mods may register [b]WorldDictionary[/b] keys. If a world was saved with certain mods enabled and they are later removed, that save may fail to load.

[b]Rule of thumb:[/b]
[list]
[*] If a world has used [b]Neat Crafting[/b] or [b]Neat Building[/b], avoid removing them from that save or server
[*] Always make a full backup before changing mod lists on MP
[/list]

[h2]🔄 How to force a Workshop update[/h2]
[list]
[*] Close Project Zomboid completely
[*] Unsubscribe from this mod
[*] Wait until Steam finishes removing it
[*] Subscribe again and wait for the download to finish
[*] Launch the game
[/list]

[h2]Credits[/h2]
[list]
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3502080466]Neat Crafting[/url] & [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3536052310]Neat Building[/url] — base mods by [b]Rocco[/b]
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3378285185]Starlit Library[/url] — reflection logic by [b]albion[/b]
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3428715523]Crafting XP Display[/url] — inspiration by [b]N0wh3re[/b]
[/list]

[h2]More mods & feedback[/h2]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199133259679/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]You can find my other Project Zomboid mods here[/url]
[img]https://images.steamusercontent.com/ugc/819002974403344349/DD18664314A26EA83C62E9B0127FFCB35092B53A/[/img]
If you find this Workshop item useful, please consider leaving a [b]thumbs up[/b] and adding it to your [b]favorites[/b] — it helps other players discover it.
Feedback and suggestions are always welcome.

publishedfiledetails.title: Share Map Notes [B42][WIP]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[b]This is a work in progress, it's still being tested in MP.[/b]

Vanilla PZ has a Share button on the map, but it's manual — you have to click it, select a symbol, configure sharing, and apply, one symbol at a time. This mod automates all of that. Symbols and notes are shared instantly when you place them, and freehand drawings sync to all players in real-time.

[h1]Features[/h1]
[list]
[*] [b]Auto-Share Symbols[/b] — Map symbols and text notes are automatically shared the moment you place them. No need to use the vanilla Share button — it just works. Uses PZ's native sharing system so symbols look and behave exactly like normal ones.
[*] [b]Freehand Drawing[/b] — Draw directly on the map to mark routes, danger zones, or anything you want. Drawings sync to other players in real-time.
[*] [b]Pen Colors[/b] — Drawing color matches your selected pen (black, gray, red, blue, green), just like native map symbols. Requires a pen in inventory by default.
[*] [b]Erase[/b] — Click on any of your own drawings to erase them. Requires an eraser in inventory by default.
[*] [b]Undo[/b] — Remove your most recent drawing with one click.
[*] [b]Toggle Visibility[/b] — Show or hide shared drawings with a button on the map.
[/list]

[h1]Sandbox Options[/h1]
[list]
[*] [b]Auto-Share Map Symbols[/b] — Control who sees your symbols: Everyone (default), Faction only, Safehouse only, or Disabled (vanilla manual sharing).
[*] [b]Max drawings[/b] per player and total on the server.
[*] [b]Require pen to draw[/b] (default: on) — disable to let anyone draw without a pen.
[*] [b]Require eraser to erase[/b] (default: on) — disable to let anyone erase without an eraser.
[/list]

[h1]Sharing Modes[/h1]
The Auto-Share setting applies to both symbols and drawings:
[list]
[*] [b]Everyone[/b] — All players on the server see your symbols and drawings.
[*] [b]Faction[/b] — Only members of your faction see your symbols and drawings.
[*] [b]Safehouse[/b] — Only members of your safehouse see your symbols and drawings.
[*] [b]Disabled[/b] — Symbols use vanilla manual sharing (Share button). Drawings are private (only visible to you).
[/list]

[h1]Compatibility[/h1]
Build 42 multiplayer. Server-authoritative with rate limiting, per-player limits, and persistent storage across restarts.

[h1]Mid-Save Compatible[/h1]
Can be added to an existing save at any time.

[h1]Support[/h1]
If your squad's map game improved, help me chart the next update:
[url=https://ko-fi.com/faitas][img]https://i.imgur.com/Utx6OIH.png[/img][/url]

[h1]My Mod Collection[/h1]
This is the list of mods I use on the server I play with my friends: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3676815412]Faitas' PZ MP Mod Collection[/url]

Workshop ID: 3676995511
Mod ID: PZShareMapNotes

publishedfiledetails.title: Inspect Items Framework
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]Inspect Items Framework – Advanced 2D & 3D Item Inspection[/h2]

[b]Project Zomboid’s first full inspection framework with dynamic 3D preview, photo mode and modular stats engine.[/b]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/47KRI8p.png[/img]

[i]This mod replaces the old vanilla tooltip with a modern, fully-modular inspection window capable of displaying both 2D sprites and real-time 3D models with rotation, zoom, and photo showcase tools.

Right-click any item → Inspect Item
Left-click any item → Key [o]

The system automatically analyzes the item, routes it to its correct module, and generates a detailed readable description with accurate stats.
Supports all vanilla items, all modded items, and third-party plugins.[/i]

[hr]

[h2]Main Features[/h2]

[list]
[*][b]Modern Inspection Window:[/b] Large, clean, Tarkov-inspired interface with adaptive layout and smooth scaling.
[*][b]Dual Mode Viewer – 2D & 3D:[/b] Switch instantly between sprite view and live 3D model rendering.
[*][b]New “Photo Mode”:[/b] Expands the 3D view into a clean showcase (no text, no stats, perfect for screenshots and thumbnails).
[*][b]Interactive 3D Controls:[/b]
 • Rotate with RMB
 • Zoom with mouse wheel
 • Double-click to AutoFit & Reset
 • Optional Auto-Rotate toggle
[*][b]Resizable Panel:[/b] Dynamic scaling of text, icons, spacing, and 3D viewport—nothing breaks at any window size.
[*][b]Smart Categorization:[/b] Weapons, Firearms, Melee, Ammo, Clothing, Electronics, Tools, Food, Books, Medical, Junk, Materials… automatically routed.
[*][b]80+ Technical Stats Supported:[/b] Condition, armor, ballistic penetration, encumbrance, nutrition, fluid data, electronics specs, durability, recoil, and much more.
[*][b]Automatic Descriptions:[/b] A clean narrative summary is generated using the item’s script data + contextual stat analysis.
[*][b]Modular Architecture:[/b] 16+ internal modules handle categories independently—maximum compatibility, zero conflicts.
[*][b]Plugin API for Modders (v3.0+):[/b] Add custom stat modules, override item logic, or post-process descriptions with one simple registration call.
[*][b]Works With ALL Mods:[/b] Brita, ORGM, Arsenal(26), Hydrocraft, custom weapons, clothing, electronics, tools—anything with an icon or model.
[/list]

[hr]

[h2]New in This Version (Framework v1.6 — UI Themes & B42.13 Fixes)[/h2]

[list]
[*][b]Global UI Theme System:[/b] Added a full framework-level theme system for the Inspect UI.
[*][b]Multiple Built-in Themes:[/b] Includes Default (VanillaPlus), Minimal, Tarkov-inspired, Retro, Amber Terminal, Green Terminal, and Vampire (Red/Black).
[*][b]Live Theme Switching:[/b] Change themes instantly from the Inspect header without closing any windows.
[*][b]Unified Theme Across Windows:[/b] All open Inspect windows stay perfectly synchronized with the selected theme.
[*][b]Persistent Theme Selection:[/b] Your chosen UI theme is saved and restored across sessions and world reloads.
[*][b]Improved B42.13 Stat Handling:[/b] Fixed incorrect Uses and missing Condition stats caused by undocumented engine changes.
[*][b]Framework-Level Stability Improvements:[/b] More robust stat validation and safer handling of edge cases in Build 42.13.
[/list]

[i]Requires Project Zomboid Build 42.13 or newer.
Earlier builds do not receive this framework update.[/i]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/6rLXChR.png[/img]

[hr]

[h2]How to Use[/h2]

[list]
[*]Right-click any item in your inventory.
[*]Select “Inspect Item”.
[*]Use the top buttons:
 • 2D / 3D – Switch viewer mode
 • ROTATE – Toggle auto-rotation
 • PHOTO – Clean screenshot mode
[*]Use mouse wheel to zoom.
[*]Hold RMB to rotate.
[*]Double-click the 3D viewer to reset & auto-fit.
[/list]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/sZyeIR2.png[/img]

[hr]

[h2]Window Controls[/h2]

[list]
[*][b]Double-click border:[/b] Reset window to default size
[*][b]Shift + Double-click border:[/b] Expanded analysis size
[*][b]Alt + Double-click border:[/b] Near full-screen mode
[/list]

publishedfiledetails.title: Dead Man's Dossier [B42][MP]
publishedfiledetails.description:
Police, military, medical, firefighter, and ranger zombies drop torn document pages. Collect all pages for a tier, assemble a dossier, and follow the coordinates to a hidden supply stash. The loot spawns on the ground at the stash location — gear up and survive.

[h1]How It Works[/h1]
[list]
[*] Kill zombies wearing police, military, medical, firefighter, or ranger outfits
[*] They have a chance to drop torn document pages
[*] Collect all pages for a tier, then right-click a page to [b]Assemble Dossier[/b]
[*] A progress bar plays while your character reads and assembles the pages
[*] The completed dossier reveals coordinates to a hidden supply stash
[*] A [b]colored marker[/b] appears on your world map showing the stash location
[*] Travel to the stash and right-click the dossier to [b]Verify Stash Location[/b]
[*] Proximity hints guide you in — color-coded "getting warmer" feedback
[*] When you're close enough, loot drops on the ground at the stash tile for you to pick up
[*] If your map marker disappears (e.g. after a server restart), [b]Verify Stash Location[/b] or [b]View Stash Map[/b] will restore it automatically
[*] Don't want the mission? Right-click the dossier and choose [b]Abandon Mission[/b] to discard it and start fresh
[/list]

[h1]Five Tiers[/h1]
[list]
[*] [b]Police[/b] (2 pages) — Pistols, shotguns, ammo, police clothing, bulletproof vest, holster, walkie-talkie
[*] [b]Military[/b] (3 pages) — Assault rifles, pistols, magazines, scopes, ammo, camo gear, military backpack, ALICE webbing, bulletproof vest
[*] [b]Medical[/b] (2 pages) — Antibiotics, bandages, suture needles, painkillers, surgical tools, scrubs
[*] [b]Firefighter[/b] (2 pages) — Axe, sledgehammer, crowbar, fireman gear, blow torch, bolt cutters, welding mask
[*] [b]Ranger[/b] (2 pages) — Hunting rifle, shotguns, knife, hatchet, scopes, ranger clothing, compass, rope, trap box
[/list]
Each page has immersive lore text — internal memos, classified reports, incident logs, and field notes that tell the story of Knox County's fall.

[h1]Where to Find Pages[/h1]
Pages drop from zombies wearing the matching outfit. They also spawn in relevant containers:
[list]
[*] Police pages in police station lockers, desks, and gun storage
[*] Military pages in army hangars, surplus stores, and military storage
[*] Medical pages in hospital drug cabinets, clinics, and medical storage
[*] Firefighter pages in fire department lockers
[*] Ranger pages in ranger outfit containers and tool storage
[/list]

[h1]Map Markers[/h1]
When you assemble a dossier, a colored marker is placed on your world map at the stash location. Each tier has a distinct color. Markers persist across saves and can be toggled on/off with a button on the map screen.

[h1]Sandbox Options[/h1]
Server admins can tune everything under [b]Dossier Mod[/b] in Sandbox Options:
[list]
[*] [b]Page drop chance per tier[/b] — High (30%) / Normal (15%) / Low (8%) / Very Low (3%)
[*] [b]Page container spawn rate[/b] — How often pages appear in world containers (Common / Uncommon / Rare / Very Rare)
[*] [b]Stash reward rarity[/b] — Multiplier for reward drop chances (Generous 2x / Normal 1x / Scarce 0.5x / Minimal 0.1x)
[*] [b]Stash proximity radius[/b] — How close you need to be to collect rewards (5-50 tiles, default 15)
[*] [b]Use Custom Rewards[/b] — Load reward items from a config file instead of the built-in tables (see below)
[/list]

[h1]Custom Rewards (Admin Config)[/h1]
Server admins can fully customize what items each stash tier drops:
[list]
[*] Start the server once — a config file is auto-generated at [b]Zomboid/Lua/DeadMansDossier_Rewards.cfg[/b]
[*] Edit the file to add, remove, or change items and their drop chances
[*] Enable [b]Use Custom Rewards[/b] in Sandbox Options
[*] Restart the server — custom rewards are now active
[/list]
The config file uses a simple format:
[code]
[Police]
Base.Pistol = 0.40
Base.Shotgun = 0.25
# Base.Sledgehammer = 0.05   (commented out = disabled)

[Military]
Base.AssaultRifle = 0.05
SomeMod.CustomWeapon = 0.10   (cross-mod items work too)
[/code]
The [b]Reward Rarity[/b] sandbox multiplier still applies on top of custom chances. Items from uninstalled mods are silently skipped. If the config file is missing or has errors, the mod falls back to the built-in defaults.

[h1]Cross-Mod Support[/h1]
Military stash caches can drop [b]X-Virus[/b] and Medical stash caches can drop [b]Knox Antidote[/b]. Works with or without those mods installed — if the mod isn't active, the item is silently skipped.

[h1]Compatibility[/h1]
[list]
[*] Build 42
[*] Multiplayer and singleplayer
[*] Mid-save compatible — can be added to an existing save
[/list]

[h1]Roadmap[/h1]
[list]
[*] Stash loot currently drops on the ground — planning to add a supply crate container in a future update
[/list]

Feedback and suggestions welcome! If a stash location seems off, please let us know and we'll fix it.

[h1]Support[/h1]
Found the stash? Help me hide the next one:
[url=https://ko-fi.com/faitas][img]https://i.imgur.com/Utx6OIH.png[/img][/url]

[h1]My Mod Collection[/h1]
This is the list of mods I use on the server I play with my friends: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3676815412]Faitas' PZ MP Mod Collection[/url]

Workshop ID: 3675740871
Mod ID: DeadMansDossier

publishedfiledetails.title: '70 Ford Escort

Watch out for the RS, it is fast!

publishedfiledetails.title: '97 ADI Bushmaster
publishedfiledetails.description:
It's 1997, Australia was under a full quarantine for four long years, but they managed to keep the virus out. Now, they are sending their first RECON teams around the world, and one of the highest priorities is the Knox County. A few small teams have tried to enter it from different sides, but as soon as they got near the barricades, strange things started happening... 

Bushmaster will not spawn in the normal zones but it has a chance to spawn on a few county barricades and some secret locations around the map, those specials spawns are very rare. Spawns can be completely disabled from the sandbox settings, they are also not affected by the normal map conditions, so you will see them spawn in visited areas, if you are lucky.

[b]Main features:[/b]
[list]
[*]2 Chassis variants with their own features:
Utility(10 seats + Gunner) and Ambulance (6 Seats + Gunner + Stretcher)
[*]Fully animated hood, trunk, doors and other parts
[*]Visible interior and character (with improved exit/enter/aim animations)
[*]Openable roof hatch that can be used to shoot
[*]All parts have 3d objects you can remove and place in the world
[*]Side storages can be removed and replaced with other items, it can carry a generator or a spare tire instead
[*]Roof can hold additional storage and another spare tire
[*]Many texture variations
[*]Armor protection for windows, doors, engine, headlights etc
[*]Run-flat Tires - They will run even when damaged, but with reduced friction
[*]Central tire inflation system - Monitors tire pressure, if it gets too low, it pumps it back up just like in rl
[/list]
[b][list]
[*]You can enable/disable it's spawn in sandbox settings for mp
[*]You can use it in your current save game, just enable the mod in the load menu
[*]It can tow and be towed
[*]Not recolorable
[/list][/b]

If you can't wait to find this mod in your game and want to test it right away, use [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2634426926]bikinitools[/url] to spawn it, hide the items that your character holds while in vehicle, or for all other good stuff that mod has, go get it!

publishedfiledetails.title: [B41/B42+] Thumping Attracts Zombies
publishedfiledetails.description:
[b]Works on B41.78, B42.13.0, B42.13.1 and MP[/b]

[h1][b]ABOUT[/b][/h1]
When zombies thump on barricades or objects, the noise they generate attracts additional nearby zombies.
The sound scales dynamically based on how many zombies are hitting the same object, causing sieges to naturally escalate over time.

[b]Sandbox Options[/b]
The mod is fully configurable through Sandbox settings:
[list]
   [*] Base sound range generated by a single zombie
   [*] How much the sound scales per additional zombie
   [*] Scaling exponent to control how aggressive the growth is
   [*] Maximum sound range limit
[/list]
[i]All settings are optional and can be adjusted to fit vanilla, hardcore, or custom server experiences.[/i]

[h1][b]FAQ[/b][/h1]
[list]
   [*] Q: Does this mod work on existing saves?
[list]
   [*] A: Yes. It works on already started games without issues.
[/list]
   [*] Q: Is it safe to enable, disable, or uninstall mid-save?
[list]
   [*] A: Yes.
[/list]
   [*] Q: Does it work in multiplayer?
[list]
   [*] A:
[list]
   [*] Build 41: Yes, it works correctly in multiplayer.
   [*] Build 42: [strike]I am currently unable to test multiplayer due to a personal issue/bug preventing me from playing MP on B42. In theory, it should work without problems.
If anyone is able to test and confirm multiplayer behavior on B42, feedback is greatly appreciated.[/strike] [b]I received information that it works correctly in multiplayer![/b]
[/list]
[/list]
   [*] Q: Does this mod cause incompatibilities with other mods?
[list]
   [*] A: No. It uses vanilla systems and should not conflict with other addons.
[/list]
   [*] Q: Does this mod impact performance?
[list]
   [*] A: No noticeable performance impact. The system runs at low frequency and is optimized for both singleplayer and multiplayer.
[/list]
[/list]

[h1][b]KNOWN ISSUES[/b][/h1]
[b]None at the moment.[/b]

[b]Enjoy.[/b]

Workshop ID: 3646787580
Mod ID: ThumpingAttractsZombies

publishedfiledetails.title: Delta-Creek-Munitions-B42 Alpha
publishedfiledetails.description:
Hidden deep in the forests along a winding river sits Delta Creek — a once-busy industrial research and logistics complex now swallowed by silence.

Originally developed as a multi-facility operations center, Delta Creek features:

Large industrial warehouses

Administrative and office buildings

Connected production wings

Security checkpoints and controlled access roads

River access and bridge crossing

Expansive parking and loading zones

Surrounded by dense woodland and natural water barriers, the site feels isolated — intentionally so. Wide service roads and structured layouts suggest planning, funding, and purpose… but the question remains:

What exactly was being done here?

The river cuts through the complex like a boundary line. Multiple structures sit positioned for oversight and containment. Some areas feel corporate. Others feel restricted.

Whether you approach as a scavenger, a survivor establishing a fortified base, or someone searching for answers — Delta Creek offers:

High-risk industrial interiors

Strong defensive potential

Large multi-floor buildings

Balanced open space and chokepoints


Designed for Build 42, Delta Creek integrates cleanly into the Knox County region while offering a distinct atmosphere separate from existing towns.

Explore. Secure. Or uncover what Delta Creek was never meant to share.


*The Fall of Delta Creek

Delta Creek was never meant to be front-line.

Listed as an industrial logistics and research facility, it was quietly marked as a military fallback point when Knox County began to collapse. As towns fell and highways became death traps, units pulled back to Delta Creek to regroup, resupply, and hold the river crossings.

Warehouses became staging grounds. Offices turned into command posts. Convoys filled the lots. Survivors were processed inside secured gates.

But the infection wasn’t just outside the perimeter.

An evac transport arrived compromised. Intake screening failed. Panic spread through tight corridors and warehouse floors built for efficiency — not containment.

By nightfall, Delta Creek was overrun from within.

The last radio transmission cut off mid-report.

Now the compound sits abandoned, vehicles scattered, doors left open — a fortified position that became a trap.

And whatever happened here… didn’t stay contained.*


Basements coming next!

 Feel free to comment and report bugs, some of these buildings were from b40 but I believe I fixed them up pretty good. :D

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3664508731

publishedfiledetails.title: Fire Resistence
publishedfiledetails.description:
This mod reduces the damage inflicted by fire, we have always thought that fire does too much damage and too fast. With this mod the fire has a more realistic damage: if it is touched for a moment you will not catch fire and there will be no burns but only minimal damage. To burn yourself you need to stop for a few moments in the fiery tile. This mod also affects the fire generated by bombs, however the blast damage will remain unchanged. The mod also includes sandbox options to customize the fire damage to your liking, but I recommend leaving them as they are by default if you want to make the fire realistic.

Workshop ID: 2863836591
Mod ID: FireResistence

publishedfiledetails.title: No more wiggly vehicle
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]No More Wiggly Vehicle[/h1]

[b]Driving in Project Zomboid shouldn't feel like fighting the keyboard.[/b]
This mod provides a subtle, physics-based steering assist to help you maintain a perfect straight line on long roads.

[h2]How it works[/h2]
[list]
[*] Automatically aligns your vehicle to the nearest 8 cardinal and diagonal directions (0°, 45°, 90°, etc.).
[*] The assist [b]ONLY[/b] activates when you are within a narrow [b]5-degree[/b] margin of the target angle.
[*] Instantly disengages the moment you touch the steering keys (A/D) or slow down below 30 km/h.
[*] You must keep your hands on the steering wheel [b](w key)[/b] to align the vehicle.
[*] You can configure whether the alignment works during cruise control in the mod [b]settings[/b].
[/list]

Tested with 'Realistic Car Physics'.
Safe to add or remove mid-save.
Multiplayer compatible.

Workshop ID: 3523472206
Mod ID: drivestraight

publishedfiledetails.title: Workshop Update Checker
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Workshop Update Checker[/h1]

A simple, lightweight mod for Project Zomboid that automatically checks your installed Workshop mods for updates and notifies you in-game if Steam has downloaded a new version since your last run. Designed for both players and server admins who want to stay up-to-date with the latest mod versions!

[hr][/hr]

[h2]✨ Key Features[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]Automatic Update Detection[/b]: Scans your active Workshop mods and alerts you if any have been updated since your last play session.
[*] [b]Copy/Open link[/b]: Allows you to open a workshop link in Steam Overlay,  or copy the link to clipboard.
[*] [b]Changelogs[/b]: Displays a changelog for any mod that includes a ChangeLog.txt in the "common" folder.
[*] [b]In-Game Popup[/b]: See a clear, user-friendly popup listing all mods with available updates, including version and changelog info (if available).
[*] [b]Performance Friendly[/b]: Uses caching and efficient Steam queries to minimize load times and avoid unnecessary checks.
[*] [b]No Impact on Gameplay[/b]: Purely informational—does not alter save files or mod data.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

[h2]⚙️ How To Use[/h2]
[list]
[*] Open [b]Mod Options > Workshop Update Checker[/b] to configure features and debug tools.
[*] The mod will automatically scan for updates on clicking the Mod Updates button (in the Main Menu).
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

[h2]🔧 Compatibility[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]Build 42.13[/b] compatible
[*] Works with all Workshop mods
[*] Multiplayer-compatible (client-side only)
[*] No conflicts—does not modify or override other mods
[*] [b]No Dependencies[/b]: No other mods required.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

[h2]🐛 Troubleshooting[/h2]
[list]
[*] If you don't see update notifications, ensure the mod is enabled and check Mod Options for configuration.
[*] Use the debug options to test update detection and popup behavior.
[*] If you encounter issues, try wiping the cache and restarting the game.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

[h2]⭐ Support & Feedback[/h2]
If you find this mod useful:
[list]
[*] ⭐ Rate on Steam Workshop
[*] 💬 Leave comments or feedback!
[*] Support: [url=https://ko-fi.com/anachroschism]Ko-fi[/url]
[/list]

Enjoy seamless mod management and never miss an update again!

[b][url=https://theindiestone.com/forums/index.php?/topic/2530-mod-permissions/#comment-36478]Ask for permission:[/url][/b]
[code]This mod can only be added to and extended with the express permission from the original creator. Having received permission, credit must be given to the original creator, both within the files of the mod and wherever the mod roams online.[/code]

publishedfiledetails.title: USS + VFE Universal Silencers Patch (B42.13.2)
publishedfiledetails.description:
🧸 English (Workshop description)

When I downloaded Vanilla Firearms Expansion + VFES and VFE93 by Marz, I noticed something missing: VFE weapons didn’t have suppressors.
The problem was that Universal Simple Silencers by Nepenthe and Simple Silencers by Monkey Magic didn’t allow suppressors to be installed on those weapons.

So I made this patch — it adds Universal Simple Silencers support for all VFE weapons, so you can enjoy your gear… quietly. 😉

Huge thanks to the authors of these mods! By creating and maintaining them, you help me (and many others) experience our favorite game in new ways every time! 🥂

✅ No extra steps required — just install the required mods and the patch.
Have fun, and thanks for checking it out!

If you’d like to support what I do, take a look here, your help lets me work on mods in my free time 🤝
https://ko-fi.com/jussy9375


Requires: Simple Silencers + NepUniversalSS + [42] Vanilla Firearms Expansion plus VFES and VFE93

🧸 Русский (Описание Мода)

Когда я скачал Vanilla Firearms Expansion + VFES and VFE93 от Marz, я заметил, что у оружия из VFE нет глушителей.
Проблема в том, что Universal Simple Silencers от Nepenthe и Simple Silencers от Monkey Magic не позволяли поставить глушители на это оружие.

Поэтому я сделал этот патч — он добавляет поддержку Universal Simple Silencers для всего оружия VFE, чтобы вы могли радоваться своему арсеналу... но радоваться тихо. 😉

Огромное спасибо авторам этих модов! Вы делаете колоссальную работу и даёте возможность каждый раз по-новому погружаться в мою любимую игру! 🥂

publishedfiledetails.title: Repair Any Clothes
publishedfiledetails.description:
Makes you able to repair any clothes you want, such as vests, bulletproof vests, jumpsuits, etc.

Working for B42!


[h1] Are you generous and thankful for the mod? [/h1]
[url=https://www.rspca.org.uk/getinvolved/donate/online] Please consider to donate to animals in need! [/url]

[i] In 4 years and over 1.3 million concurrent subscribers, I have only earned 5 euros in that entire time from mods.
It was a very rewarding 5 euros, I mean who the hell ever gets paid for their hobby?
But that's exactly what it is, a hobby, and I would honestly feel better in my heart knowing that an animal gets to live a happy life instead of me having some pocket change for a coffee because of a zombie game, and my wife being a seamstress. [/i]

[i] 
Thank you CarlosRojasCu for updating the mod when I was busy
Thank you everyone for almost half a decade of positive comments, happy moments and for being grateful.
You all helped me stay focused and inspired to challenge myself and be a better person.[/i]




Have you ever found a snazzy Jumpsuit, only to be forced to throw it out, because of ugly holes?
And what of the lovely Santa Suits, for when you wanted to bring Christmas Joy to the Undead of Kentucky, only to have them crying like Snoopy from "The Peanuts" because all the Santa Suits were ruined.

Have you ever wanted to express your inner fursona in an apocalypse, but alas, you had to make due with your human skin, because all Spiffo suits were fit for peasants?
What about all the poor gopniks and lovely slavs who need to show off their perfect Adidas suit only to have some filthy rotter chomp in their lovely shell suit jacket or pants?
And of course, your ultra cool survivors with mood swings had no way to express their darkness, and how unique they were without their fingerless gloves.

What about your trusty boots or fancy leather shoes? Got a hole in them, and for some reason you can't slap some duct tape on it?
That's also taken care of now, as you too can sit down, and master the art of becoming a cobbler, also known as a shoemaker!
Fix those pesky holes with the corresponding material, and you can walk over all the puddles Kentucky has to offer, water or brains from the zombies you've stomped!

Search no longer weary traveler, because gone are the days of throwing out clothes you, for some reason, couldn't fix!
WASTE NOT WANT NOT with this mod, as it allows you to fix all clothing, so whether you're a hobo, furry, teenage flick vampire, military man with enough equipment to break your back, YOU TOO, can look your very best in the Apocalypse!

This mod puts all the clothes you couldn't fix before, and makes them fixable.
No limitations, no telling you "You can't fix broken shoes".
Just this one mod that does everything, and it's completely vanilla friendly.




[h1] FAQ [/h1]
Q: Does this work with my version, multiplayer, singleplayer?
A: It works with Build 41 both multi and single, and build 42 too!!!

Q: How do I fix my clothes completely without having patches on them?
A: Level your tailoring to at least 8!

Q: I can't repair something
A: Make sure you have needle, thread and ripped sheets, denim or leather strips.

Q: I still can't repair it!
A: Then another mod is breaking it for you. Enable only this mod to see if it works, then start enabling the other mods you use to find the broken mod.

Q: I use Brita's Armor Pack, Rileh's SWAT pack and or Authentic Z, can I repair those items now?
A: Yes!

Q: Am I allowed to repack your mod, alter it in any way and upload it to the workshop?
A: Only if you put it under a different name, artwork, and if you're running a server and need to alter it, privatize it.

Q: Do I need to enable both mods? (Repair Any Mod / Repair Any Clothes)
A: You only need Repair Any Clothes. "Repair Any Mod" is left from B41 and will be discontinued when B42 is fully released. I left it so multiplayer servers won't break.


In your server settings turn off AntiCheatProtectionType21.
Use link below if you're still having issues.
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3417055526] If you're having trouble joining or starting your server that was running the old version, a B41 version can be found here, from before the mod was improved.[/url]


[img]https://steamuserimages-a.akamaihd.net/ugc/819002974403344349/DD18664314A26EA83C62E9B0127FFCB35092B53A/[/img]

Workshop ID: 2142622992
Mod ID: RepairAnyClothesOld
Mod ID: RepairAnyClothes
Mod ID: RepairAnyMod
Mod ID: RepairAnyMod

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42] Plain Moodle Resurrection
publishedfiledetails.description:
A remake of the original Plain Moodles mod, completely redrawn in a higher resolution and utilizing the Moodles in Lua framework for broader compatibility. Build 42 only.

This mod changes the background style of your moodles, giving them a unique design that stands out compared to the vanilla background design.

[h3] Background [/h3]

The original mod is one of my favorite designs for moodle backgrounds. With the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3008416736]original mod[/url] and its [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3394582336]successor mod[/url] being essentially abandoned for proper support, I figured that I should take the matter into my own hands to ensure that these moodle background designs do not get lost to time. I also wanted to ensure that this version is backed by a reliable moodle framework so that compatibility issues do not occur again. All credit goes to the original authors. They deserve the respect, not me.

[hr][/hr] 
[h3] Styles [/h3]

Background + Bar (Plain Moodles Original)
[img]https://i.imgur.com/xYxVOYy.gif[/img]

Minimal Bar (Plain Moodles Original)
[img]https://i.imgur.com/t8LZ4H6.gif[/img]

Full Background (New, following the Plain Moodles design)
[img]https://i.imgur.com/1HbfjiD.gif[/img]

[hr][/hr] 

[h3]How do I use this mod?[/h3]
[olist][*]Subscribe to this mod, as well as Moodles in Lua.
[*]Within Project Zomboid, open up your mods section in the main menu and make sure both mods are enabled. Then, open up your options menu and navigate to the "Mods" tab.
[*]Under Moodles in Lua, select the drop-down menu for "Border Texture Pack" and select which variant you would like to use. You are able to modify the style in-game, so try them all![/olist]

[h3]Compatibility?[/h3]
Should theoretically be compatible with everything that utilizes either Moodles in Lua or Moodle Framework. Any mods that change the default moodle textures or borders should work as well, just set the texture pack to "Disabled" so Moodles in Lua can use the textures from other mods. Mod order configuration may be necessary.

[h3]My moodles are inconsistently sized and overlapping![/h3]
In your options menu, under "User Interface (UI)", set the drop-down menu option for "Moodle Size" to be any predetermined size other than "Scale with font size."

[h3]I have a suggestion for a new style or for a modification to an existing style![/h3]
I'm always open to feedback, let me know and I'll be happy to add new styles or change existing ones.

[h3]Build 41?[/h3]
Moodles in Lua only supports B42 as of right now, so it is all I will support for now regarding this small revival project.

[hr][/hr]

Workshop ID: 3569718104
Mod ID: PMResurrection

publishedfiledetails.title: '85 Chevrolet Step-Van
publishedfiledetails.description:
Vehicle that doesn't really need any introduction, boxy urban workhorse that comes in base variant or some 30+ variants for various professions and brands found in Knox. Variants are based on step-van for b42 but work in b41 too, but without special spawns. It is big and slow but has lower requirements for fixing and crafting in some cases because of the simple body so it will be a good nomad companion when paired with a large roofrack that can hold some useful extras. Now with a SWAT variant!

[*]30+ variants
[*]3 Front bumpers and a rear bumper with 2 tiered protection
[*]Fully animated hood, trunk, doors, windows etc
[*]Visible interior and character (with improved exit/enter/aim animations)
[*]All parts have 3d objects you can remove and place in the world
[*]Extra parts to craft and find in the world to make the vehicle truly yours (bumpers, bullbars, window armor, roofrack etc)
[*]Multiple texture variations
[*]Armor protection for windows, doors, engine, headlights etc
[*]2 seats
[/list]
[b][list][*][*]You can use it in your current save game, just enable the mod in the load menu

publishedfiledetails.title: Better Server Settings
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Note:[/h1] There seems to be critical bug in the game. Sometime your local server can cache some information about server settings, which lead to termination on server start. Renaming server config should help with the problem.

[h1]Description:[/h1]
Better Server Settings change server setting: making it auto-polulate mod list, copying workshop ids and maps, enabling the use of Mod Manager inside server page and changing server window resizing behavior.

[list]
    [*]Auto-add mods: Every time you create [u]new[/u] server config, all your [b]active[/b] mods, including their workshop ID and maps, will be automatically added according to current [b]load order[/b].

    [*]Choose Mods: Instead of prompting to choose mods on every create/edit of setting file, the buttons inside [u]"Mods"[/u] tab, which is hooked to vanilla Mod Selector, become new way to manage server mods. This will allow you to change active server Modlist, and after saving it, [b]mods info will be copied to other tabs.[/b]

    [*]Resizing: UI now scales with game window.

Crucially, after you save changes, all lists will be reset and repopulated to follow order from Mod Manager for workshopitems, maps and spawnregions (if region does not follow standard path, like the one added manually, then it should not be reset)

[h1]Compatibility:[/h1]
Better Server Settings does not affect Mod Manager in any way, only hook it to server button. In such, any mod that alter mod manager should work fine. But if another mod also alter server setting interface, then compatibility can break

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3423660713][B42] Mod Load Order Sorter[/url] - have it's own patches for server settings, but skip it if Better Server Setting enabled, so both mods work together.
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3567084868][B42] Mod Manager[/url] - should work fine for not. This mod and any of it dependency work perfectly.. [u]Configuration[/u] probably target local configs for mods, not the server side, but if you run server locally too, it should catch them from [u]zomboid/documents[/u] anyway.
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2694448564][B41] Mod Manager[/url] - version of build 41 is recommended to be used alongside. All functionality supported.

[h1]Credits:[/h1]
All credits goes to The Indie Stone for creating this function and then commenting it.

Workshop ID: 2756434288
Mod ID: ClientModsToServer

publishedfiledetails.title: Reactive Sound Events
publishedfiledetails.description:
[quote]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/A7PzGG7.png[/img]
A complete overhaul of the meta-event system. Sounds now react to the world, spawn physical scenes you can investigate, and tell a story through a new survivor radio station.

The sounds adapt to [b]time[/b], [b]weather[/b], [b]location[/b], and [b]your playstyle[/b].
[/quote]

[quote]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/oU4MfpD.png[/img]
When you hear a distant sound, something actually happened there. Go find out what — if you dare.

[h3]Scene Types[/h3]
[list]
[*] [b]Gunfight[/b]: Battle aftermaths with weapons and ammo.
[*] [b]Vehicle Crash[/b]: Wrecked cars with lootable trunks and gloveboxes.
[*] [b]Campsite[/b]: Campsites and evidence of other survivors.
[*] And more...
[/list]
[/quote]

[quote]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/OmoMfmA.png[/img]
A directional [b]on-screen marker[/b] shows where the sound came from.
Customizable colors, opacity, and duration. Also available when the player enters a scene radius.

[h3]Indicator Features[/h3]
[list]
[*] [b]Directional[/b]: The marker points in the direction of the sound.
[*] [b]Customizable[/b]: Colors, opacity, and duration are customizable in Mod Options.
[*] [b]Scene Radius[/b]: Available when the player enters a scene radius.
[/list]
[/quote]

[quote]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/HIkDXiG.png[/img]
[b]A survivor radio station broadcasting from Knox County on 112.4 MHz.[/b]
Tune in to hear from [b]Marcus, Jenna, and Ray[/b]—three survivors with their own personalities, perspectives, and struggles.

[h3]Broadcast Schedule[/h3]
[list]
[*] [b]7:00 AM[/b] — Morning Show with survival tips and skill lessons.
[*] [b]1:00 PM[/b] — Midday broadcast with scene reports / story progression.
[*] [b]7:00 PM[/b] — Evening broadcast with scene reports / story progression.
[/list]

[h3]Features[/h3]
[list]
[*] [b]Tactical Intel[/b]: Hosts report coordinates of nearby scenes. Right-click your Radio, Walkie-Talkie, Ham Radio, or use your vehicle's radio to [b]Mark Intel[/b] on your map — now with custom map icons and animation feedback!
[*] [b]Learn & Survive[/b]: Pay attention. The hosts share survival tips that can actually teach your character new [b]skills[/b].
[*] [b]Evolving Narrative[/b]: Follow their story over 90+ in-game days. Listen as they adapt, fight, and face the horrors of the Exclusion Zone.
[/list]
[/quote]

[quote]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/vvChqxN.png[/img]
Your character is part of this world, not above it. Reactions include:
[list]
[*] [b]Panic[/b]: Loud events will increase your panic level. Cowardly characters suffer more.
[*] [b]Wake Up[/b]: Events near you can wake you up. Insomniacs beware.
[*] [b]Zombie Hearing[/b]: Zombies also hear these sounds and may investigate the source.
[*] [b]Adaptation[/b]: A veteran survivor (high days survived) won't panic as easily as a fresh spawn. You get used to the horror.
[/list]
[/quote]

[quote]
[h2]FEATURES[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]7 Event Types[/b] — Gunfight, Gunshot, Scream, vehicle Crash, Animal, Weather, Zombie.
[*] [b]Physical Scenes[/b] — Investigate the aftermath of events.
[*] [b]Visual Sound Indicator[/b] — Know where the sound came from.
[*] [b]Knox Relay Radio[/b] — In-game broadcasts with intel and story.
[*] [b]Deep Configuration[/b] — 50+ sandbox options to tweak every aspect.
[/list]
[/quote]

[quote]
[h2]COMPATIBILITY[/h2]
[list]
[*] Works in 42.15.2
[*] Build 42.13+ [b]REQUIRED[/b] (visit [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2969551071]Reactive Sound Events (Legacy)[/url] for Build 41 / Build 42.12 or less)
[*] Multiplayer [b]SUPPORTED*[/b] — Works on non-dedicated and dedicated servers (pending testing on the later).
[i] * Scenes are [b]SINGLEPLAYER ONLY[/b] for now. Waiting for a stable MP version before implementing them[/i]
[/list]

[h3]WITH OTHER MODS[/h3]
[list]
[*] [b]Weapon Mods[/b] — The mod will automatically detect and add modded [b][i]RANGED[/i][/b] weapons to the appropriate weapon pools. No manual configuration is required.
[*] [b]Vehicle Mods[/b] — The mod will automatically detect and add modded vehicles to the appropriate vehicle pools. No manual configuration is required.
[/list]
[/quote]

publishedfiledetails.title: '93 Ford Mustang
publishedfiledetails.description:
A very special Mustang, Special Service Package Mustang. It is smaller and lighter than the CVPI series, it comes in 4 variants: Regular Police, KY State Police, Unmarked, Decommissioned aka civilian variant and a brand new Colonel's Special with some serious power. They share 2 body chassis variants but have different equipment, some can be moved over to other variant, some are vehicle specific. They all can have a full set of craftable armor and a big old push bumper. Now with GT and SVT!
 
[b]Main features:[/b]
[list]
[*]9 Vehicle variants
[*]Multiple bumpers with 2 tiered protection
[*]Fully animated hood, trunk, doors and windows
[*]Visible interior and character (with improved exit/enter/aim animations)
[*]All parts have 3d objects you can remove and place in the world
[*]Extra parts to craft and find in the world to make the vehicle truly yours (spoilers, push bumper, bumpers, window armor etc)
[*]Multiple texture variations
[*]Armor protection for windows, doors, engine, headlights etc
[*]2 seats in the front, 2 in the back.
[/list]
[b][list]
[*]You can use it in your current save game, just enable the mod in the load menu
[*]It can tow and be towed
[*]Not recolorable

publishedfiledetails.title: Uncle Red's Bunker B42
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Description:[/h1]

[b]Uncle Red’s Bunker B42! Now With Redux Version Bundled![/b]

Crazy Uncle Red was an… Interesting dude. Served in "’Nam", conspiracy nut and all around crazy old guy, made some killer moonshine though.
He built this Bunker to live out whatever doomsday situation he dreamed up. He just finished it when the Feds got on his tail for bootlegging.
He ran down to mexico before he had a chance to stock up on supplies. He used to let you hunt out near his shine shack. Now that the dead are walking maybe uncle red wasn’t so crazy. Now For B42!

[b]Features[/b]
[list]
[*]Large machine Shed for vehicle storage
[*]Huge Yard for all your ranching needs
[*]Full household amenities
[*]Massive store rooms including: warehouse, armory, closet and pantry
[*]A Bar to just wait for all this to blow over while sipping some of Uncle Red’s ‘shine
[*]Two lakes perfect for fishing or foraging around.
[*]Near muldraugh with easy access to the Highway
[*]It’s a Bunker, man!
[*]Objects for use with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3042138819] Functional Appliances 2[/url] (hopefully let me know if something doesn't work)

[b]New Features In B42[/b]
[list]
[*]The Big One, it's actually underground now!
[*]More Buildings and rooms
[*]Actual garden plots
[*]Easier access to parking both exterior and interior for you semi truck users
[/list]
Just wanted to say thanks to [url=https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLc7_sQ-Tb6e3siC4Cwu4JfxuqJZkGO6sy] Daddy Dirkie dirk for his amazing tutorial series[/url] and fred cooper for helping me with the room defs, and inspiring me to create this mod after playing with his fantastic [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2889983340&searchtext=bunker] Last Minute Prepper mod [/url]

F.A.Q
Q: How do I unlock the doors?
A: it’s honestly luck of the draw whether they are unlocked or not, or if a key spawns if anyone has info on how to guarantee a key spawn let me know.

Q: Will it work in multiplayer?
A: No Idea, with multiplayer being in early beta for B42 I can't really say.

Q: mod conflicts with [Blank]
A: Sorry to hear that, through a lot of trial and error this was found to be the best spot (thanks to Postal)
and there are currently no plans to move it.

Q: Can I add this to an existing save?
A: Sure can, Use [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2547762158] This Guide [/url]

[b] Let me know if you run into any problems with the mod and any changes you'd like to see, thanks for your patience and support while I worked on updating this. hope you all enjoy it! [/b]

Cell: 36,36(B41)
Cells: 43,43 - 42,43 - 42,42 (B42)

Mod ID: UncleRedsBunkerRedux
