publishedfiledetails.title: [B42] Useful Barrels
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1] Barrels can actually be more than just a sprite! [/h1]
[b]B42 only! Now with MP support! [/b]
[b]Important: Mod development is currently discontinued. I no longer have the time or motivation to continue working on it. You have my full permission to adopt it and use any of the code or assets. Feel free to contact me if you have any questions about how the mod works.[/b]

[h2] Features available [/h2]
[list]
    [*]Use your Pipe Wrench to open the lid of the barrel!
    [*]Store any fluid inside
    [*]The amount of liquid inside the barrel affects the encumbrance
    [*]Rubber Hose to take gas from the barrel
    [*]Funnel for pouring gas into the barrel
    [*]Funnel may speedup pouring action
    [*]Refueling vehicle from the barrel. and vice versa
    [*]Refueling generator from the barrel
    [*]Randomized initial fluid and amount
    [*]Sandbox vars to control mod behaviour as you want
    [*]Chuckleberry Finn's Mod Update and Alert System integration for ingame ChangeLog
[/list]

[h2] Notes [/h2]
[list]
    [*]Uses new FluidContainer system of B42
    [*]Barrels are now less fragile! But anyway Walkers will break it pretty quickly
    [*]Gasoline does not evaporate from the barrels
    [*]Modded vehicles and trailers are supported if the vehicle has a part with the "gastank" category and the part containerType contains the word "Gasoline" (this information is mostly for modders)
    [*]Please! Any full 800L barrels in your characters pocket or car trunk is your responsibility, there is no proper way to haul them. For now at least.
    [*]I'd also recommend checking out the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3439305933]Functional Gutters[/url] mod.
[/list]

[h2] How to [/h2]
[h3] Base usage [/h3]
[list]
    [*]Find a Pipe Wrench and any metal barrel in the game (there are only four types available)
    [*]Move it to the garage at your base
    [*]Right click on it and select "Uncap ..." option
    [*]Find a Rubber Hose and a Funnel, then place them on the barrel to enable fluid transfer
[/list]
[h3] Refuel generator [/h3]
[list]
    [*]The generator must be near the barrel with at least 1 liter of fuel
    [*]The generator must be turned off and have free space for fuel
    [*]Find a Rubber Hose if you haven't found it yet, then place it on the barrel (or keep it in your inventory)
    [*]Right click on the generator and select "Refuel From Barrel"
[/list]
[h3] Refuel vehicle [/h3]
[list]
    [*]The vehicle must be near the player
    [*]The vehicle's engine must be turned off
    [*]The gas tank must have free space
    [*]The barrel with gas must be near the vehicle
    [*]Find a Rubber Hose if you haven't found it yet, then place it on the barrel (or keep it in your inventory)
[/list]
[h3] Rain collector barrel [/h3]
[list]
    [*]Find a Welding mask and a Welding torch with at least 2 uses
    [*]Right-click on the barrel
    [*]Find the "Cut Lid" option - it may appear at the top or inside the existing barrel submenu
[/list]

[h2] WIP/Future plans [/h2]
[list]
    [*]Ability to add fluid from other world objects such as: sink, bath, toilet or even other Useful Barrel (MP)
    [*]Cut the barrel lid and turn it into a rain collector (MP)
    [*]Visually represent fluid lvl in the lidless barrel at 75%, 80% and 90% (MP)
    [*]Compatibility with other mods (MP)
    [*]Ability to add fluids from items that are lying on the ground (MP)
    [*]Proper plumbing
    [*]Turn existing lidless barrels into functional ones
    [*]Polishing context options, translations
    [*]Add some UI
    [*]Connect the barrel to the generator with a hose and refuel it over time
[/list]

[h2]Mods compatibility[/h2]
[list]
    [*] Modded vehicles (and fluid trailers) should be compatible if: vehicle has part and part category contains "gastank", part contentType contains word "Gasoline".
    [*] Not tested with other mods so far
[/list]

[h2] Known bugs [/h2]
[list]
    [*]In case of game crash FuidContainer may not save it's contents.
[/list]

[h2] FAQ [/h2]
[list]
    [*]Q: b41 when?
    [*]A: I dont have any plans to port it on b41. And there is already a mod for b41, it's called [url=steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2688538916]FuelAPI[/url]
    [*]Q: How to move my 800l barrel now?
    [*]A: It's quite hard to implement right now properly. I hope TIS devs add some good functionality to makeit in the future.
[/list]

[h2] Credits [/h2]
Thanks a lot to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/ThisIsSP4RTAN]SP4RTAN[/url] for updating the translations for the 42.15 game update.
Thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199774161911]Mr_Ordinary_[/url] for UA translation.
Thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198022328017]浪里个浪2346134[/url] for CN translation.
Thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/NopOYes]NopOYes[/url] for PTBR translation.
Thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/mainfish]MainFish[/url] for RU translation.
Thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198062695591]Hx[/url] for KO translation.
Thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198355106439]RedSystem[/url] for TR translation.
Thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199781026961]Dust Runner[/url] for ES translation.
Thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198027815009]Bobitec[/url] for FR translation.
Thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561197963614977]Steven Ts[/url] for IT translation.
Thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199190958423]anormalЬ[/url] for DE translation.

publishedfiledetails.title: Open Ammo Boxes While Walking
publishedfiledetails.description:
B42.13 (Multiplayer update) Note: Only the vanilla version is available. The mod now makes the following tweaks:
- Allows ammo boxes and containers to be packed in the dark.
- For some reason, there's a small chance that the crafting recipe will do nothing when completed. You can just re-do it and it should go through.

Please also note that the devs of this game have been refactoring the crafting system during the B42 unstable beta, so each update tends to break this mod ;-;

------

This is a basic mod that edits the crafting recipes for opening ammo boxes to allow you to walk while opening ammo boxes. It also allows you to put ammo back into boxes while walking. Running and sprinting will still interrupt the action.

Includes 7 versions to support the many gun mods that exist :)
1) Vanilla
2) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2297098490]Arsenal/Brita[/url]
3) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2256623447]Firearms B41[/url]
4) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/nocapitals/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]Vanilla Firearms Expanded[/url]
5) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2874163136]CJ Firearms Expansion[/url]
6) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3077078907]Guns of 93[/url]
7) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3233647204]Rain's Firearms[/url]

For those using the non-vanilla versions, make sure that this mod loads AFTER Arsenal's/B41/VFE/CJ's mod. Otherwise, this mod will be overwritten and do nothing. Have trouble with the mod order? Usually, switching this mod off and then back on will fix it.

Enjoy!

publishedfiledetails.title: Vehicle Military Zones
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1] I warn you, all screenshots were taken on B42, it has quite noticeable differences from B41. [/h1]

The main goal of the mod is to add spawn zones for military vehicles in the appropriate places on the vanilla map. However, at the moment, several mod maps are also supported. [b][i][u] The mod doesn't add structures or anything like that. [/u][/i][/b]
If you have ideas where else I can add military zones, I will listen to you.

The mod is designed to use modded vehicles, as there are no military vehicles in vanilla. So if you only play with vanilla vehicles, this mod is useless for you.

[b][i][u] Can be added to/removed from existing save. [/u][/i][/b] When added to an existing save, the changes will only apply to unexplored areas. When removed from a save, already spawned cars will remain, but will no longer appear in other places.

If you want the cars to spawn only in military zones then load it after vehicle mods.

[h3] ---> [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3682033568]Old version[/url] <--- [/h3]

[hr][/hr]
[h3] Where are the zones: [/h3]
[list]
[*]Military base
[*]Louisville checkpoint
[*]East Army Camp near Louisville
[*]Brandenburg
[*]March Ridge
[*]Bridge near West Point
[*]Crossroads near Muldraugh and Louisville
[*]In small camps in Doe Valley and near Rosewood and Irvington
[/list]
[h3] Supported maps: [/h3]
[list]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1516836158]Fort Redstone[/url]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1414568604]Fort Knox[/url]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2806456685]Fort Rock Ridge[/url]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2934132344]Louisville Quarantine Zone[/url]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2196102849]Raven Creek[/url]/[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3484263516]Raven Creek B42[/url]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3476333350]Echo Creek Military Base[/url]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3494374578][B42] SecretZ Pandemic (Alpha)[/url]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3600377019]Foxtrot Warehouse [B42][/url]
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h2] Little bit of technical part [/h2]

All vehicles are sorted from VehicleZoneDistribution.military, but it also works with army, just adding vehicles from army to military.
The mod automatically removes all vehicles from other zones, so if you don't enable a special sandbox option, military vehicles will only spawn in military zones.

As I said earlier, the mod versions for B41 and B42 are slightly different and the main difference is the number of different zones.
[b] B41 version: [/b]
-> military_burnt (burnt military vehiles; determined by the presence of "burnt" in the vehicle's script name)
-> military_container (containers added by the mod [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2625625421]Containers![/url]; determined by the presence of "container" in the vehicle's script name)
-> military_trailer (trailers; determined by the presence of "trailer" in the vehicle's script name)
-> military_trailer_light (only small trailers; determined if offroad efficiency of trailer is less than or equal to 1)
-> military_trailer_heavy (only huge heavy trailers; determined if offroad efficiency of trailer is more than 1)
-> military_vehicle (military cars; remainder after sorting)
-> military_vehicle_light (light military cars; determined if (mass*engineForce)/(maxSpeed*1000) is less than or equal 60)
-> military_vehicle_heavy (heavy military cars; determined if (mass*engineForce)/(maxSpeed*1000) is more than 60)

[b] B42 version: [/b]
-> military_burnt (burnt military vehiles; determined by the presence of "burnt" in the vehicle's script name)
-> military_container (containers added by the mod [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2625625421]Containers![/url]; determined by the presence of "container" in the vehicle's script name)
-> military_container_angled (slightly rotated containers)
-> military_trailer (all trailers; determined by the presence of "trailer" in the vehicle's script name)
-> military_trailer_angled (slightly rotated trailers)
-> military_trailer_light (only small trailers; determined if offroad efficiency of trailer is less than or equal to 1)
-> military_trailer_light_angled (slightly rotated light trailers)
-> military_trailer_heavy (only huge heavy trailers; determined if offroad efficiency of trailer is more than 1)
-> military_trailer_heavy_angled (slightly rotated heavy trailers)
-> military_vehicle (all military cars; remainder after sorting)
-> military_vehicle_angled (slightly rotated military cars)
-> military_vehicle_light (light military cars; determined if (mass*engineForce*engineLoudness)/10^6 is less than or equal 425)
-> military_vehicle_light_angled (slightly rotated light military cars)
-> military_vehicle_heavy (heavy military cars; determined if (mass*engineForce*engineLoudness)/10^6 is more than 425)
-> military_trailer_heavy_angled (slightly rotated heavy military cars)

[h3] Making your own compatibility [/h3]
[b] For vehicle mods: [/b]
Just add your vehicle in VehicleZoneDistribution.military or VehicleZoneDistribution.army. If you are interested in where your vehicle will be distributed, read above about the zones.

[b] For map mods: [/b]
Will be a bit later.

[hr][/hr]

[url=https://dalink.to/pupsich_0w0][img]https://i.postimg.cc/bvP9RTRz/dalogo.png[/img][/url][/code]

publishedfiledetails.title: Yet Another PZ LIbrary
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]Umm... And what kind of library is this?[/h2]

YAPZLib is essentially a repository for all the main functions I used in my mods. The reason for creating it was that I often started using similar functions. So here it is.

The functions are primarily related to vehicles, their spawn zones (registering new ones, editing existing ones), loot, etc.

A couple of new features have also been added to VehicleZoneDistribution, namely, vehicle spawning at an angle and spawning without parts.

In general, if you need it, you can use this library for your mods. Here's a [url=https://github.com/pupsich0w0/YAPZLib/wiki]wiki[/url] for everyone who's interested.

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42-MP] Mod Load Order To Server Export Tool
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]This tool lets you easily export your mod's load order.[/h1]

[h2]How to use[/h2]

1. Activate the mod in the mod manager
2. In main menu click on the button "Export mods" located in the bottom right corner of the screen near the "Report a bug" button.
2a. If you can't see the button (it sometimes can happen on wider screens) you can alternatively press [CTRL + ~] or, when on Mac [CMD + ~]
3. A confirmation pop-up appears - it tells you where the generated file is on your machine, and it also copies the content of the file automatically into your clipboard
4. Navigate to the file and send it to the host or use [url=https://pastebin.com]pastebin[/url] to create a copy in the cloud and send the link do the host.
5. Profit!

[h1]Mod Load Order Exporter[/h1]
A simple tool to export your active mod load order to a text file and automatically copy it to your clipboard. Perfect for multiplayer—share your exact mod list with the host in seconds.

[h2]What it does[/h2]
Exports your enabled mods in load order
Saves a .txt file on disk and copies the same content to your clipboard
Shows a confirmation pop-up with the file location
Does not change your mod order—only reads and exports it

[img]https://i.imgur.com/bPKQYFi.png[/img]

[h2]How to use[/h2]
Enable the mod in the Mod Manager.
In the main menu, click the Export mods button (bottom-right corner, near Report a bug).
If you can’t see the button (it can sometimes happen on wider screens), use the shortcut:
Windows/Linux: CTRL + ~
macOS: CMD + ~
A confirmation pop-up appears: it shows where the generated file is saved, and it also copies the export to your clipboard.
Send the file to the host, or paste the content to https://pastebin.com and share the link.
[h2]Where the file is saved[/h2]
The export is saved in your Project Zomboid exports folder, with a name like ModLoadOrder_Export_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.txt.

[h2]Notes[/h2]
Works from the main menu—no need to load into a game.
Useful for troubleshooting mod mismatches and MP desync issues.

[h2]ver. 1.0.1[/h2]
[i][h2]Load order exported with unwavering focus
by [b]pimatstudio[/b][/h2][/i]

[url=https://ko-fi.com/pimatstudio]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/8bZ7Z5P.png[/img]

publishedfiledetails.title: Fluffy Hair [B41 & B42ish]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[b]This mod will add for almost all hats new adjusted hair models for the default hairs to keep their original shape when wearing something on the head. No more Ponytails or flat hairs!
You can still turn your hair into ponytail if you wish so.[/b]
(Also i improved the Ponytail(Braided) a bit to make it look more fluffy!)

Credits to "Chuckleberry Finn" to help me out with the LUA part to hide the new hair-styles in the character creation menu so you don't accidentally pick the wrong ones! [At the Old mod version of it in 41.50]
Check out his other mods:  [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/Chuckleberry_Finn/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600] Chuckleberry Finn's workshop[/url]


[h1] [b]I need you![/b][/h1]
Please give feedback if you found a bug or glitching [b]vanilla[/b] hair like so  :

[Gender] -  [Hairstyle] - [Hat]
[Additional Info]

Since there are alot of different Hair/Hat combinations i have not tested every single possible combination.
But i will write down every feedback for issues or improvements and will work through them until this mod is somewhat perfect!
(Please add Feedback in the discussion thread)


[h1] [b]FAQ:[/b][/h1]
[b]Is "Fluffy Hair" compatible with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2463184726] "Spongie's Hair Pack" [/url], [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2761200458] "Yaki's Hair Salon" [/url] and [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2456540487] "Harry's Hair" [/url] ?[/b]
I gave it a test and it seems work great together.
They also took care of making their hairs working properly with hats.
At Harry's Hair the alternative hat-hairs do show up in the hair menus for some reason.

 [b]I want to make a hat/hair compatible with your mod, how do i do?[/b]
Just Set the Hat-Category or the hair groups like that:
- Group01 If its a normal hat (CowboyHat, Tinfoil, Beany)
- Group02 If its a band (Bandana, Visor, Bandage)
- Group03 If its a hoodie
- Group04 If its over-ear (Winterhat, bicycle helmet)
- Group05 If its just default hair if you dont want any alternative hair
- Group06 If your hairs are fully covered (Shower caps, Surgeon caps)
- Group07 If its the default HatHair Style

[b]Will this mod work with a existing savegame?[/b]
Yes

 [b]Which game version works with this mod?[/b]
I have tested it with game version B41 and B42 and Hosted Multiplayer

 [b]Is this mod compatible with other Mods?[/b]
This Mod will not be compatible with mods which do:
- Change default hat items
- Change default hair styles
Mods which add completly new hairs should be compatible.

[b]Am i allowed to use this mod in a Server mod pack?[/b]
Yes

[url=https://ko-fi.com/scavengersmods][img]https://i.imgur.com/k8tvuaC.png[/img][/url]

Workshop ID: 2447729538
Mod ID: FH

publishedfiledetails.title: [42] Hot Brass - Ammo Craft
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Ammo craft[/h1]

[h2]Now you'll want to pick up those casings![/h2]

Welcome to Ammo Crafting for Hot Brass. There's not much to explain here.

Casings now have recipes that let you craft ammo again. You will find a reloading tool in different places.

Ammo crafting is a rather simple process: find a box of primers, gunpowder, and the projectile type for the round you want to craft.

Currently, it supports vanilla and VFE ammo types. More in the future.

This is still a work in progress. I'm slowly trying to get distribution right, and I may add boxes of empty casings so you can craft bullets from scratch.

Any feedback is appreciated!

[b]Requirements[/b]
[list]
[*] Hot Brass
[/list]

publishedfiledetails.title: Simple Silencers
publishedfiledetails.description:
Simple Silencers adds new weapon attachements to the game for vanilla firearms. They are:

Suppressor: This is a factory made suppressor, and can be attached to rifles, assault rifles and pistols. This retail suppressor can NOT be attached to revolvers or shotguns. Suppressors can be found in gunstores, police stations and a few other places.

Makeshift Suppresor: This has been hand-crafted, and can only be attached to pistols due to the poor quality. These can usually be found in crime-related containers. Additionally, this can be crafted by the player, provided they learn the recipe.

I made this mod because the few silencer mods made specifically for vanilla firearms are either out-of-date, buggy with red errors, or apply to every firearm under the sun. Revolvers do not benefit from a suppressor - they exhaust from the chanber, not the barrel D: Shotgun suppressors are bulky - there is no universal size for every gun D:

Finally, I wanted to add more balance between firearms. Revolvers are easy to reload, and shotguns are powerful from the onset, and do not need additional buffs from a suppressor mod.

[b]IMPORTANT[/b]

I have added a picture that gives the general idea of how far sound will carry for each suppressor. The line designates the approximate safe distance, while the positions of the character is the point where the sound can be heard by the target group.

Please note: This distance only applies to zombies with [b]NORMAL[/b] hearing as per the "survivalist" default. Please understand that "apocalypse" zombies have [b]RANDOMISED[/b] hearing, so different zeds will hear you from different distances!

Hopefully this will finally put rest to the misconception that this mod is not working as intended.

For B42:  Options are back! MSR700 is default now. Yeah, I kinda got annoyed that I couldn't use a suppressor on my M3, so I took another stab at updating the mod. So, we're back to attaching suppressors uniquely.

B42MP: Fixed to work for SP, haven't checked if it is working on MP. Please let me know.

[url=ko-fi.com/wyr3d] [img]https://i.ibb.co/pyYz3q0/kofii.png[/img][/url]

Workshop ID: 3309896124
Mod ID: SimpleSilencers

publishedfiledetails.title: Universal Simple Silencers
publishedfiledetails.description:
This mod allows the suppressors from Monkey Magic's [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3309896124]Simple Silencers[/url] to be used on any ranged weapon, both vanilla and modded.

There are no checks to remove combinations that make no sense, so if you want a silenced revolver or wish your modded bow was quieter you can do that.

[h3]Suppressor Selection Logic[/h3]
[list]
[*]Ranged weapon that fires more than one projectile? It's a shotgun.
[*]Ranged weapon that needs two hands? It's a rifle.
[*]Any other ranged Weapon? It's a pistol.
[/list]

[h3]FAQ[/h3]

[b]Do I need to install both this mod *and* Simple Silencers?[/b]

Yes.

[b]Putting a suppressor on <weapon> has the suppressor floating in the air, or at the wrong angle, or some other problem.[/b]

This happens when the muzzle attachment locations isn't placed perfectly... and Zomboid is *very* fussy about weapon model rotation/scaling so it's quite likely the gun works fine until some fool makes a mod that lets you slap a suppressor on it.

[b]Putting a suppressor on <weapon> makes no sense![/b]

So don't put a suppressor on it.

publishedfiledetails.title: [41/42] Hot Brass - Visible Casing Ejection Framework
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1][b]NEW MAJOR VERSION 1.2[/b][/h1]

[h1][b]Rework the mod into modules, for more compatibility. new ways to register ammuniton for casings.[/b][/h1]

[b]Works in MP for 41 and 42, casings are tight to server tick rate hence why the slow FPS of casings.[/b]

[h2] Hot Brass - Visible Casing Ejection Framework [/h2]

Is a passion project and my first official mod. I always love keeping an eye on small details, and that's why I started on a journey of making casings visible and now it has evolved into something more than I originally envisioned.

BIG thanks to the modding community from Zomboid and all the wonderful people in there.

[h2] Changes to Gameplay [/h2]

Racking now causes you to eject an unfired round, make sure you pick them up. Revolvers and manually-loaded shotguns behave the same as in vanilla for racking.

[h2] Native support for mods in version 42.15 onwards[/h2]

[list]
[*] Guns of 93 Beta
[*] [42] VFE and Addons
[*] Firearms and Firearms Beta
[*] Gaels Gunshop
[/list]

[h2] Native support for mods in version 41[/h2]

[list]
[*] VFE and Addons
[*] Guns of 93
[*] Firearms
[*] Firearms Revamp
[*] Britas Weapons
[/list]
[b]If you are hosting this mod in MP for version 41, make sure it loaded last! [/b]

[h2] Mod is still a Work in Progress! [/h2]
[b]Next step, ammo crafting![/b]

Some of the features regarding casings
[list]
[*] React to the world around, fall to different levels, react against walls, land on objects (tables, chairs, counters, etc...), open or close windows or doors
[*] Bounce around
[*] Play appropriate casing sounds to the tile they are landing on
[*] be cool looking :D
[/list]

[b]If you find a bug, please report it in the thread for it, not the comments.[/b]

[h3] Special recognition [/h3]

Thank you to everyone in the PZ Modding Discord and Special thanks to Vilespring for allowing me to use VFE ammo icons and Sven for the constant support and bughunting for version 41!

[h2] For Modders [/h2]

I have redone the structure of the mod, so if you wish to implement it or want to change the functionality of a function, you can patch it by calling the main object and the desired function.
Look over at some of the integrated params table for examples on custom fly patterns for casings.

If you wish to support my work!

publishedfiledetails.title: [42] Vanilla Firearms Expansion
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Unofficial Port of VFE and Addons[/h1]

[h2]Updated to version 42.16[/h2]

[h3]New update in .16 make weapon reloads be Enumerates, and with no current way to register new animsets custom animations caused players to do the raise hands emote. I have to update all weapons to use vanilla animations for now.

I have still not found a clean solution to MP issue for alternative operations (Folding Stock, Using Alt Weapon, Folding Bipod, etc) So please avoid mentioning that same issue in the comments I am aware.
[/h3]

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3644959783]
[b]VFEResprites[/b]
[/url]

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3686107766]
[b]Shazbot's Ammo Icon Retexture[/b]
[/url]

[h1]VFE Post 93 weapons Out![/h1]
Ported VFE93 weapons and added some new features missing from it. Ammo now has packs, cases, and crates. SD variants are rarer than non-SD, but suppressors have made it into the game. If you find one for the gun, it can be attached or removed like a bayonet.

[h2]VFE Stalker Edition Out now![/h2]
Ported version of Stalker included in the pack.

With approval from Vilespring, and after months of work, I have ported VFE, one of the greatest addons in the community, to Build 42 (Unofficially).
This version serves as a temporary port while Vilespring works on officially updating VFE for Build 42, taking full advantage of all the new systems with skills far beyond mine.
Consider this a tribute to the great VFE and its devs while official development continues.

[h2]About This Port[/h2]

I have taken the time to faithfully port all the core functionality from the original Build 41 version, while also incorporating some of the new systems introduced in Build 42.

[b]Changes[/b]
[b]VFE[/b]
[list]
[*] JungleMags (Coupled Mags) can be equipped with slings due to new attachment categories introduced in B42.
[*] Ammo cartons are introduced along with packs, cases, and crates as a cheaper alternative.
[/list]

[b]VFES[/b]
[list]
[*] New caliber types for rifles and the VSS: 7.62x54mm and 9x39mm can be found alongside the existing 5.45mm.
[*] Introduces the same ammo packing logic for all new VFES calibers; they can now be condensed into packs, cases, crates, or cartons.
[/list]

[b]VFE93[/b]
[list]
[*] Added suppressors as an item that can be found, allowing you to alternate between the SD and non-SD variant of certain guns.
[*] Added ammo packing for new ammunition types as well.
[/list]

[h2]Additional Integration[/h2]

RAF, Hot Brass, and Hot Brass Ammo Craft are marked as dependencies [b]BUT THEY ARE NOT[/b]. They are all optional plugins to enhance the experience in the game.

[h2]Development Intent[/h2]

The mod is now complete compared to its original version in Build 41. Development will now cease, and no new features will be added.
If you encounter any bugs, please report them in a proper discussion thread, not in the comments.
And please, do not contact Vilespring regarding issues with this unofficial port.

[h2]Availability[/h2]

This mod will remain available until the official VFE update is released. Once that happens, it will be set to unlisted.

[b]Enjoy![/b]


If you wish to support my work!

publishedfiledetails.title: [42] RAF - Real Automatic Firerate
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]RAF - Real Automatic Firerate[/h1]

[b]This mod simulates automatic firerate as close as Zomboid permits to real-life weapons' Rounds Per Minute.[/b]

[h2][b]New Burst fire mode is out! I have patched the Assault Rifle to use the new mode.[/b][/h2]

As I kept experimenting and learning with the game, I was surprised to see automatic fire being kind of slow, plus it was affected by the game's refresh rate if it was high enough. I also realized that many of the current approaches in the Workshop required an animset with not much room for customization.

Having made Hot Brass and enjoying putting this little attention to detail into the game, I got distracted while finishing the Ammo Crafting module for Hot Brass and came up with this.

[b]I present to you RAF![/b]

What does the mod do?

I use a script in the Attack Hook event of the game, and with a timer and some functions, I limit the automatic fire based on the RPM of that weapon.

As a modder, do you want to implement this?

All you have to do is add a tag to your weapon script with the desired RPM.

Right now, RPMs covered by the mod range from 300 to 1200 with 50-step increases each, which I feel is plenty enough to play around with.

If you also want to use the Burst mode from the mod, all you have to do is enable Burst as a fire mode for your weapon. Optionally, you can add a tag to specify how many bullets to shoot in each burst volley. Currently covered: 2 to 4 rounds.

I can add more RPM options or rounds per burst if modders request it.

This mod is now a hard dependency for my port of VFE.

Hope you enjoy it as much as I enjoyed making this! :D

[h3]Supported Mods[/h3]
[list]
[*] VFE and Addons [42]
[*] Hyzo Firearms
[*] Guns of 93
[*] **Coming Soon** Gaels Gunshop
[/list]

[h3]Incompatibilities[/h3]

- Any mod that overwrites the attack hook instead of monkey-patching it.

If you wish to support my work!

publishedfiledetails.title: Persistent Player Corpses
publishedfiledetails.description:
Prevents player corpses from being removed by corpse cleanup (e.g. 168 hour removal). Designed for dedicated multiplayer servers so player bodies and their loot remain permanently.

IMPORTANT VEHICLE NOTE:
When dragging a corpse into a vehicle and later removing it, you must manually GRAB the corpse from the vehicle inventory first.
If you use the quick "drop to ground" action, the game treats the body as newly created and the zombie removal timer will start again,
which can cause the corpse to disappear despite this mod.

publishedfiledetails.title: Better Auto Mechanics [B42.17+ MP+SP]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Better Auto Mechanics[/h1]
[b]Train mechanics with a single click. Smarter, more intuitive, and lightweight. In Multiplayer and Singleplayer.[/b]

Rewritten from the ground up, [b]Better Auto Mechanics[/b] is a comprehensive quality-of-life upgrade for the mechanic skill. Inspired by the original mod "[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3387539308]AutoMechanics[/url]", it improves on several key features while still allowing you to train mechanics on vehicles with a single button. And it actually works in multiplayer!

Compatible with all B42 versions! B41 is not supported, use [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2588598892]this[/url] instead.

[hr][/hr]
[h2]🔧 Key Features[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]NEW! Batch Uninstall:[/b] Quickly strip vehicles for spare parts! Use the new context menu to batch-uninstall specific categories like all doors, all tires, or completely strip the entire car bare with a single click.
[*] [b]Smart XP Tracking:[/b] The mod knows exactly when you can't gain XP from a specific car anymore. It will alert you if a vehicle isn't worth training on, so you never waste time.
[*] [b]Resume Interrupted Training:[/b] If zombies interrupt you (or you cancel an action), you can pick up exactly where you left off. No more starting from scratch and wasting part durability!
[*] [b]Efficiency First:[/b] The mod will never perform an action if it won't yield XP.
[*] [b]Clearer Tooltips:[/b] Know exactly what tools you need and how your skill level, known recipes, and other factors impact the training before you start.
[*] [b]Fast-Forward Supported:[/b] Fully supports speeding up the game in SP, allowing you to breeze through your training sessions. Nearby zombies will still reset the game speed to normal.
[*] [b]Translated into:[/b] English, German, Russian, French, simple + trad. Chinese, Japanese, Spanish, Brazilian Portuguese, and Polish.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]🚗 How to Use[/h2]
[olist]
[*] Open the mechanics window on any vehicle.
[*] Right-click any part.
[*] Select [b]"Train Mechanics"[/b].
[*] Watch the levels go up!
[/olist]
[quote][b]Note:[/b] Pathfinding can sometimes impact the training session. Please ensure your character has some walking space around the car.[/quote]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]💡 Tips[/h2]
[list]
[*] You can only gain XP from a specific vehicle once per day.
[*] Right-click directly on a vehicle in the world to access the "Train Mechanics" button faster.
[*] The "Train Mechanics" button is automatically hidden once you reach Mechanics Level 10.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]⚙️ Mod Settings[/h2]
You can customize the [b]"Minimum Vehicle Part Success Chance"[/b] in the options. This value determines the safety threshold for installing/uninstalling parts; any part with a success chance below this value will be skipped during training.
[list]
[*] [b]0 (Risky):[/b] All parts will be worked on. Be careful—parts [i]will[/i] break or lose condition at lower skill levels.
[*] [b]30 (Default):[/b] A good balance. Parts with a very low success chance are skipped to prevent excessive damage, while still allowing for decent XP gain.
[*] [b]100 (Safe Mode):[/b] Only parts that are guaranteed to succeed will be worked on. This is completely safe at all skill levels, though training may be slower as fewer parts are eligible.
[/list]

[b]Multiplayer / Server Admins:[/b]
Server owners can enforce this setting via Sandbox Options.
[list]
[*] [b]-1 (Default):[/b] The setting is [b]not enforced[/b]. Players are free to use their own local Mod Option setting.
[*] [b]0 to 100:[/b] The setting is [b]enforced server-wide[/b]. Any value greater than -1 will override individual player choices. The value set by the server will be visible in the button tooltip.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]✅ Compatibility & Save Info[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]Safe to add and remove mid-save.[/b]
[*] [b]Compatible[/b] with all [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/KI5/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]KI5's Vehicles[/url], [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3564950449]Project Summer Car[/url], [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2757712197]Vehicle Repair Overhaul[/url], and most other vehicle mods.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]FEEDBACK & SUPPORT[/h2]
If you find this mod useful:
[list]
[*] ⭐ Rate on Steam Workshop
[*] 💬 Leave comments or [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/discussions/3635856965]Feedback[/url] 
[*] 🐛 Post bug reports in [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/discussions/3635856965]Discussions[/url] or on [url=https://github.com/Darkblader24/pz-better-auto-mechanics/issues]Github[/url]
[*] 🚀 Contribute to this mod on [url=https://github.com/Darkblader24/pz-better-auto-mechanics]Github[/url]. Translations are very welcome!
[*] ❤️ Buy me a [url=https://ko-fi.com/hotox]Ko-fi[/url]
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

Fixes bugged VHS tapes in multiplayer by safely forcing them out of the player’s inventory when they get stuck after being watched on a TV .
The mod adds a dedicated “[FIX] Drop VHS” context-menu option that only appears for VHS tapes in the player’s personal inventory, not in external containers, to avoid duplication or desync issues .
When used, it cleans the tape’s internal modData/state, unequips it from both hands and attachments, and then drops it on the ground so it behaves like a normal world item again .

publishedfiledetails.title: Burd's Survival Journals (B41/42)
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Burd's Survival Journals[/h1]
Record your survival skills in personal journals and learn from the fallen. Find worn journals in bookcases and desks, loot bloody journals from zombie corpses, risk cursed journals for powerful rewards, or unwrap seasonal Yuletide gifts packed with supplies.

Your knowledge doesn't have to die with you. Pass your journals to new characters, trade them with friends, or discover what survivors left behind before they turned.

Leave a like or favorite if you're enjoying the mod, more features & polish to come down the line. Any feedback or bugs? Drop them in the comments or start a discussion!

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Features[/h1]

[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/TheBurd/PZ-BurdSurvivalJournals/refs/heads/master/docs/assets/BSJ-PlayerJournalDetails.png[/img]
[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/TheBurd/PZ-BurdSurvivalJournals/refs/heads/master/docs/assets/BSJ-WornJournalDetails.png[/img]
[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/TheBurd/PZ-BurdSurvivalJournals/refs/heads/master/docs/assets/BSJ-BloodyJournalDetails.png[/img]
[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/TheBurd/PZ-BurdSurvivalJournals/refs/heads/master/docs/assets/BSJ-CursedJournalDetails.png[/img]
[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/TheBurd/PZ-BurdSurvivalJournals/refs/heads/master/docs/assets/BSJ-ForgetTabDetails.png[/img]

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Sandbox Options — Fully Configurable[/h1]

[b]Journals - Player:[/b] Enable/disable, XP recovery modes, baseline restrictions, pen/eraser requirements, learning times, capacity limits

[b]Journals - Loot:[/b] Spawn chances, XP ranges, trait/recipe/forget chances, limited-claim dissolution

[b]Journals - Loot (Fun):[/b] Cursed journal settings (curse types, disguise mode), Yuletide season timing and gift tiers, Krampus integration

[b]Journals - Advanced:[/b] Skill book multipliers, reading skill speed bonuses, light requirements, batch processing, multiplayer permissions, animated visuals toggle

[b]Journals - Other Mods:[/b] Compatibility toggles for Traits Purchase System, Adaptive Traits, Evolving Traits World, VHS skills

Nearly everything is adjustable. Run it hardcore with strict limits, or generous with plentiful rare drops. Your server, your rules.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]FAQ[/h1]

[b]Q: Does this work in the latest multiplayer build?[/b]
A: Yes. Full server-authoritative multiplayer support with claim tracking, sharing permissions, and admin tools.

Working Builds Checklist:
⚠️ B41 SP/MP - Currently only SP compatible. Recently released, may not be as stable as B42 branches
✅ B42 SP/MP (Up to 42.14) - Fully working
✅ B42.15+ SP/MP - Fully working

[b]Q: Will this break my existing save?[/b]
A: No. Safe to add to current saves. "Only Record Earned Progress" takes effect on your next character respawn if you already have an established run. Removing the mod during save could destroy active player or loot journals.

[b]Q: Can I exploit this with profession skill farming?[/b]
A: Not easily. Baseline restriction (enabled by default) prevents recording starting profession XP. You'd have to earn it the hard way first.

[b]Q: What traits can bloody/cursed/Yuletide journals have?[/b]
A: Positive traits like Keen Hearing, Cat's Eyes, Organized, Brave, Fast Learner, and more. Negative traits are disabled by default but configurable. Cursed/Yuletide have higher drop chances for traits and forget slots.

[b]Q: Is this compatible with other mods?[/b]
A: Generally yes. Works with most skill/trait mods that use standard B41/B42 registration. Compatibility toggles included for major trait mods. Safe function capture prevents crashes from conflicting mods.

[b]Q: How do cursed journals work?[/b]
A: 0.08% zombie drop chance to replace a normal bloody journal spawn. Opening one applies a curse (10 possible effects), then transforms into a premium reward journal with 2-5 skills at 75-300 XP each. Can disguise as bloody journals if sandbox setting enabled - no warning prompt, immediate curse.

[b]Q: When do Yuletide journals spawn?[/b]
A: During December by default, but configurable for in-game December, real-world December, or always-on. Replace worn (default 8%) and bloody (default 4%) spawns when active.

[b]Q: Can I use player journals as a memory system for my whole server?[/b]
A: Yes. Record skills/traits/recipes, drop the journal in a shared base, let others claim. Per-character tracking means everyone gets their own copy of the progress.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Translations[/h1]
[list]

[/list]

publishedfiledetails.title: '70 Plymouth Barracuda - Rust Edition
publishedfiledetails.description:
Overhaul of textures for all variants of '70 Plymouth Barracuda. It doesn't add a new car, it changes the looks of all the existing ones of this type.

Ever wondered what a car could look like after several years of erosion in project zomboid?
Probably like this.

Rust added and details highlighted. Includes bodywork, interior and parts as seperate objects. Armor not touched by corrosion, since it's supposed to be created by player with new materials. Also didn't touch the wheels to make the mod lighter and potentially less buggy.

Intended to be used with mods like Project Seasons, Barricaded World, 10 Years Later, etc. Set starting erosion to maximum for better immersion.

Made for personal use, but you can use it, comment on it and tell me what other vehicles i should prioritize on making alternative rusty versions for.

Thanks KI5 for best vehicle mods!
I wouldn't be still playing zomboid if it was not for mods like his.

Part of collection of rust-themed vehicles updates:
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3658512002

Reshade used when taking screenshots:
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3655671723

Workshop ID: 3662731107
Mod ID: 70barracudaRusty
Mod ID: 70barracudaRusty

publishedfiledetails.title: Realistic Dashboard & Gauges [MP] [Beta]
publishedfiledetails.description:
This beta version mod adds a realistic vehicle dashboard for Project Zomboid B41/B42/MP. [b] SAFE FOR EXISTING SAVES [/b]

Workshop ID: 3635591071
Mod ID: RealisticDash

[h1]Patch note[/h1]
[list]
[*]1/7/2026: Compatibility fix for Autostar Motor Club mod. Small artistic changes (see change note).
[*]1/2/2026: Added option to change stain and crack opacity (only for B42).
[*]1/1/2026: Added HD texture for high resolution screens (only for B42). [b] For users of 4k / 2160p screens, please go to OPTIONS -> MODS to select the 2x size texture. [/b]
[/list]

[h1] Tip for smoother animation[/h1]
In Options → Display, increase UI Render FPS (higher = smoother UI). Recommended value is 60 fps.

[h1]Features and highlights[/h1]
[list]
[*]Full radio, A/C, locks, and window control on dashboard.

[*]Dashboard for driver AND front row passenger.

[*]Physics-based gauge needle motion.

[*]Gauge backlit illumination.

[*]Dynamic stain and crack effect. (Affected by driver seat and windshield condition)

[*]Revamped warning lights. (with startup bulb-check)

[*]Dynamic cranking voltage sag effect. (Affected by battery remaining charge)

[*]Two different radio ui for ValueTech/Premium Technologies radio.

[/list]
[h1]Version/ MP compatability[/h1]
Tested on Project Zomboid B41 (Singleplayer), B42 (Singleplayer), and B42 (Multiplayer). This mod is safe for existing saves.
This is a beta version — bugs and issues may occur.

[h1]Mod compatability[/h1]
This mod respects vanilla game logic and does not currently rely on any custom data saving.
It should be compatible with most non-dashboard mods that also follow vanilla behavior. Mods tested being compatible (but not limited to):
[list]
[*] TV and Radio Reinvented

[*] True Music

[*] Project Summer Cars (Please load Project Summer Cars before this mod. However its thermometer and warning lights may be covered. This can be patched in future update)

[*] KI5 car mods

[/list]

[h1]How to use[/h1]
This mod does not require explicit setup. It should replace vanilla dashboard UI automatically when mod is installed. [b] For B42 users of 4k / 2160p screens, please go to OPTIONS -> MODS to select the 2x size texture. [/b]
For radio and warning lights, please see the flyers:
[img]https://images.steamusercontent.com/ugc/17835549219215167773/FE3DA36A458F242C68E47DB2B0279D14F7B38B57/?imw=637&imh=358&ima=fit&impolicy=Letterbox&imcolor=%23000000&letterbox=true[/img]

[img]https://images.steamusercontent.com/ugc/16101101754868648390/B7EE2D261590F544EE73CAA77C8A47250EF3F34B/?imw=637&imh=358&ima=fit&impolicy=Letterbox&imcolor=%23000000&letterbox=true[/img]

[h1]Planned updates[/h1]
[list]
[*] Emergency light control dashboard for emergency vehicles

[*] Different dashboard design for different vehicles

[*] Ham radio interface

[/list]

[h1]Bug report[/h1]
If you encounter a bug, please include your build number, game mode, and name of other mods if there is a compatibility issue.

[h1]Author’s Note[/h1]

Hi everyone! I’m the creator of this mod. I’m originally from Nanjing, China, and I’m currently studying in the United States. Cars have always been my biggest passion, especially the small design and mechanical details. Beyond dashboard and gauges, I’m also interested in hybrid powertrain architectures and how real-world engineering choices shape driving experiences.

This is my first game mod, and it’s still in beta, so bugs and compatibility issues are to be expected. I’ll continue to actively maintain it and push updates as issues come up, and I truly appreciate any feedback you’d like to share.

Thank you for downloading this mod, giving it a try, or even just taking the time to read this note. I hope it adds a bit more immersion and enjoyment to your apocalyptic driving experience!

publishedfiledetails.title: Ford Transcontinental
publishedfiledetails.description:
[img]https://i.ibb.co/FLZ5PWSz/ford1.png[/img]
[quote]
This mod adds the legendary Ford Transcontinental truck series to the world of Project Zomboid.

Designed as a true long-haul survival vehicle, it features a fully functional living cabin, immersive details, and all the utilities you need to survive the post-apocalyptic roads — all built directly into the cabin.
[strike]
This mod is still a work in progress. Some features may be unfinished or subject to change.
If you encounter any bugs or issues, please report them in the discussion section.[/strike]
[/quote]

[img]https://i.ibb.co/DPRBDv69/ford2.png[/img]
[quote]
[h3]Chassis Types[/h3]
[b]• 4x2[/b] – Short chassis  [/b]
[b]• 6x2[/b] – Long chassis  [/b]
[b]• Vehicle parts are now generated automatically on spawn. [i](etc: light cover, lightbars, plastic cover)[/i][/b]

[h3]Animations[/h3]
[b]• All doors and the trunk are fully animated.[/b]

[h3]Variants[/h3]
[b]• Civilian[/b] (color variations)
[b]• Army[/b] (desert tan, forest green)
[b]• Fire Department[/b]
[b]• Fossoil / Gas 2 Go[/b]

[h3]Cabin Equipment[/h3]
[b]• Working microwave[/b] (4x2)  (Right Seat.)
[b]• Stove and fridge[/b] (6x2)  (Right Seat.)
[b]• Interior storage and utility items[/b]

[h3]Trailer Support[/h3]
[b]• Fully compatible with standard trailers and custom fuel tankers and trailer.[/b]  [i](etc Tsar's lib mods)[/i]
[b]• Trailer parts are now generated automatically on spawn. [i](etc: plastic cover, bumper, some details)[/i][/b]

[h3]Loots[/h3]
[b]These trucks do not spawn empty.[/b]
[b]Gloveboxes, storage cabinets, and toolboxes spawn stocked with useful loot such as tools, spare parts, cigarettes, and snacks.[/b]
[/quote]

[quote]
[h3]Trailers[/h3]
This mod includes heavy-duty trailers designed specifically for the Ford Transcontinental.
Like the trucks themselves, all trailers come with proper spawn zones and are treated as vehicles.

[h3]Trailer Variants (Cargo & Fuel)[/h3]
[b]• Standard[/b] (no brand, color variations)
[b]• Fossoil / Gas 2 Go[/b]
[b]• Spiffo’s[/b]

[i](Military and water tanker variants are planned for future updates.)[/i]

[h3]Fuel Tankers[/h3]
[b]Fuel tanker trailers can be filled at gas stations and used as a mobile gas station.[/b]
[b]Fuel can be pumped directly from the tanker into nearby vehicles.[/b]

[h3]Fuel Capacities[/h3]
[b]• Big Fuel Tanker:[/b] 1200 units
[b]• Small Fuel Tanker:[/b] 550 units

[h3]Cargo Trailers[/h3]
[b]• Cargo Trailer[/b]

[h3]Capacity[/h3]
[b]Standard cargo capacity:[/b] 1000
[i](Due to the Build 42 container capacity bug, the effective maximum capacity is currently limited to 100.)[/i]
[/quote]

[quote]
[h3]Compatibility & Notes[/h3]
[b]• Multiplayer compatible[/b]
[b]• Save-game compatible[/b] (vehicles will spawn in unexplored areas)
[b]• Designed for Build 42+[/b]
[b]• Frequent updates and improvements planned[/b]

[b]If you like my work and want to support the development of this mod (and maybe buy me a warm coffee), you can do so via Patreon. This is the best way to support me![/b]
[url=https://www.patreon.com/posts/tip-148580806]Click here to Support Me / Buy me a Coffee (Patreon)[/url]

[quote]
[h3]Vehicle IDs[/h3]

[b]FordTranscontinental4x2 — FordTranscontinental6x2[/b]
[b]FordTranscontinental4x2_army — FordTranscontinental6x2_army[/b]
[b]FordTranscontinental4x2_fossoil — FordTranscontinental6x2_fossoil[/b]
[b]FordTranscontinental4x2_fire — FordTranscontinental6x2_fire[/b]

[b]FordTranscontinentalTrailer[/b]
[b]FordTranscontinentalTrailer_biggastank[/b]
[b]FordTranscontinentalTrailer_smallgastank[/b]

[b]Workshop ID: 3649632887
Mod ID: FordTranscontinental
Mod ID: FordTranscontinentalVanillaTires[/b]
[/quote]

publishedfiledetails.title: '95 Subaru Impreza WRX STI
publishedfiledetails.description:
Time for another classic import, with right hand drive for fans of the original WRX and the left hand converted one, pick your Impreza and go for a fun drive! It was made for rally, everything was made to last and to perform well in bad conditions, so what can a little zombie apocalypse do to it really? It comes with some unique features, rims that stand out and many more little things, so have fun finding and enjoying one.

[b]Main features:[/b]
[list]
[*]3 Front bumpers and a rear bumper with 2 tiered protection
[*]Fully animated hood, trunk, doors and windows
[*]Visible interior and character (with improved exit/enter/aim animations)
[*]All parts have 3d objects you can remove and place in the world
[*]Extra parts to craft and find in the world to make the vehicle truly yours (bumpers, bullbars, window armor, roofracks etc)
[*]Multiple texture variations
[*]Armor protection for windows, doors, engine, headlights etc
[*]4 seats
[/list]
[b][list][*]You can use it in your current save game, just enable the mod in the load menu
[*]It can tow and be towed
[*]Not recolorable
[/list][/b]

If you can't wait to find this mod in your game and want to test it right away, use [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2634426926]bikinitools[/url] to spawn it, hide the items that your character holds while in vehicle, or for all other good stuff that mod has, go get it!

[b]If you like my mods, please consider supporting me with a coffee[/b],
so I don't fall asleep while making new mods and supporting old ones :)

[url=https://ko-fi.com/ki5mods][img]https://i.imgur.com/2uTskWl.png[/img][/url]

Check out my vehicle mods:

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2490220997][img]https://i.imgur.com/1C4iQ6U.png[/img][/url]

[b]Credits:[/b]

[*]This mod is commission work by request of fredders
[*]Thanks to The Indie Stone for making this awesome game and all this possible.
[*]Special thanks to bikinihorst for the huge help with code, optimization, and taking care of the multiplayer and part code.[/list]

This mod can only be added to and extended with the express permission from the original creator. If no permission is received you may not alter the mod, and it must be treated as a mod that is 'On Lockdown'. You are not allowed to repack this mod under any circumstances, ever. [/quote]

publishedfiledetails.title: Here Goes the Sun [B42MP]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]AREN'T YOU ABSOLUTELY, COMPLETELY TIRED OF LIVING THE SAME DAMN DAY OVER AND OVER? I KNOW I AM.[/h1]

If your survivor lived for years, they spent three-quarters of their entire life watching the exact same sunset, again and again and again (except during storms).
It's time to put an end to that.

[hr][/hr]

[h2]Let me introduce my first mod: HERE GOES THE SUN[/h2]

[h3]Sunrises and sunsets redesigned with seasonal and weather-based transitions[/h3]
Spring and summer feel brighter and more alive; autumn comes with golden, brown and muted palettes; winter brings icy, grey or deep-blue tones. Golden hour? All yours.

[h3]Storms reshape the world into something bleak and suffocating[/h3]
Muted colors and dim daylight transform rain into a claustrophobic, cinematic experience that slowly wears the player down.

[h3]Fully dynamic and deterministic color system[/h3]
Every day the game picks a new variation based on season and probability, never random noise.

[h3]Compatible with other weather mods[/h3]
I aimed to avoid collisions with climate mods. Preferably load it last, just in case.

[h3]Made to capture beautiful screenshots[/h3]
Designed from the very beginning to create photographic, atmospheric moments in every season.

[h3]Includes SportXAI's god rays[/h3]
To keep compatibility high and revive his BRILLIANT idea, they are included.

[h3]Fully safe for old and new saves[/h3]
No new world required. Just enable and enjoy.


I'm open to all feedback and ideas. My coding skills are somewhat limited, but if inspiration strikes, I'm absolutely willing to experiment.

[hr][/hr]

[b]Future ideas:[/b]
[list]
[*]More morning variations (currently one per season)
[*]Chromatic moods for nighttime
[*]Rare lunar and solar eclipses
[*]Better blending between god rays and the day's palette
[*]And probably even more daily color variations, because I can't help myself
[/list]
[hr][/hr]

[h2]You want more? Try my other mod![/h2]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3645980077] [img]https://i.imgur.com/89YVdK9.gif[/img] [/url]

Workshop ID: 3618557184
Mod ID: HereGoesTheSun

publishedfiledetails.title: Darker Fog [B41/B42]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]Build Support: Build 41.78.16 and Build 42 up to 42.15.2[/h2]
[b]Darker Fog[/b] lets you make fog darker — and optionally add a subtle color tint — so fog feels more atmospheric and less washed out.

It works on both [b]Build 41[/b] and [b]Build 42[/b] through separate internal versions, and only changes the [b]visual appearance[/b] of fog while it exists.  
It does [b]not[/b] create fog, extend fog duration, shorten fog events, or change gameplay balance.

[h2]Features[/h2]
[list]
[*] Darken fog with a [b]brightness multiplier[/b]
[*] Optional [b]color tint[/b] for a moodier atmosphere
[*] [b]Build 42:[/b] brightness slider + color picker through Mod Options
[*] [b]Build 41:[/b] brightness + color presets through the external [b]Mod Options[/b] mod
[*] Optional [b]Sandbox Options[/b] to lock settings on multiplayer servers
[*] Visual-only mod: safe to add or remove, with no gameplay or balance changes
[/list]

[h2]How to configure[/h2]
[b]Mod Options (recommended for single player)[/b]
[list]
[*] ESC → [b]Options[/b] → [b]Mods[/b] → [b]Darker Fog[/b]
[*] Changes apply after pressing [b]Apply[/b] / [b]Accept[/b]
[/list]

[b]Sandbox Options (useful for MP server control)[/b]
[list]
[*] Set [b]Use Mod Options[/b] = [b]Off[/b] to force Sandbox values
[*] Configure [b]Fog brightness multiplier[/b] and [b]Fog color preset[/b]
[/list]

[b]Build 41 note:[/b] the external [b]Mod Options[/b] Workshop mod is only needed if you want to change Darker Fog from the in-game Options menu.  
Without it, the mod still works with its default values or with Sandbox Options.

[h2]What this mod changes[/h2]
[list]
[*] Makes fog darker
[*] Optionally tints fog with a selected color
[/list]

[b]What it does not change:[/b]
[list]
[*] Fog spawn frequency
[*] Fog duration
[*] Weather timing
[*] Any gameplay mechanics
[/list]

[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]Build 41[/b] and [b]Build 42[/b] supported through separate internal branches
[*] Confirmed compatible with recent [b]Build 42.15.2[/b]
[*] Client-side visuals only
[*] Safe to add to existing saves
[*] Safe to remove
[*] No known incompatibilities unless another mod overrides the same climate / fog visuals
[/list]

[h2]Languages[/h2]
Sandbox Options and Mod Options are available in:
[b]EN, ES, FR, DE, PT, PTBR, RU, TR, PL, UA, CN, CH, IT, KO, TH, JP[/b]

[h2]More mods & feedback[/h2]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199133259679/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]You can find my other Project Zomboid mods here[/url]
[img]https://images.steamusercontent.com/ugc/819002974403344349/DD18664314A26EA83C62E9B0127FFCB35092B53A/[/img]

If you find [b]Darker Fog[/b] useful, please consider leaving a [b]thumbs up[/b] and adding it to your [b]favorites[/b] — it helps other players discover it.
Feedback and suggestions are always welcome in the comments.

Workshop ID: 3613351176
Mod ID: DarkerFog

publishedfiledetails.title: Cold Winters
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h3][b]Compatible with both B41 and B42[/b][/h3]
[h3][b]Works in multiplayer for any version[/b][/h3]
[h3]This mod only affects the climate during winter - [b]other seasons won`t be changed[/b].[/h3]

[h2]Introduction:[/h2]
Vanilla Zomboid climate of Kentucky is set up in such a way that winters are quite warm and mild. For instance, snow barely stays for more than a month between December and February, and it melts pretty often. Additionally, you may only encounter quite low temperatures a couple of times throughout the whole winter. Even if you change the temperature preset in Sandbox to "Cold" the temperatures will drop to -15 degrees for a few weeks in January. Actually, these temperatures are historically accurate if you check KY weather history in 90`s, but some players may find it not challenging enough.

[h2]What does this mod do:[/h2]
The "Cold Winters" makes winter tougher: [b]temperatures can drop to -30 degrees Celsius[/b], and you'll find yourself facing real cold by lowering winter temperatures, which is more challenging for your survival. Additionally, [b]blizzards will occur more often (can be disabled in Sandbox settings)[/b], so be ready to cope with extreme weather. Moreover, [b]there is a little chance in winter that your generator will failure due to cold and you have to restart it[/b]! You can disable it on world creation in sandbox settings.
Winter will turn into a season that requires serious preparation. It will also take some time in the beginning of spring to make plus temperature back. And for a better experience, I`d recommend you set the temperature preset to "Normal" if you play in sandbox. This will make the beginning of autumn/end of spring less cold.

[h3][b]FAQ:[/b][/h3]
[h3]Is this mod compatible with "Surviving Through Seasons" or "Hypothermia Kills"?[/h3]
      - Yes.
[h3]Can I add this mod at mid-game?[/h3]
      - Yes, you can add it whenever you want.
[h3]Is this mod compatible with "Realistic Temperature Mod"?[/h3]
      - Yes.
[h3]How many times do blizzards start?[/h3]
      - Approximately 3 to 6 times per month. Each day has 12.7% chance to trigger new blizzard, which means you`ll face it a few times per month.
[h3]Is this mod compatible with "Cryogenic Winter"?[/h3]
      - No.
[h3]Is this mod compatible with "Weather Manager"?[/h3]
      - No.


[h3]Note: this mod is NOT compatible with other weather changing mods. I do not recommend you to use it with "Winter is coming" challenge because it may not work properly.[/h3]

Keep in mind that [b]you are not allowed to repack this mod without permission[/b].

I hope you enjoy this mod!
Please rate it if you liked it. I really appreciate that.

Workshop ID: 3577100076
Mod ID: coldwinters
Mod ID: coldwinters

publishedfiledetails.title: ExtraNoise's Newburbs Tiles
publishedfiledetails.description:
Standardized suburban development exploded in popularity in the 1980s and 1990s and continues to this day. The kind of communities where all the houses look a little too similar, where the sprawl stretches for miles in all directions. This is a collection of tiles for Project Zomboid that add those kinds of style walls, roofs, doors, windows, and other details that you would typically see these kind of communities.

[h1]Regular players[/h1]
You only need to subscribe to this mod if you've downloaded a map that makes use of the tiles in this pack.

[h1]Map makers[/h1]

publishedfiledetails.title: '84 Oldsmobile 98 Regency

Time to finish the GM C-body lineup strong with the Olds 98 sedan and coupe. Just like the rest of the C-body machines, it is big and has a smooth ride, it has the iconic styling of the 80s land yachts and it is great for cruising in the end times. It comes with a set of C-body parts and some unique ones too and has support for B-body armor, both types and most of the tires too, so as usual have fun mixing and matching stuff.

[b]Main features:[/b]

[*]Armor protection for windows, doors, engine, headlights etc

[/list]

publishedfiledetails.title: Better Generator Info
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Better Generator Info[/h1]

Tired of guessing how long your generator fuel will last or the power range?
[b]Better Generator Info[/b] enhances the in-game generator window with clearer, more useful details.

[hr][/hr]
[h2]Features:[/h2]
[list]
[*] Shows [b]exact days and hours remaining[/b] before your generator runs out of fuel.
[*] Option to display remaining time in [b]real-world hours[/b] (based on sandbox day length).
[*] Keeps the vanilla look & feel - seamlessly extends the [i]Generator Info Window[/i].
[*] Accurate calculations for Build [b]42.12+[/b] (uses the new [i]litres per hour[/i] values).
[*] Fuel Gauge that provides a visual representation of fuel - Can be toggled ON via Mod Options (OFF by default)
[*] Power Range Overlay - Display selected generator power range outline (Green when on, Red when off and (when enabled) a Unified outline of all powered areas (Powder blue).
[*] [b]NEW:[/b] Held Generator - Power Range Overlay
[*] Overlays are shown for every floor and indicate if generators are powering that floor.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]Mod Support and Twitch[/h2]
Any questions you can find me in any of the places below:
[list]
[*][url=https://twitch.tv/eurymachus]Twitch[/url]
[*][url=https://discord.gg/eurymachus]Discord[/url]
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[list]
[*]Works with [b]Build 42.12 and 42.13[/b]
[*]Should not conflict with other mods unless they also override the generator info window.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[list]
[*]Original mod concept by [b]Bananaut[/b] and [b]Undefined[/b].
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]Important Notes:[/h2]
[list]
[*]Requires no new save; can be added or removed at any time.
[*]To work on [b]Dedicated Servers[/b] (B42.13+) set [i]DoLuaChecksum=false[/i] in your server settings.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

Workshop ID: 3576056135
Mod ID: BetterGeneratorInfo

publishedfiledetails.title: U.S. M998 Humvee by Papa_Chad
publishedfiledetails.description:
[img]https://i.imgur.com/NrstAKw.gif[/img]

This mod adds M998 HMMWV (Humvee) with Animated Doors and a fully functioning Turret to Project Zomboid. It is necessary to have the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=2705406713]Military Tool Kit[/url]installed.


*Only for B42 SP and MP
*Compatible with old saves
*Compatible with KI5 vehicles

[h1]Resources[/h1]
[list]
[*] [b]Animated Doors
[*] [b]Animated Addons
[*] [b]Turret[/b]
    The turrets have heavy coding that adds a completely new ballistics system that is independent of the systems in the Standard Game, performing calculations for each projectile independently, calculating its energy, ability to cause damage and transfix bodies, vehicles and walls.
[*] [b]Doors, hoods, trunk lids and more.[/b]
They have their own colors, so you can assemble your Humvee with the color variation of the parts you want.
[/list]

[h1]How to use the Machine Gun[/h1]
[list]
[*]	The machine gun must be installed in the vehicle
[*] You must sit in the Gunner's seat
[*] You must place the [b]12.7x99mm (.50 BMG) Ammo Box[/b] already loaded with [b]12.7x99mm (.50 BMG) Round[/b] in the gun compartment.
[*] Press the [b]Right Mouse Button to aim[/b] and the [b]Left Mouse Button to fire[/b].
[*]	[b] ATTENTION[/b], the projectile will be fired where the barrel of the machine gun is pointing and not necessarily where you are aiming, so for more precise shots it is recommended that you wait for the turret stop before firing. (It is still possible to fire with it moving)
[*]	[b] ATTENTION[/b], when the projectile is fired, it will travel all the way until it completely loses its synergy and stops, so if there are vehicles in its path and the projectile has enough synergy, it will cause damage and may or may not transfix them. The same logic applies to walls, just as in real life a .50 BMG projectile can transfix some walls and still have the energy to destroy things in its path. [b] So use with caution, a Browning M2HB is not a toy[/b].
[/list]


It is necessary to have the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=2705406713]Military Tool Kit[/url] installed.

[code]
	
	[url=https://ko-fi.com/papachadmods][img]https://i.imgur.com/2uTskWl.png[/img][/url]		[url=https://www.paypal.com/donate/?hosted_button_id=7XQQZQHFTFHBN][img]https://i.imgur.com/thadRPh.png[/img][/url]
[i][b]	If you want me to keep updating my mods, please consider supporting me :)
	I'm open to commissions. Contact me via my Ko-Fi or add me as a friend on Steam.[/b][/i]
[/code]

 This mod cannot be modified by anyone else, for anything else, at all, ever. Even if the user disappears, he or she would much rather you didn't touch the fruits of their hard labour.

publishedfiledetails.title: U.S. M163 VADS by Papa_Chad
publishedfiledetails.description:
[img]https://i.imgur.com/NrstAKw.gif[/img]
This mod adds the M163 Vulcan Air Defense System with Animated Tracks, Doors and a fully functioning Turret to Project Zomboid. It is necessary to have the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=2705406713]Military Tool Kit[/url] and [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2705655822]U.S. M113 APC by Papa_Chad [/url] installed.

 The M163 VADS (Vulcan Air Defense System) is a self-propelled anti-aircraft weapon based on the M113 chassis. Equipped with the M61 Vulcan 20 mm Gatling gun, this vehicle is capable of unleashing devastating firepower
[h1]Resources[/h1]

*Only for B42 SP and MP
*Compatible with old saves
*Compatible with KI5 vehicles

[list]
[*] [b]Animated Tracks[/b]
[*] [b]Tracks Sound[/b]
[*] [b]Animated Doors[/b]
[*] [b]Animated Addons[/b]
[*] [b]Fully Functional Turret with 20 mm Vulcan Cannon[/b]
    The turret uses an advanced custom-coded ballistics system independent of the standard Project Zomboid mechanics, calculating the trajectory, kinetic energy, and penetration of each projectile individually. Damage is applied realistically to vehicles, walls, and bodies.
[/list]

[h1]Advantages[/h1]
[list][*][b]Firepower[/b] - The 20 mm M61 Vulcan is capable of firing up to 3,000 rounds per minute, shredding anything in its path.
[*][b] Rounds[/b] The M61 Vulcan can be loaded with armor-piercing rounds or explosive rounds.
[*][b]Heavy Support[/b] - Perfect for base defense or clearing large hordes.
[*][b]Tracked Mobility[/b] - Able to move over rough terrain where normal vehicles would be stuck.
[/list]

[h1]Disadvantages[/h1]
[list]
[*] [b]High Fuel Consumption[/b] - Its weight and tracked system consume much more gasoline than regular vehicles.
[*] [b]Moderate Speed[/b] - Slow but unstoppable.
[*] [b]Light Armor[/b] - Although based on the M113, its armor protection is limited.
[*] [b]Extremely Noisy[/b] - The tracks and Vulcan cannon make it easy for hordes to hear from a great distance.
[*] [b]Tracks and gun system may require maintenance and repairs.
[/list]

[h1]How to use the Vulcan Cannon[/h1]
[list]
[*]	You must sit in the Gunner's seat.
[*]	Load the [b]Vulcan M61[/b] filled with [b]20 mm Ammo Rounds[/b] into the gun compartment.
[*]	Hold [b]Right Mouse Button to aim[/b] and [b]Left Mouse Button to fire[/b].
[*]	[b]ATTENTION:[/b] The rounds follow the barrel direction, not the mouse cursor. Wait until the turret stabilizes for precise shots.
[*]	[b]WARNING:[/b] The 20 mm Vulcan projectiles are extremely powerful and can penetrate vehicles and walls depending on their energy. Use with caution this weapon is not a toy.
[/list]

[h1]Variations[/h1]
Military versions can be found in military zones, airbases.


[h1]Known BUGS[/h1]
[list]
[*]	Armor does not protect against firearm attacks from NPC mods.
[*]	When loading a save, the turret may temporarily reset its position.
[/list]

It is necessary to have the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=2705406713]Military Tool Kit[/url] installed.

	[url=https://ko-fi.com/papachadmods][img]https://i.imgur.com/2uTskWl.png[/img][/url]		[url=https://www.paypal.com/donate/?hosted_button_id=7XQQZQHFTFHBN][img]https://i.imgur.com/thadRPh.png[/img][/url]
[i][b]	If you want me to keep updating my mods, please consider supporting me :)

[/code]

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42] Mod Manager
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1][b]Description[/b][/h1]
A comprehensive mod manager that adds numerous missing features and fixes existing ones for the in-game modifications menu.

[h1][b]Key Features:[/b][/h1]
- Added sorting by installation & update date.
- Added display of mod changelog (if provided by the author):

[img]https://i.postimg.cc/02KNtjk2/Screenshot-2025-09-13-at-2-48-52-AM.png[/img]

- Added ability to hide modifications.
- Added display of the total number of mods:

[img]https://i.postimg.cc/KcTYQN1L/Screenshot-2025-12-18-at-10-08-05-PM.png[/img]

- Simplified mod information panel:

[img]https://i.postimg.cc/qMT6fYKD/Screenshot-2025-12-18-at-11-19-56-PM.jpg[/img]

- Added "Reload Lua" and "SORT & ACCEPT" buttons.
- Added keyboard navigation with arrow keys and spacebar to toggle mods.
- Added coloring for mods being enabled to distinguish them from those already active.
- Added display of mod status (Up to date / Update available) and last update date.
- Added a warning dialog when disabling library mods and those that other mods depend on:

[img]https://i.postimg.cc/Hxzj4WZr/Screenshot-2025-12-18-at-11-10-12-PM.png[/img]

- Added filter to display only disabled mods.
- Favorited mods now appear at the top of the list.
- Completely redesigned the mod settings menu:
[img]https://i.postimg.cc/BbChT4jT/zomboid.gif[/img]
[img]https://i.postimg.cc/FHf0VNqj/zomboid2.gif[/img]

- Added the ability for mod authors to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3567084868/596287646583594599/]add images[/url] in the configuration window:

[img]https://i.postimg.cc/QC89Gtbb/Screenshot-2025-10-03-at-10-57-06-AM.png[/img]

- Added ability to view and search mods by author.
- Various other interface fixes and improvements.

[h1][b]F.A.Q[/b][/h1]
[h3]1. The mod doesn't show up in the mod list, is this a bug?[/h3]
No, this means you have Build 41, and the mod is intended for Build 42.
[h3]2. Will Build 41 support be added?[/h3]
No.
[h3]3. Is this compatible with [...]?[/h3]
I don't know, check it yourself. And if not, report it.
[h3]4. Will [...] feature be added?[/h3]
While I consider suggestions for improvements, I prefer to work on developing and implementing ideas independently.
[h3]5. I found a bug! Read the comments![/h3]
Bug reports in the comments are ignored and/or deleted. There is a [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3567084868/546746241084660241/]dedicated discussion[/url] for that:

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3567084868/546746241084660241/][img]https://i.postimg.cc/GhgNbmPy/bugreports.png[/img][/url]

[h1][b]Updates:[/b][/h1]
If a mod isn't getting updates and you've already reported any bugs you found in the relevant discussion thread, please [b]DON'T[/b] bother me about it anywhere — Steam, Discord, wherever.

I do this for free in my spare time, and if I lose interest in the game or have stuff going on in my personal life, don't expect updates during that period — that includes replies to comments too.

[quote]
[url=http://theindiestone.com/forums/index.php/topic/2530-mod-permissions/?p=36478]Ask for permission[/url]
This mod can only be added to and extended with the express permission from the original creator. Having received permission, credit must be given to the original creator, both within the files of the mod and wherever the mod roams online.
[/quote]
[quote]

publishedfiledetails.title: '65 Pontiac Banshee
publishedfiledetails.description:
Concept cars are usually used to get a feel for a brand direction but Banshee was more than that, it looked too good to be produced, GM was scared that it would be too big of a in house competition so they had to cancel it. Only a few vehicles were made, a lot of the info was lost, but here we are, with a special little part of automotive history, ready for some action in the apocalypse. There are 3 variants with different performance and handling setups, they share all parts and are deadly, so have fun and be warned!

[b]Main features:[/b]
[list]
[*]3 variants
[*]2 Front bumpers and a rear bumper with 2 tiered protection
[*]Fully animated hood, trunk, doors and windows
[*]Visible interior and character (with improved exit/enter/aim animations)
[*]All parts have 3d objects you can remove and place in the world
[*]Extra parts to craft and find in the world to make the vehicle truly yours (bumpers, bullbars, window armor, roofracks, rollbar, roofs etc)
[*]Multiple texture variations
[*]Armor protection for windows, doors, engine, headlights etc
[*]2 seats
[/list]
[b][list]
[*]You can use it in your current save game, just enable the mod in the load menu
[*]It can tow and be towed
[*]Not recolorable
[/list][/b]

If you can't wait to find this mod in your game and want to test it right away, use [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2634426926]bikinitools[/url] to spawn it, hide the items that your character holds while in vehicle, or for all other good stuff that mod has, go get it!

[b]If you like my mods, please consider supporting me with a coffee[/b],
so I don't fall asleep while making new mods and supporting old ones :)

[url=https://ko-fi.com/ki5mods][img]https://i.imgur.com/2uTskWl.png[/img][/url]

Check out my vehicle mods:

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2490220997][img]https://i.imgur.com/1C4iQ6U.png[/img][/url]

[b]Credits:[/b]
[list]
[*]This mod is commission work by request of Fearne
[*]Thanks to The Indie Stone for making this awesome game and all this possible.
[*]Special thanks to bikinihorst for the huge help with code, optimization, and taking care of the multiplayer and part code.[/list]

[quote=KI5]

Ask for permission
This mod can only be added to and extended with the express permission from the original creator. If no permission is received you may not alter the mod, and it must be treated as a mod that is 'On Lockdown'. You are not allowed to repack this mod under any circumstances, ever. [/quote]
