publishedfiledetails.title: [B42] SecretZ Pandemic (Alpha)
publishedfiledetails.description:
[img]https://i.imgur.com/BrQUXVd.png[/img]

[h1]LINK FOR B41 VERSION[/h1]

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3017588820



[h1]ONLY B42[/h1]

[h1]PLEASE READ BEFORE USE[/h1]

Hello everyone, for all the people who already played SecretZ and SecretZ Pandemic in B41, well you already know what it is, for all the new ones, this a map pack, adding several military checkpoints, bunkers, and some reworked vanilla places into military bases and refugee camps.

In this case, for everyone who already played it, you will notice there's a lot left behind at B41, B42 still under development and official modding tools are not released yet, so this was done thanks to our skillful modding community effort and support which is trying to keep the rythm with all the new changes and so i am.

Said that, still a lot of work to do, probably i was only able to get back 40% of my B41 maps, some of them are lost forever so i'll need to remake them once the stable version get's released.

Tilepacks are not available yet for B42 officially, so you'll probably find that some places are somehow "empty", not at all but it will be easy to notice there are some tiles which should be there and aren't, so this is "normal" for the moment.

I will keep this separated from my B41 versions, because i'll need to update this one with some frequency and still under development so, in advance take that into account, if everything goes well maybe for B42 stable release i'll be able to drop a full version updating my B41 mods and deleting this one, this is important, is just a temporary version, keep that in mind.



[h1]WHAT WE HAVE HERE[/h1]

As you can guess, everything reworked from vanilla Louisville is lost, so main part of the maps are checkpoints around Muldraugh, the reworked Valley Station Mall, and some more checkpoints past Rosewood, plus the Military Complex of Louisville. In resume, you'll get several Military Checkpoints around the map, some reworked places as the Mall like military checkpoint/base, and some hidden underground military bunkers.

Any error/bug you find, post it, so i can take a loot and try to fix it while i keep working on bring back all i can from my B41 maps.

Use it with caution, i wouldn't recommend to use it on an already made world, so just in a new one.

* North-East Muldraugh Checkpoint
* South-East Muldraugh Checkpoint
* North-West Rosewood Checkpoint
* North-West Checkpoint
* Deerhead Lake Military Camp
* Muldraugh EVAC Point Train Depot
* Military Blockade at Rosewood-Muldraught crossing
* Military checkpoint at Marchridge entrance, and a research complex
* Valley Station Mall as Military base


[h1]RECOMMENDATIONS TO USE WITH[/h1]

[h1]THEY KNEW B42[/h1]

Lore friendly with the theme of SecretZ, adding more special items related.

[url= https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3387110070 ][img]https://i.imgur.com/6alTPPv.png[/img][/url]

[h1]KNOWN CONFLICTS[/h1]

In case of conflicts, just be sure to overwrite the prefered map by changing the load order.

publishedfiledetails.title: More Vehicle Options [41.78 and 42.17]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]More Vehicle Options[/h1]

This mod adds more options for vehicles in Sandbox Settings.

Explanation of options:

[b]-Super Low Spawn Chance:[/b] Makes a very low amount of vehicles appear. Recommended to set "Car Spawn Rate" option to "Very Low".

-[b]Vehicle May Be Destroyed[/b]: Determines the probability of finding vehicles with all their parts in terrible conditions.

-[b]Chance Has Battery:[/b] Determines if the vehicles in the world can have battery charge (between 15% and 50%). Only affects if the "Months since apocalypse" option is set to 12. Does not affect vehicles of recent survivors.

-[b]Vehicles May Be Incomplete:[/b] Determines whether vehicles in the world may be missing parts.

-[b]Incomplete Vehicles Chance:[/b] Determines the probability of finding incomplete vehicles. If the selected option is other than "All", vehicles with no missing parts can be found.

-[b]Missing Parts Chance:[/b] Determine the number of parts that will be missing from incomplete vehicles. The higher the probability, the more parts will be missing. If
"Random" is selected, the probability will be different for each vehicle.

-[b]Minimum and Maximum Vehicles Rust:[/b] How rusty will the cars be.

-[B41] Compatible with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2788428718]Vanilla Vehicle Enhancer[/url], [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2807356025]Shark and Cytt's Kentucky Car Overhaul[/url] and [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3001223107]Standarized Vehicle Upgrades[/url].

-I didn't test compatibility with mods in B42.

If you find bugs, please let me know. Suggestions are also welcome.

If you like my mods and would like to support me, you can do so at: [url]https://ko-fi.com/gzeta[/url]

For commissions you can write to me on Discord: [b]gzeta24[/b]

Workshop ID: 2904475897
Mod ID: MoreVehicleOptions

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42.16.3] Ogrim Mod's - My Own Well
publishedfiledetails.description:
[b]Surviving the apocalypse just got a little more hydrating.[/b]

My Own Well lets you build your own functional well — no more relying on pre-placed ones! With a deep crafting system, skill-based progression, and realistic mechanics, this mod brings a new layer of survival immersion to your world.

[h1]🧑‍🤝‍🧑 Multiplayer Testing (Build 42.15.2)[/h1]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/3Vp7eDy.png[/img]

Build 42.15.2 is her and making multiplayer even better, and I’ve updated the mod to support it — and after some effort I managed to set up a local server for testing. However, testing is different from the actual use of the mod.

If you can, please help stress-test it in multiplayer and report anything weird:
• Actions not syncing (well refil system)  
• UI options not applying on server/client  
• Errors in the console / log spam

You can report issues either on my Discord or in the Workshop discussion threads (bugs / comments).  
The more details you can include (server/client, mod list, steps to reproduce, logs), the faster I can fix it. ❤️

[h1]🔨 Build Requirements[/h1]

You'll need more than just a hammer — only true survivors can construct a well!
To craft your own, you'll need:

[h2]Rustic Well[/h2]

[h3]— Skill Requirements:[/h3]
• Masonry: 8
• Woodwork: 6

[h3]— Materials Needed:[/h3]
• Hammer → May Degrade Light
• Pick Axe → May Degrade Heavy
• Shovel → May Degrade Heavy
• Sharp Knife → Not Dull and May Degrade Light
• 8 × Log
• 16 × Plank
• 64 × Nails
• 32 × Stone Block
• 2 x any of: Long Sticks
• 2 × Small Stone Wheels
• 1 × any of: Wood Handles
• 1 × Bucket
• 2 × any of: Sheet Rope or Rope

[h2]Pump Well[/h2]

[h3]— Skill Requirements:[/h3]
• Metal Welding: 8
• Blacksmith: 6

[h3]— Materials Needed:[/h3]
• Hammer → May Degrade Light
• Pick Axe → May Degrade Heavy
• Shovel → May Degrade Heavy
• Bolt Cutters → May Degrade Light
• Pipe Wrench → May Degrade Light
• Welding Mask 
• 10 × Blow Torch
• 8 × any of: Lead Pipe or Metal Pipe
• 16 × Small Sheet Metal
• 64 × Screws
• 32 × Welding Rods
• 4 × any of: Iron Bar, Iron Bar Half, Steel Bar Half or Steel Bar
• 1 × TirePump

[b]The mod includes options to set the requirements and materials and skills to 50%.[/b]

[h1]💦 New Water System Features[/h1]

• See how much water is left in each well (Using default liquid container system, personal fixed after last game release).
• Wells collect 15% of rainwater automatically.
• Every in-game hour, wells refill with 25–75L of water (configurable).
• Water gets split between nearby wells within 20 tiles to avoid construction abuse.

By default, between 25 and 75 liters per hour can fill a 10,000 liter well in 7 to 10 days.

[h1]☣ Contamination System[/h1]

• If you leave rotting zombie corpses near wells, each in-game hour there is a chance the well water will become contaminated by the bodies. 
• The chance increases with both the number of corpses nearby and the time they remain unremoved. 
• It is possible to clean a contaminated well using charcoal. (The amount of charcoal required depends on how much water is in the well)
• The contamination risk and the cleaning cost are configurable in the mod options.

[h1]🚧 Smart Restrictions[/h1]

You can’t build wells underground or on rooftops. Designed to be realistic and balanced for long-term survival. [b]But you can enable build underground in sandbox settings.[/b]

Whether you’re building a forest base, running a self-sustaining farm, or just tired of boiling toilet water — My Own Well is your new best friend.

[h1]☕🧡 Want to Support me?[/h1]
https://ko-fi.com/ogrimdooh

[h1]👥💬 Join the community![/h1]
https://discord.gg/rTd2P6pbEu

[img]https://i.imgur.com/FQt7p8A.png[/img]
This mod cannot be modified by anyone else, for anything else, at all, ever. Even if the user disappears, he or she would much rather you didn't touch the fruits of their hard labour.
http://theindiestone.com/forums/index.php/topic/2530-mod-permissions/?p=36479

Mod ID: GRM-MyOwnWell-50

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42] Moodle Descriptions Expanded
publishedfiledetails.description:
[H1]Please read the description[/h1]

[i]Updates paused until early-May.[/i]

[H1]Version 1.27[/h1]
Track the progress of updates here: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/changelog/3389003300]Changelog[/url]

[H3]Mod Overview[/h3]
MDE aims to re-write vanilla descriptions to be less vague and provide players with more informational negative or positive status effects caused by in game Moodles by introducing the appropriate +/-% and qualitative ratings to indicate severity of status effects.

This mod is by no means related to any other previous Moodle description mods - This mod is purely based on my own interpretations and of the translators who have contributed to the mod.

All descriptions should be represented accurately according to [url=https://pzwiki.net/wiki/Moodle]PZWiki Moodles[/url].

[h3]Compatability[/h3]
• Compatible with everything that does not change Moodle descriptions.
• Compatible with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3495906499] Survival HUD [/url]

publishedfiledetails.title: Washing Menus Improved [B42]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]B42.17.x + Earlier B42 Support (SP/MP)[/h1]
[h3]Better [b]Wash[/b] and [b]Clean Bandages[/b] menus for every water source[/h3]

Improves the vanilla washing menus with cleaner submenus, smarter batching, clearer tooltips, and reliable handling of items inside [b]equipped bags[/b].

Instead of failing the whole action when water is limited, the mod cleans [b]as many items as the available water allows[/b].

[b]✅ Safe to enable or disable mid-save[/b]
No vanilla files are overwritten.

[h2]✨ Main features[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]Smarter washing menus:[/b] cleaner and easier-to-read submenus on valid water sources.
[*] [b]Partial washing:[/b] if there is not enough water for everything, the mod washes what it can instead of canceling the whole action.
[*] [b]Better equipped bag support:[/b] items can be transferred from equipped bags, washed, and returned to their original bag automatically.
[*] [b]Clearer tooltips:[/b] shows water and soap requirements, plus dirt and blood information.
[*] [b]Dedicated bandage cleaning:[/b] adds a separate [b]Clean Bandages[/b] submenu right after [b]Wash[/b].
[/list]

[h2]🧼 [b]Wash[/b] submenu[/h2]
Replaces the vanilla [b]Wash[/b] submenu on [b]all valid water sources[/b], including:
[list]
[*] Sinks
[*] Wells
[*] Rivers
[*] Bathtubs
[*] Rain collectors
[*] Water containers placed on the ground
[/list]

The washing menu includes:
[list]
[*] [b]Yourself[/b]
[*] [b]Inventory[/b] → All / Clothing / Weapons / Equipped / Other + per-item lists
[*] [b]Equipped Bags[/b] → All / Clothing / Weapons / Other + per-item lists
[/list]

[h2]🩹 [b]Clean Bandages[/b] submenu[/h2]
Adds a dedicated [b]Clean Bandages[/b] submenu immediately after [b]Wash[/b] on each valid water source.

Works with items in both your [b]main inventory[/b] and your [b]equipped bags[/b]:
[list]
[*] Dirty bandages
[*] Dirty rags (ripped sheets)
[*] Dirty denim strips
[*] Dirty leather strips
[/list]

[h2]⚙️ Sandbox options[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]Allow tainted water for cleaning[/b] (default: ON)
[*] [b]Water per bandage/strip[/b] (default: 4.00, decimals supported)
[/list]

[i]Note:[/i] the vanilla [b]Enable "Tainted Water" tooltip[/b] option only shows or hides the warning tooltip. It does [b]not[/b] enable or disable cleaning actions.

[h2]🛁 Compatibility: Take A Bath And Shower (TABAS)[/h2]
Compatible with the context menus from [b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3592172476][B42] Take A Bath And Shower[/url][/b].

[list]
[*] [b]Bathtubs:[/b] WMI adds [b]Wash[/b] + [b]Clean Bandages[/b] to the end of the TABAS bathtub submenu.
[*] [b]Showers:[/b] WMI replaces the TABAS shower [b]Wash[/b] entry with the WMI [b]Wash[/b] menu and adds [b]Clean Bandages[/b] right after it.
[*] Works regardless of load order.
[/list]

[h2]🔧 Build 42 support[/h2]
Designed for [b]Build 42.17.x[/b] and earlier Build 42 versions through versioned mod branches.

Recent compatibility updates include:
[list]
[*] Support for the newer [b]JSON-based translations[/b] used by recent B42 builds.
[*] Adaptation to vanilla washing and soap-handling changes introduced in newer B42 versions.
[*] Fixes for recent B42 [b]Wash[/b] and [b]Clean Bandages[/b] action compatibility issues.
[*] Fixes for clothing washing from [b]equipped bags[/b] and other transferred containers on recent builds.
[/list]

[h2]✅ Compatibility and load order[/h2]
[list]
[*] If you use other mods that also edit water-source context menus, you may need to adjust load order.
[*] If you see missing, duplicated, or misplaced menu entries, please post a [b]Bug Report[/b] with your mod list.
[*] Compatibility patches can be added when needed.
[/list]

[h2]💬 Feedback[/h2]
If you find [b]Washing Menus Improved[/b] useful, please consider leaving a [b]thumbs up[/b] and adding it to your [b]favorites[/b].

Feedback, bug reports, and compatibility reports are always welcome.

publishedfiledetails.title: '86 Oshkosh P19A + Military Trailers
publishedfiledetails.description:
It's the end of the world, food, fuel and beer are running low, zombies are ruining every base you make, it's time to go full nomad. Move from place to place, loot all the places and move on. P19A is the perfect vehicle for that. Built to last for decades in warzones and harsh terrain. There are 4 variants, every with it's own set of unique features.

[b]Main features:[/b]
[list]
[*]3 Chassis variants: KY Fire Department, USMC Truck and Rare Fortress Truck (Burnt one included)
[*]They all have escape hatch on the roof that takes passenger of the 4th seat to the back left corner of truck
[*]Seats 3 and 4 can't be accessed by zombies, so they are good for sleeping in case you don't have armor plates.
[*]Fully animated hood, trunk doors, doors and windows
[*]Visible interior and character (with improved exit/enter/aim animations)
[*]All parts have 3d objects you can remove and place in the world
[*]Extra parts to find in the world to make the vehicle truly yours (fenders, armor plates, roofracks, spare tire mounts etc)
[*]Run-flat Tires - They will run even when damaged, but with reduced friction
[*]Many texture variations
[*]Armor plates for windows and engine protection[/list]

[b]Military Trailers[/b]
[list]
[*][b]M1082 Trailer[/b]
[*][b]M1082 Covered Trailer[/b]
[*][b]M1095 Trailer[/b]
[*][b]M1095 Covered Trailer[/b]
[/list]

Trailers are compatible with any military vehicle mods, just don't try to attach them to small cars, it will probably not work. 

[b][list]
[*]New vehicles, not replacing any other
[*]You can use it in your current save game, just enable the mod in the load menu
[*]4 seats inside
[*]It can tow and be towed
[*]Not recolorable
[/list][/b]

publishedfiledetails.title: Military Tool Kit
publishedfiledetails.description:
This is a support mod for my other mods, it adds scripts and tools to create [b]Real Military Vehicles[/b].
   It is compatible with [b]B42 Single and Multiplayer[/b] and partially compatible with [b]B41 Single and Multiplayer[/b]

   The mod is partially functional with the B42 MP, I'm working on bugs and synchronization issues. I had to give up many aesthetic aspects, but the mod is much more optimized now, please let me know of any bugs in the appropriate discussion.

	[b]Keep in mind that the mod is a work in progress for the B42 MP.[/b]


[h1]Features [/h1]
[list]
[*] [b]Animated Tracks[/b]
[*] [b]Animated Doors[/b]
[*] [b]Animated Addons[/b]
[*] [b]Armored Vehicle Body[/b]
[*] [b]Cross-Drive Transmission[/b]
	Which allows some vehicles to rotate on their own axis while stationary.
[*] [b]Ammo Storage[/b]
[*] [b]Turrets for vehicles[/b] (It's not implemented to B41)
    The turrets have heavy coding that adds a completely new ballistics system that is independent of the systems in the Standard Game, performing calculations for each projectile independently, calculating its energy, ability to cause damage and transfix bodies, vehicles and walls.
[*] [b]Common Items and Tools[/b]
   This mod also adds items and parts common to my mods. Note, none of these items will spawn in your game unless you have the mod in which it is used.
[/list]
[h1]Cannons/Heavy Machine Guns[/h1]
 My mod currently adds support for the following heavy machine guns
[*] [b]Browning M2HB[/b]
	That uses [b]12.7x99mm (.50 BMG) Round[/b], which must be loaded into the [b]12.7x99mm (.50 BMG) Ammo Box[/b].
[*] [b]M61 Vulcan Cannon[/b]
	That uses [b]20mm Round[/b] and [b] 20mm Round HE (High Explosive)[/b].
[*] [b]76mm Gun M32[/b]
	That uses [b]76mm Round (Armor Piercing)[/b] and [b] 76mm Round HE (High Explosive)[/b].
[*] [b]M1919A4 Coaxial Machine Gun[/b]
	That uses [b]7.62mm NATO Round[/b].
[/list]
[list]
[h1]Enhanced Aiming System[/h1]
	When aiming with the tank, a Circle will indicate the mouse position, and the Triangle will indicate where the cannon is actually pointing. The shot can be fired at any moment, but it will go where the cannon is aiming, not where the mouse cursor (circle) is, unless both are aligned.
[/list]
[list]
[h1]Ammo Storage[/h1]
	Main cannon ammunition is stored in the Ammo Storage; you can reload the cannon by dragging the ammo onto the cannon.
	Or [b]Pressing the R key[/b], which will play an animation with custom sounds and also grant XP in Reloading.

[/list]
[*] PTBR translate by "Tanque Soviético T-34-85".


   If you like my mod feel free to buy me a coffee :)

[url=https://ko-fi.com/papachadmods][img]https://storage.ko-fi.com/cdn/kofi6.png?v=1[/img][/url]

 [img]https://projectzomboid.com/images/MODS_04.png[/img]
 [b]On Lockdown[/b]
 This mod cannot be modified by anyone else, for anything else, at all, ever. Even if the user disappears, he or she would much rather you didn't touch the fruits of their hard labour.

Mod ID: Military_Tool_Kit
Mod ID: Military_Tool_Kit

publishedfiledetails.title: U.S. M113 APC by Papa_Chad
publishedfiledetails.description:
[img]https://i.imgur.com/NrstAKw.gif[/img]

This mod adds M113 APC with Animated Tracks, Doors and a fully functioning Turret to Project Zomboid. It is necessary to have the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=2705406713]Military Tool Kit[/url] installed.

The M113 is a fully tracked armored personnel carrier (APC).Extremely powerful vehicle with its tracks is the ideal vehicle to smash hordes and with your Browning M2HB carnage is guaranteed.


*Only for B42 SP and MP
*Compatible with old saves
*Compatible with KI5 vehicles

[h1]Resources[/h1]
[list]
[*] [b]Animated Tracks[/b]
[*] [b]Tracks Sound[/b]
[*] [b]Animated Doors[/b]
[*] [b]Animated Addons[/b]
[*] [b]Armored Vehicle Body[/b]
[*] [b]Turret [/b] (It's not implemented to B41)
    The turrets have heavy coding that adds a completely new ballistics system that is independent of the systems in the Standard Game, performing calculations for each projectile independently, calculating its energy, ability to cause damage and transfix bodies, vehicles and walls.
[/list]

[h1]Advantages[/h1]
[list][*][b]Armor[/b] - Its fully armored and can crush entire hordes without suffering damage to its body.
[*][b]Browning M2HB[/b] - It uses the powerful 12.7 x99mm (.50 BMG) ammunition capable of annihilating anything in front of it. (Just like in real life, the projectile can transfix walls, vehicles and bodies).[/list]

[h1]Disadvantages[/h1]
[list][*] [b]High consumption of gasoline[/b] - Because of its weight and tracks it will need much more gasoline than normal vehicles.
[*] [b]Low speed[/b] - Slowly but with style.
[*] [b]Very noisy[/b] - Make a lot of noise that can attract a lot of attention wherever it goes.[/list]
	In future updates, The tracks may break and will need to be repaired, and the machine gun will not spawn in the vehicle. As the mod is in BETA, I left it installed by default in order to find and resolve any bugs it may have.

[h1]How to use the Machine Gun[/h1]
[list]
[*]	The machine gun must be installed in the vehicle
[*] You must sit in the Gunner's seat
[*] You must place the [b]12.7x99mm (.50 BMG) Ammo Box[/b] already loaded with [b]12.7x99mm (.50 BMG) Round[/b] in the gun compartment.
-Due to limitations in B42.13, the Ammo Box is no longer necessary in this version, so just drop the ammunition into the weapon’s compartment.
[*] Press the [b]Right Mouse Button to aim[/b] and the [b]Left Mouse Button to fire[/b].
[*]	[b] ATTENTION[/b], the projectile will be fired where the barrel of the machine gun is pointing and not necessarily where you are aiming, so for more precise shots it is recommended that you wait for the turret stop before firing. (It is still possible to fire with it moving)
[*]	[b] ATTENTION[/b], when the projectile is fired, it will travel all the way until it completely loses its synergy and stops, so if there are vehicles in its path and the projectile has enough synergy, it will cause damage and may or may not transfix them. The same logic applies to walls, just as in real life a .50 BMG projectile can transfix some walls and still have the energy to destroy things in its path. [b] So use with caution, a Browning M2HB is not a toy[/b].
[/list]

[h1]Variations[/h1]
Today, there are military and police variations, which can be found in traffic jams, military zones, police stations and farms.

[h1]Future Updates[/h1]
[list][*]Military extra parts and own tools will be added to reapir them.
[*]More paint variations will be added.
[*]It will need to find the M2HB and then install it
[*]Tracks may break with use
[/list]

It is necessary to have the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=2705406713]Military Tool Kit[/url] installed.

[code]
	
	[url=https://ko-fi.com/papachadmods][img]https://i.imgur.com/2uTskWl.png[/img][/url]		[url=https://www.paypal.com/donate/?hosted_button_id=7XQQZQHFTFHBN][img]https://i.imgur.com/thadRPh.png[/img][/url]
[i][b]	If you want me to keep updating my mods, please consider supporting me :)
	I'm open to commissions. Contact me via my Ko-Fi or add me as a friend on Steam.[/b][/i]
[/code]

publishedfiledetails.title: [42MP] Excavation - Basement Expansion
publishedfiledetails.description:
Allows digging through walls to create and expand basements. To dig, you need a shovel and some sacks to carry the dug out dirt in. You will need to sledgehammer any walls or pull up any flooring in the way first. Digging will tire you out very quickly.

[h1]Future features[/h1]
While it is to the best of my knowledge stable, this mod is still WIP. Intended future features are:
[list]
[*]Building stairs upwards out of underground areas.
[*]Stability system that requires you to build sufficient supports or risk a lethal cave-in.
[*]'Minecraft' mode with underground ores.
[*]Allow building new walls directly on top of dirt walls.
[/list]

[h1]Known issues[/h1]
[list]
[*]Excavated areas are not considered indoors, so you will be rained on. This seems to be a hard limitation of the game, but I am still researching ways to bypass this. [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3392001801/595156583817744399/]See the FAQ detailing why this is so complex.[/url]
[*]Digging more than one layer down causes extreme visual bugs, so it is disabled by default.
[*]Some flooring cannot be removed or dug through.
[/list]

publishedfiledetails.title: P42 Animal Trailers Exorcised
publishedfiledetails.description:
Animal trailers are possessed by the devil. Especially that horse one.

It moves by itself, and has horrid setup which makes it incredibly easy to flip and fling both it and your car by simply trying to connect it for towing. Topping it all off is that the capacity is low... meaning more trips and which makes flipping these things your reality.

Screw that noise!

This mod makes animal trailers significantly harder to flip with driving and flipping on connection, does much to mitigate/prevent violent movement on connect and is unlikely to cause damage to itself or your towing vehicle. Also increases capacity so that you can fit at least two cows. The bigger trailer can fit even more but is a bit bulkier to tow so there is some practical difference between both trailer types.

You don’t want to know how many times I flipped the stupid horse trailer before being pushed to the edge and modding it out. Or how many times my trailer decided to drive itself into the woods or a wall, sometimes taking my car with it. As luck would have it, it also was a pain to fix. I seriously hate these things.

Also prevents animals from immediately dying when leaving a trailer: animals are coded to die in any non-zero velocity vehicle impact and trailers no longer move on their own with this mod.

You are welcome.

Workshop ID: 3462845821
Mod ID: P42AnimalTrailersExorcised

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42MP/B41]Surviving Through Seasons
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Update 12/11/2024[/h1]
[list]
[*]Updated for B42 Multiplayer (Should work with future B42 MP Updates)
[/list]

[h1]Update 11/8/2024[/h1]
[list]
[*]New parameters per season!
[*]You can now change month lengths per season (Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter). So you can have shorter spring months, and longer winter months for example.
[*]You can now disable whole season by setting the parameter to 0. So if you want full length winters, set winters to 0, and Spring, Summer, and Fall you can set to between 1-15.
[*]You can set all parameters to 0 to disable the mod so you don't need to unsubscribe if you want to disable temporarily.
[*]Save game compatible, safe to add/remove mid-save (you may need to run your game with -debug mode to update your sandbox variables on an existing save else they will default to 10).
[/list]

[h1]Mod Summary:[/h1]
[list]
[*]Experience a multi-year/season playthrough without having to put in hundreds of hours or have incredibly short days to do so.
[*]Always have something to prepare for eg. cold and scarce winters, hot and dry summers.
[*]This mod allows the player (or host of a world) to set the max amount of days in a month. Min 1 to Max 15.
[*]Based on the value set, the mod will dynamically simulate a shorter in-game month.
[*][b]NEW 11/8/2024:[/b] You can now change month lengths per season (Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter). So you can have shorter spring months, and longer winter months for example. You can also now disable whole seasons or all seasons without having to unsub from the mod.
[*]Should be compatible with all mods.
[*][b]Example:[/b] 10-day months create a 2 day skip. At every end of the day, 2 days will be skipped. [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fb9GInHQW7I] See time-lapse video and pay attention to clock which simulates roughly 7-day months[/url]
[/list]


[img]https://i.imgflip.com/656yvw.jpg[/img]
[h2]Time-lapse of Seasons:[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/8uKAIwp.gif[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/kehPlLT.gif[/img]

[h2]How does this affect water/electric shut-off, farming, erosion, days survived, etc.?[/h2]
[olist]
[*]Time-based sandbox settings (zombie/loot respawns included) will only progress when time progresses normally through gameplay and or sleeping (also sped-up time in singleplayer).
[*][b]Example:[/b] Lets say your world starts on 7/9/1993 (MM/DD/YYYY) and you set water/electric to shut-off on day 10.
[*]If you set this mod with 10-day months. Your water/electricity will NOT shut-off on 7/19/1993.
[*]Instead it will shut-off around 8/13/1993, almost a month later because you've only played 10 in-game days but simulated a month of time passing.
[/olist]

[h2]More Info and recommendations:[/h2]
[list]
[*]If playing with 10-day (or less) months, set vanilla hours per day to 2+ so you have more time per day for tasks.
[*]Generally, players may want to increase farming speeds and yields if playing with 10-day (or less) months.
[/list]

[h2]Compatibility:[/h2]
[list]
[*]Tested w/ B41, can be added/removed from an existing save. (Likely works w/ B40).
[*][b]Multiplayer and dedicated server compatible.[/b]
[*]Shouldn't conflict with any other mods.
[/list]

[h2]Future plans:[/h2]
[list]
[*]Additional settings to allow specific seasons or quarters of the year to be longer (such as winter).
[*]If possible, develop settings to automatically scale farming speeds, erosion etc.
[/list]

[h2]Known issues:[/h2]
[list]
[*]Adjustments to code needed to factor in carry-over day settings above 15-20. Currently recommended to play with 20 or less days, though the calculations will still be off.
[/list]

[h2]Credits:[/h2]
[b]Inspired from Initial mod:[/b]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/nigeleuh]nigel[/url]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2739115437]A faster months mod[/url]


Workshop ID: 2753086629

publishedfiledetails.title: Multi-Cpu Enhance
publishedfiledetails.description:
The mod does NOT parallelize zombie AI/pathfinding (impossible via JVM)

It's not "magical multi-threading," but optimized GC + memory =
less lag spikes even if the main thread remains single-core.


RIGHT CLICK ON ZOMBOID PROJECT ON STEAM -- MANAGE -- BROWSE LOCAL FILES
AND REPLACE PROJECTZOMBOID64.JSON WITH THIS:

Working with B42.13+ / B42.14+ BUILDS / B42.15+

[code]
{
  "mainClass": "zombie/gameStates/MainScreenState",
  "classpath": [".", "projectzomboid.jar"],
  "vmArgs": [
    "-Djava.awt.headless=true",
    "-Xms4096m",
    "-Xmx8192m",
    "-XX:+UseParallelGC",
    "-XX:ParallelGCThreads=4",
    "-XX:+UseNUMA",
    "-XX:+AlwaysPreTouch",
    "-XX:+DisableExplicitGC",
    "-XX:+ParallelRefProcEnabled",
    "-XX:+OptimizeStringConcat",
    "-XX:+UseStringDeduplication",
    "-XX:+UseCompressedOops",
    "-Dzomboid.steam=1",
    "-Dzomboid.znetlog=1",
    "-Djava.library.path=win64/;.",
    "-XX:-CreateCoredumpOnCrash",
    "-XX:-OmitStackTraceInFastThrow"
  ],
  "windows": {
    "6.1": {
      "vmArgs": [
        "-XX:+UseParallelGC",
        "-XX:G1NewSizePercent=20",
        "-XX:G1ReservePercent=20",
        "-XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=50"
      ]
    },
    "10.0.17134": {
      "vmArgs": [
        "-XX:+UseParallelGC",
        "-XX:ConcGCThreads=2",
        "-XX:+UnlockExperimentalVMOptions"
      ]
    }
  }
}
[/code]

---->Click here [url=https://youtu.be/vvJ0VpGTs4Y?si=I5Ob0wRPprAO6lXM/]  B41.78.16 [/url]

[url=https://ko-fi.com/4zeta85][img]https://i.imgur.com/y0IIKa9.png[/img][/url]

You can freely use this mod on your server.
Mod permission according to TIS policy: Ask for permission [theindlestone.com]. Redistribution of this mod without explicit permission from the original creator is prohibited under any circumstances. This includes, but not limited to, uploading this mod to the Steam Workshop or any other site, distribution as part of another mod or modpack, distribution of modified versions. The product can be published on Steam only through an account with the name 4Zeta

publishedfiledetails.title: Beauty of zomboid
publishedfiledetails.description:
***MOST OPTIONS HAVE BEEN MERGED INTO ONE, UNTIL B42 IS STABLE***
***GRASS TYPES AND SEPARATE FOLIAGE/NATURE WILL BE IMPLEMENTED***

[img]https://i.imgur.com/MEk9k9y.png[/img]

[h1] 5000+ Retextures! [/h1]

[h1] "Why? why does the apocalypse look so CLEAN? WHY IT LOOKS LIKE AN IKEA ROOM?" [/h1]

[h1] A Complete retexture of zomboid world [/h1]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/84AEScK.gif[/img]

[h3] Important notes [/h3]

B41 Version adapted by VVentos: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3418910140

[list]

[*]How to use it:
1-Enable beauty of Zomboid base mod
2-Enable Dense environment  addon from this mod - Credits to mazisky for original code
3-Enable either grass 1 or 2 depending on your tastes
[img]https://i.imgur.com/MdCvil6.gif[/img]

[*]This is technically a lightweight mod, the texture replacements use the same pixels amount as original game, the desne environment addon may impact performance a bit!

[*]It works with maniks 10+ years later, but choose your load order to see wich one you want priority

[*]This uses a modified version of dense environment on it`s own, dont  use it along with the original

[*]By selecting on ~workshop~ tab you automatically selected the correct versions for your server

[*]It should be fine to install it mid playtrough[/list]

[*]It has a couple issues still like, whitelines where i forgot to overpaint, some props connecting with blacklines in the middle, but nothing gamebreaking, i tested it for about some days on a normal playtrough[/list]


After going trough a surgery and having 15 days to rest and chill, I came across the idea: What if zomboid tiles were better? the game still looks like from 2013, why are there almost no mods to improve textures and make the quality of them better? Also Devs seem to have prioritized the mechanics updates wich is completely understandable, So I began this project! to make zomboid look better.

What first was intended as a kitchen prop-apocalypse remake now is a  complete retexture/re-render/improvement/paintover in almost all zomboid tiles and textures, I made sure to make the textures fit both a vanilla and post-10 year later Runs, so you`re good with both! everything has been re-rendered, with AI assisted renders and photoshop manual overpainting, with some years of concept art industry knowledge to back it all up! Almost everything has had a retexture, trees, floors, houses, grass, dirt, containers and props.


[h3] How many people are working with you on this? [/h3]
Me, 5 cans of monster energy, a cintiq wacom and an rtx 3060ti

[h3] Does this kills my FPS? [/h3]

In theory no! every texture is still the same amount of pixels and weight of the original ones, just overall more modern art direction now( but controlled), no upscaling(I tried but the game doesnt let  you go beyond a certain amount of pixels per tile)

[h3] Is it buggy? [/h3]
It`s more than 5000+ retextures, bugs will appear, sometimes white lines between tiles I forgot to overpaint, or a repeating pattern looking way to uncomfortable, but nothing that should break your save or that I wont update later, feels free to tell me repeating textures or things i didn`t notice.


[h3] You cant improve the graphics it can`t render volume and shadows!! [/h3]
Partially right, It doesnt have a modern render engine, but that doesn`t mean volume cant be faked with well positioned painted ambient occlusion and good render passes on props, but not too much that would deviate from the game original art style, I had to be really carefull with that.

[h3] How is this different from manik 10 years retexture? [/h3]

Manik didn`t touch sprites or structure of props, yet manik overlayed textures on top of basegame pre-created sprites, in my case im trying to go a step further and completely remake some of the tiles and props, trough AI + Paintover techniques used in concept art industry

[h3] What are parts that are hard to improve?  [/h3]
Right now the big three is
1- Floors( they are square tiles put near each other, if i apply a texture it needs to be seamless other wise it looks horrendous, I want to find a way to integrate scripting to create more variety on floor but that`s for later
2-Repeating tiles, same problem as floors, i need to be really conservative on the texture and render of those
3- Ambient occlusion, needs to be done manually in order to have great results, and I can only have a confirm every  time i open the game, enter and see the prop myself and If I painted the occlusion right, Doing this with trees right now because they`re less amount in comparison to other props

[h3] What are the other mods in  your pictures?? [/h3]

Currently there are some other mods I recommend, and were intended to be run along with this
they are:

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2820757649] 10 years later [/url]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2975848784] Enhanced environement [/url]

[url=https://www.instagram.com/kingames.psd/l/] [img]https://i.imgur.com/ndhVNZY.png[/img] [/url]

publishedfiledetails.title: Functional Appliances 2
publishedfiledetails.description:
[b][h3]New Extractor Hoods in B42![/h3][/b]
Build them in a square where you place your generator to extract the fumes from it out of the building. All automatically, but the hood will not start until power is there and will not extract unless a running generator is under the hood in the same square.

[h3]Popcorn Machines:[/h3] They now have function and will convert instant popcorn packets and Sapph's corn kernels into edible popcorn without the pain of burning the food. Popcorn buckets are also added and you can butter the popcorn when in a bucket.

[h3]Hotdog Machines:[/h3] They also have function and will keep from burning those priceless sausages. It uses the base sausages and sliced bread in the game to make hotdogs. If you have Sapph's Cooking mod installed you can also use the sausages and hotdog buns from that mod as well.

[h3]Soda Fountains:[/h3] Are now plumbed objects and will dispense water for all the uses. It also dispenses soda to fountain cups and refills soda/water bottles for preserved use. They use soda syrups and CO2 tanks to operate. Just drop them into the counter the table top unit is on or put them inside the Slurp 'n Burp stand alone unit. These can now pull materials from any static container in the square or the square behind them. If water is a problem, try using water jugs from the Water Dispenser mod or plumb it like a sink.

[h3]Bar Taps and Kegs:[/h3] Setup the kegs in furniture containers with the taps connected and you can pour them into red cups, beer mugs, and refill bottles. Kegs pulled from any static container in the square with the bar taps. Compatible with More Brews and More Brews kegs now spawn in bar tap counters in 2.0!

[h3]Food Display Counters, Buffet Islands, Rounded Glass Display Counters and Vending Machines:[/h3] These are now temperature settable appliances for food storage. Some have Hot, Warm, or Cold others just Warm and Cold. Each segment has it's own settings. Now automatically spawn on when they can and switch to cold in ice cream parlors, cafes, bakeries, etc. Pizza parlors and other restaurants will default to warm. Soda Machines automatically default to cold.

[h3]Industrial Deep Fryers:[/h3] The deep fryers are now setup to randomly have vegetable oil in them. You can add and drain the oil. If you have power you can turn them on and fry various foods that usually need a pan of oil with Sapph's Cooking mod. A few new recipes have been added: Doughboys, Chicken Fillets, Chicken Tenders, Blooming Onions, and Fried Cheese Sticks. Added Sapph's Cooking Oil for a second option to fill the vats.

[h3]Pay Phones:[/h3] They have a minor interaction. If you touch them they might just ring but they'll attracted anyone that happens to be listening...

[h3]Wall Clocks:[/h3] They will now display the time. Clicking on them will give you the current time. Wall clocks work based on electrical availability, once off the grid they run off a built in battery that slowly drains. Power up a generator and they will recharge over time. If you are desperate, you can drop a new standard flashlight battery into them, but that's a one way trip. Clocks now show 12hr format and stop when dead. All times accurate to the minute when powered.

[h3]Paper Towel and Napkin Dispensers:[/h3] They show up as small containers and come standard with a few loose napkins. Napkins can now dry off players and wet napkins can wash off dirt and blood. In order to allow compatibility, the new ones drop in the dispensers. Vanilla game napkins have a conversion recipe to use them as well.

[h3]Coffee X-Press and Espresso Machines:[/h3] Turn on the machines and make coffee. Use mugs, teacups, and thermos or lowballs from Sapph's. Tea and instant coffee options exist in the vanilla game to make a quick fix. Add Sapph's to get coffee beans that can be ground in the espresso machine. Then you can brew coffee or espresso. Take the espresso and foam it up in the espresso machine with any "Milk" type item and turn it into a latte or cappuccino. Don't forget you need power and water. If the water is off, any source in inventory will be used for the coffee.

[h3]Bulletin Boards:[/h3] Both large and small corkboards, you can now add things into it's container and the random notices appear as overlays.

[h3]Peg Boards:[/h3] Containers that spawn with tools and have an overlay.

[h3]Pool Cue Stands:[/h3] Containers that spawn with pool cues and have an overlay.

[h3]Green Oven Silo Generators:[/h3] It has the same depletion of fuel as a standard generator and the same radius of power. It can be turned on/off manually and has an auto start feature. It can be used as a backup to other generators and sense when it's time to start. It can be repaired with electronic scrap or scrap metal but will need the generator skill, electrical 4, or mechanics 4. The standard generator manual can spawn with the machine. It has a 500 fuel unit capacity and can be filled or emptied while running. There is no need to connect these as they are stationary and already connected. If found indoors they are also vented to keep the toxins out. If they run out of fuel they will shutdown, if they run out of condition they make a loud *BOOM* that attracts zombies in the area. Now moveable in 2.0 thanks to the Green Oven Corporation! "When your power is lean, buy Green!"

[h1][b]***Fuel and Condition of generators zeros out when moving, empty first!***[/b][/h1]

v2.07 Updated for B42 Not all aspects of the original Functional Appliances are available due to changes in the game.
   Display cases, buffets, and vending machines still have temperature settings.
   Peg boards still load tools.
   Bulletin boards now have photos and fliers.
   Napkins can be used to dry or wet them to wash yourself off in a pinch. Vanilla napkins can be converted to FA
      versions.
   Beer taps work with the kegs and pour into an empty vessel of choice.
   Soda Fountains now have all the flavors available in the game, non-vanilla sodas will spawn with cans and bottles
      too.
   Popcorn machines use vanilla items to still make the popcorn.
   Hotdogs can now be made with sausage or wieners and use the vanilla buns or bread slices. All can be cooked in
      the machine.

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42]Project RV Interior
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h3][i] Based on the mod by Maxwelt, RobRendell, and AuD; credit to them. 📜✍[/i][/h3]


Do you enjoy the nomadic lifestyle? Then this is the mod for you!
This mod adds interior environments to industrial vehicles. It lets you enter and exit whenever you want, store your items, and furnish the space however you like. Basically, you’ll be able to have the RV Interior you always dreamed of — now in Build 42.


[h2] FEATURES 🚀 [/h2]
[list]
    [*]MULTIPLAYER COMPATIBLE NOW!

    [*]Supports over 200 interior environments

    [*]Built-in electrical system (battery-dependent)

    [*]Rain collectors on the RV roof

    [*]Multiple access points (seats, trunk)

    [*]Customizable interiors

    [*]All trucks and vans (with two seats) are supported

    [*]Supports co-op/split screen
[/list]


[h2] HOW DO I GET IN AND OUT ❓ [/h2]
For some vehicles, you need to stand behind the trunk and press V. For others, you have to be in the seats and press V. Then the radial menu will open and give you the option to enter. To exit, right-click on the ground and at the very bottom you’ll find the option to exit.


[h2] HOW CAN I ACTIVATE IT IN MULTIPLAYER ❓ [/h2]
[code] Go to C:\Users\YourUser\Zomboid\Server
Open your server.ini file; search the following tags and configure it as follows:

Mods=PROJECTRVInterior42

Map=map_distanciado;Muldraugh, KY

DoLuaChecksum=false

SaveWorldEveryMinutes=15

WorkshopItems=3543229299

Save the changes and restart your world with this settings
[/code]

[h2] IMPORTANT NOTES ⚠️ [/h2]
-This mod must be placed first in the mod load order
-This mod is not compatible with challenge modes
-To add it mid-save, follow this [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3656042211]guide[/url]. If you add it without following the guides, you will be sent to the Howling Forest to cry eternally.
-If your interior was reassigned (reset) due to an update error, here's a tool to reassign the one you had before [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3605452142][B42]Project RV Tools[/url].


[h2] FOR MODDERS 🛠️ [/h2]
If you're a modder and would like to create add-ons to add custom interiors to certain vehicles, you'll find the necessary resources [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3604969476]here[/url].


[h2] While this mod is compatible with the following listed mods, I cannot guarantee that all of them are functional in multiplayer; so test them and use them at your own risk ⚠️ [/h2]

publishedfiledetails.title: Unmaintained Vehicles
publishedfiledetails.description:
This mod changes vehicle part spawning to make Project Zomboid experience much harder. Getting vehicle up and running fells like a genuine achievement in comparison with getting vehicle running in Vanilla.

[b]What does this mod do?[/b]
When vehicle part spawns, the mod rolls a die to determine whether each part should be skipped. For some parts however it does not end there -- they can be "Hard-ified" further:

[list]
    [*]Engine and Brakes can spawn damaged [spoiler]new_condition = 2^ln(old_condition)[/spoiler]
    [*]Battery can be discharged and Gas-tank can be Emptied
    [*]Tires can be flat (their pressure will be almost empty)
[/list]

All settings can be configured via Sandbox-Options when starting custom game.
Each setting uses a "1 in N" chance system. Setting a value of 0 disables de-spawning for that part.

[b]FAQ:[/b]

Q: Does this mod work with modded vehicles?
[i]It should! I tested it with KI5's vehicles and several other mods.[/i]

Q: Does it work in Multiplayer?
[i]Works for B42 MP (tested at version: 42.13.1)[/i]

This mod is a spiritual successor to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3231347240]Worse Vehicle Condition[/url], originally made by planetalgol, later re-uploaded by JennsyVonnar. However, I rewrote the mod from scratch, added plenty of Quality of Life features as well as expanded and improved some sub-optimal systems.


Workshop ID: 3490864571
Mod ID: UnmaintainedVehicles
Mod ID: UnmaintainedVehiclesProgressive

publishedfiledetails.title: Easy Distributions API
publishedfiledetails.description:
A library to simplify loot distribution for modders by allowing a new item to copy an existing item's loot probabilities.

[h2]For non-modders:[/h2]

Install this mod if it is a requirement for another mod you want.  This mod can be added and removed freely, but the mods that depend on this may not be so easy-going.

[h2]For Modders:[/h2]

[h3]Enabling the Library[/h3]

Just add the following require line to a lua file in your mod's /media/lua/server folder, then place any calls to library below that so they execute as the .lua file is loaded.

[code]local easyDistroLibrary = require("EasyDistro")[/code]

In your mod.info file add [b]require=\NepEasyDistro[/b] and add the library as a requirement on Steam to make it easier for users to download.

[h3]Adding Items[/h3]

To copy the loot distribution of an existing item call [b]EasyDistro.Add[/b] with the identifier of your new item and the identifier of the item you want to copy. For example, if you create a new type of handgun and want it to show up everywhere the standard 9mm pistol shows up:

[code]easyDistroLibrary.AddItem("Base.MyNewGun","Base.Pistol")[/code]

The [b]Add[/b] function supports an optional third parameter that will multiply the new items probability. For example, to add a purple pen that appear with 25% of the probability of a green pen:

[code]easyDistroLibrary.AddItem("Base.MyPurplePen", "Base.GreenPen", 0.25)[/code]



That's it.  Everything else is optional.


[h3]Enabling verbose mode[/h3]

To aid troubleshooting, you can call [b]EnableVerbose()[/b] which will list every entry as they are found, and again as they are added to the loot table.  Please don't leave this enabled in your mod when publishing, because it will affect all mods using this library and result in log file spam for all users!

[h3]Force Updating the Item picker[/h3]

If for some reason you update the loot tables after the ItemPicker has already initialized, [b]UpdateItemPicker()[/b]  will force the ItemPicker to re-read the ProceduralDistributions table.  This isn't needed if you call [b]AddItem()[/b] as described above, but it you call it later for some reason this allows your changes to take effect.


[url=https://github.com/DrStalker/Zomboid-EasyDistributions/]Github Link[/url]






Workshop ID: 3487312010
Mod ID: NepEasyDistro

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42] More Item Information
publishedfiledetails.description:
Provides additional useful information in the tooltip for a variety of items.

Some items such as food will have the amount of information shown be tied to a relevant skill, such as cooking. This can be disabled in the sandbox settings.

All types of information can be toggled on or off in the mod options if there is something that you don't want to see.

This mod is work in progress. If you have suggestions for more information that could be shown on items or have noticed any bugs then please comment it!

[h3][b]Liquids[/b][/h3]
Can see the hunger satiation and calories of edible fluids such as milk or soda. If the player has the nutritionist trait they can also see the additional nutritional information.

[h3][b]Food[/b][/h3]
[b]Cooking 1:[/b] Shows number of days until the food rots.
[b]Cooking 2:[/b] Shows number of days until the food gets stale.
[b]Cooking 3:[/b] Shows more precise amount of time until the food rots.
[b]Cooking 4:[/b] Shows more precise amount of time until the food gets stale and shows the rough amount of calories the food has, to the nearest 100.
[b]Cooking 5:[/b] Shows more precise amount of calories to the nearest 10.
[b]Cooking 6:[/b] Shows exact number of calories.

[h3][b]Weapons[/b][/h3]
[b]Maintenance 1:[/b] Shows numerical value of condition.
[b]Maintenance 2:[/b] Shows percentage chance to lower condition on use.
[b]Maintenance 3:[/b] Shows more precise percentage chance to lower condition on use.
[b]Weapon Skill 1:[/b] Shows Damage.
[b]Weapon Skill 2:[/b] Shows Crit Chance.
[b]Weapon Skill 3:[/b] Shows Attack Speed.
[b]Weapon Skill 4:[/b] Shows Weapon Knockback.

[h3][b]Firearms[/b][/h3]
[b]Aiming 1:[/b] Shows Damage.
[b]Aiming 2:[/b] Shows Crit Chance.
[b]Aiming 3:[/b] Shows Accuracy.
[b]Aiming 4:[/b] Shows Noise Radius.
[b]Reloading 1:[/b] Shows Reload Time.

[h3][b]Seeds[/b][/h3]
Shows the growing months and the growth time of the seed if the information has been learned from a seed packet.

[h3][b]Clothing[/b][/h3]
Shows numerical values for most of the clothing stat bars as well as the material that the piece of clothing is made out of.

[h3][b]Metal[/b][/h3]
All metal items that can be smelted down or scrapped will show how much metal they can be turned into.

[h3][b]Fire Fuel[/b][/h3]
All items that can be burned will show how much fuel time they provide.

[h3][b]Skill Books[/b][/h3]
Shows time left in hours and minutes to finish reading a skill book.

[h3][b]Electronics[/b][/h3]
Indicates if an item is an electronic that can be dismantled for electrical XP.

[h3][b]Uses Left[/b][/h3]
Items with uses such as lighters or pills will show a numerical value of how many uses are remaining. Batteries and items powered by batteries such as flashlights show the percentage of remaining battery life left.

Workshop ID: 3494474677
Mod ID: MoreItemInformation

publishedfiledetails.title: NeatUI Framework [B42]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]Build Support: B42.0.2 to B42.16
(Build 42 only, SP / MP Host tested on recent builds)[/h2]
NeatUI Framework is a lightweight UI framework/library used by other mods to build cleaner, faster, and more consistent interfaces. You generally don’t “use” it directly — you install it because a mod depends on it. [b]If another mod requires NeatUI Framework, make sure NeatUI Framework is loaded BEFORE that mod.[/b]

[hr]
[h2]What This Mod Is[/h2]
[list]
[*]A shared [b]UI framework[/b] for Project Zomboid mods.
[*]Provides reusable UI helpers (drawing, layout, text rendering, widgets, etc.).
[*]Helps mod authors build interfaces faster and keep them consistent across updates.
[/list]

[h2]Who Needs This[/h2]
[list]
[*]You need this mod [b]only if[/b] another mod lists it as a requirement.
[*]If you don’t have any mods that depend on it, you can leave it disabled.
[/list]

[h2]What It Does[/h2]
[list]
[*]Adds common UI utilities (e.g. text measuring/truncation, consistent rendering helpers, reusable UI components).
[*]Improves maintainability for UI-heavy mods by centralizing shared code.
[*]Keeps dependent mods smaller and easier to update.
[/list]

[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[list]
[*][b]Build 42 (all released versions)[/b]
[*]Tested from [b]42.0.2[/b] up to [b]42.15.3[/b] (SP/MP)
[*]This framework is a library; behavior depends on the mods that use it.
[/list]

[h2]Linux Dedicated Servers (Temporary Workaround)[/h2]
Some [b]Linux dedicated servers[/b] may fail Lua checksum validation with mods due to [b]case-sensitive file paths[/b] (workshop mods sometimes mix filename casing).  
[b]Temporary workaround:[/b] set the following in your server .ini:
[code]DoLuaChecksum=false[/code]
[b]Note:[/b] This is a temporary workaround. A proper fix is planned so Linux servers can keep checksum validation enabled.

[h2]Credits:[/h2] 
[list]
[*] Original mod by [b]Rocco[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199485575276/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600] PZ Workshop Items [/url]
[*] Maintenance / compatibility updates for newer B42 builds by [b]Afyrmo[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199133259679/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600] PZ Workshop Items [/url]
[/list]

Workshop ID: 3508537032
Mod ID: NeatUI_Framework

publishedfiledetails.title: Neat Building V1.5 (B42)
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]Build Support: B42.12 to B42.17
(SP / MP Host tested on recent builds)[/h2]

[b]Neat Building[/b] is a cleaner, faster, and more practical replacement for the default [b]Build[/b] menu in [b]Project Zomboid Build 42[/b].

It reorganizes building into clearer categories, adds better icons, stacks related recipes, includes practical filters, lets you switch between [b]Neat UI[/b] and [b]Vanilla UI[/b] whenever you want, and adds extra buildable constructions to expand the Build menu.

[b]Dependency:[/b] [b]NeatUI Framework[/b] must be enabled and placed [b]before Neat Building[/b] in your mod load order.
[hr]
[h2]Important: Existing saves / servers[/h2]
[b]This is not specific to Neat Building.[/b] Like many mods that add constructions, recipes, items, or placed world content, removing a mod from a world that has already used it can cause problems.

In [b]single-player[/b], removing [b]Neat Building[/b] may still leave existing constructions in the world, but some mod-specific interactions may no longer be available (for example, some custom disassembly options).

⚠️In [b]multiplayer servers[/b], removing the mod from an existing world is more risky and may cause [b]WorldDictionary[/b] issues that can prevent the server world from loading correctly.

[list]
[*] If you only want the [b]vanilla Build menu[/b] back, you do [b]not[/b] need to remove the mod
[*] You can switch UI mode from:
[list]
[*] [b]ESC → Options → Mods → Neat Building[/b]
[*] [b]Right-click (RMB)[/b] the [b]Build[/b] sidebar button for a quick in-game toggle
[/list]
[*] For [b]active MP worlds/servers[/b], the safest option is to keep the mod enabled once that world has already used it
[*] Always make a [b]backup[/b] before removing any mod from an existing save or server
[/list]
[hr]
[h2]Optional addon: XP & Mod Display[/h2]
If you want build XP gain and source mod information to be displayed, try this addon:
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3540503606][b]Neat Crafting & Neat Building – XP & Mod Display[/b][/url]

[h2]Build 42 only[/h2]
If the mod does not appear in your mod list, you are probably running [b]Build 41[/b].
This mod requires [b]Build 42[/b].

[h2]Recent updates[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]Build 42 maintenance:[/b] updated across supported B42 versions, including recent compatibility work for [b]B42.17[/b]
[*] [b]Vanilla ↔ Neat UI toggle:[/b] persistent via [b]Mod Options[/b]
[*] [b]Quick in-game toggle:[/b] [b]Right-click (RMB)[/b] the vanilla [b]Build[/b] sidebar button
[*] [b]Improved Filter Bar:[/b] more filters, cleaner layout, and compact min-size mode
[*] [b]Translation support:[/b] updated for recent Build 42 translation loading changes
[*] [b]Recent fixes:[/b] improved disassembly support, translated disassembly names, floor placement fixes, and recent B42 compatibility updates
[/list]

[h2]Neat Building UI (Optional)[/h2]
You can keep the mod enabled and choose which interface you want to use:
[list]
[*] [b]ESC → Options → Mods → Neat Building[/b] → [b]Use Neat Building UI[/b]
[*] [b]Quick toggle:[/b] [b]Right-click (RMB)[/b] the vanilla [b]Build[/b] sidebar button
[/list]

[b]Note:[/b] Left-click behavior is unchanged.

[h2]Minimal Building Menu[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/NQ4XJMO.png[/img]

The interface is designed to stay compact, so you can keep a better view of the world while planning and placing structures.
[list]
[*] [b]Collapsible window:[/b]
[list]
[*] The recipe details panel is only shown while hovering over it
[*] After double-clicking to build, the panel collapses and leaves only the category panel visible
[*] Moving the mouse back over the category panel reopens the recipe list
[/list]
[*] [b]Recipe stacking:[/b] similar or advanced-design recipes are grouped together, with the currently buildable one prioritized
[*] [b]Transparent recipe info panel:[/b] gives you more visibility while building
[/list]

[h2]Better Categories / Clearer Icons / More Buildings[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/xnGoF3u.png[/img]
[list]
[*] [b]Better categories:[/b] buildings are grouped by type rather than by skill
[*] [b]Clearer icons:[/b] missing icons are added so it is easier to understand each build at a glance
[*] [b]More buildings:[/b] extra builds are included, and suggestions are welcome
[/list]

[h2]Filter Bar[/h2]
The filter bar includes more useful options and a cleaner layout for faster browsing:
[list]
[*] [b]Only Can Build[/b]
[*] [b]Only Met Skill Requirements[/b]
[*] [b]Only Known Recipes[/b]
[*] [b]Min-size mode[/b] for a more compact panel
[/list]

With the addon [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3540503606][b] Neat Crafting & Neat Building – XP & Mod Display [/b][/url] enabled, you can also filter recipes that award XP or come from mods.

[h2]Why use Neat Building?[/h2]
The goal is simple: make building faster to browse, easier to understand, and less intrusive on screen.
You still keep the information you need, but in a cleaner and more efficient layout.

[h2]Gamepad Support[/h2]
Gamepad support is not planned in the short term.
The current focus is finishing the UI redesign work first.
For now, this mod is mainly designed for mouse / keyboard play.

[h2]Mod Compatibility[/h2]
[b]Known conflict:[/b]
[list]
[*] [b][B42] Buildables Expansion[/b]
[/list]

Mods that modify the same Build panel files may also conflict depending on load order.

[h2]Credits:[/h2]
[list]
[*] Original mod by [b]Rocco[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199485575276/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600] Rocco's PZ Workshop Items[/url]
[*] Maintenance / compatibility for newer B42 builds by [b]Afyrmo[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199133259679/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600] Afyrmo's PZ Workshop Items[/url]
[/list]

[quote]About permission
You are free to extract and use any content from this mod without asking for permission.
If you want to republish this mod, you need to follow these rules:
1. There must be substantial and important improvements.
2. If this mod has not been updated for a long time and has caused many issues, you may modify and republish it.[/quote]


Workshop ID: 3536052310
Mod ID: Neat_Building

Workshop ID: 3536052310
Mod ID: Neat_Building

publishedfiledetails.title: More Traits
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]IMPORTANT: Build 42.13 [/h1]
Thanks for the efforts of the community - and most especially FallenxSowerby - the state of Multiplayer Support should be much improved from the previous build.

Please be cautioned that there may still be edge-cases that we have been unable to test/verify which may result in desyncs. As always, please use the Discord or GitHub to report issues. Steam comments/discussions are seldom checked.

Playing as an administrator or disabling all anti-cheat may help resolve client-server disagreements, but this is not recommended.


[h1]A Personal Appeal To Decency: [/h1]
Please do not add me directly on Steam or comment on my profile for help with this mod. Use the discord for that.

[h1]Information: [/h1]
This mod adds a great deal more variety to the traits available to players during character creation.

This includes, but not limited to: traits that add some starting equipment, traits that increase your starting level in specific skills but don't give exp multiplier, a bunch of unique traits with interesting effects (examples: better weapons proficiencies, better exp gains for specific playstyle, zombie infection immunity with cure period, scavenging perks, driving perks, and so on). Of course, your typical +1/2 in some skills perks are also present.

The mod also attempts to add a greater variety of expense, as in there are more -1, -2, -3, -5 traits. This should help prevent the annoying feeling of having one solitary point left with nothing to spend it on.

Dynamic Traits submod is present - you can earn/lose traits by leveling skills, list of all available perks can be found [url=https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1YM0g4fTXrDiDYuvFvNkmqEjFIaTIK4_Bb0dQvmLnIvA/edit?usp=sharing]here[/url]. Note, that almost everything is configurable through sandbox settings (120 Sandbox options). Want to make only specific perks dynamic? I got you covered,choose which perks you want 1by1. Want to change Skill level requirement to make it easier or harder? No problem, do that. Out of 90+ or so available perks, 50+ can be made dynamic. Some additional info can be found [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/1299328280/3551679689856511505/]here[/url]

[h1]Known Issues[/h1]
[list]
[*] Made of Glass trait sometimes hurts players while sleeping. (Haven't experienced this in a while. Might be fixed?)
[*] Killing players in Multiplayer with Martial Artist does not sync their death to the server.
[*] Evasive Insta-Death is usually caused by Instant Infection Mortality. Fix is not expected, as we got no clue why this is happening.
[/list]

[h1]Mod Compatibility: [/h1]
[list]
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2914075159]Evolving Traits World (ETW)[/url] - ETW submod marks which traits are dynamic, also makes Butterfingers dynamic.
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2459400130]Dynamic Traits[/url]  - The mod will opt to use their Alcoholic trait over its own.
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2703664356]The Only Cure - The Amputation[/url] is now officially supported, and the mod will opt to use their amputation method instead of its own.
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2169435993]Mod Options[/url] - Allows for Client-Side settings to be adjusted. Install if you would like extra options.
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2859296947]Moodle Framework[/url] - Adds in support for Alcoholic as a moodle.
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2721945297]Driving skill[/url] - some traits will be auto-disabled to avoid conflicts
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2903135820]Scavenging skill (fixed)[/url] - some traits will give exp boost in scavenging if mod is present. Scrounger perk is disabled if mod is present, it does same thing as Skill. [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2721290568]Original[/url] works too.
[/list]

[h1]Opening The Project Up To The Community: [/h1]
The Mod is now available on GitHub for others to contribute.

https://github.com/hypnotoadtrance/MoreTraits

Here you will find the bleeding-edge version of the mod as I and the community continue to build upon it.

Regular updates will be posted to the Steam Workshop when appropriate.

[h1]Public Discord: [/h1]
Because Steam comment sections aren't the best space for collaboration, I've set up a Discord server for this mod.

Since the project is officially moving to Github and opening up, it stands to reason that anyone working together on the project will want a place to work together.

https://discord.gg/6Czm3uqn4z (p.s. if you tried joining earlier and found you were unable to get passed the react roles, that should be fixed now. Sorry!)

There, you will be able to share servers, discuss suggestions, make bug reports, perform translations, and get help coding for the project.

[h1]Authors:[/h1]
Creators:
HypnoToadTrance - main mod
Fajdek - main mod
MusicManiac - dynamic submod

Additional Coding:
SteadySpring
Xenu47
Aristrofl
Vaikke

hi117
curveo

FallenxSowerby

Translations:
Simplified Chinese, by EndsM
Traditional Chinese, by ????

Portuguese , by Cinissh
Korean, by teray
French, by MonsterKillers1973
Turkish, by Sariserpito, Atlantic
Spanish, by franz, HawK
Polish, by Tony
Italian, by Lore11

publishedfiledetails.title: Somewhat Traits [42.17]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Adds 16 positive, 16 negative and expands 14 vanilla traits[/h1]
(and optional "bonus to skill" traits module)
The information below is for the most recent version of the game, older versions might slightly differ.

[h3]This is Multiplayer focused mod. Though it should work fine overall in Single Player[/h3]
It targets hardcore setting: high pop, tough zombies, sprinters, pinpoint. Please report bugs in discussion thread which corresponds to the version used

[h3]About the traits which affect character stats[/h3]
[list]
[*]The positive traits have very subtle effects, stacking is helpful but won't be game changing
[*]The negative traits on the other hand hit pretty hard as soon as player meets the conditions and some go away as soon as those are no longer met
[*]While positive traits won't counter the effects of negative traits while those are active, they will help with the aftereffects and the amount of character maintenance in general
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h1]MODULES[/h1]
[h3]Somewhat Traits[/h3]
[list]
[*]Adds traits with special mechanics, includes traits like "Dexterous" which make corresponding timed actions twice faster
[*]Removes the restriction of First Aid actions on other players from "Fear of Blood"
[*]Removes the fatigue increase from small amounts of alcohol (i.e. a bottle of beer)
[*]Adds a small chance to trip when running while being very drunk which grows with exhaustion
[*]Frog Companion trait can be expanded with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3582919946]Somewhat Companions[/url] mod
[*]Here you can read all [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3498347699/563626538148338477/]Traits Descriptions[/url] and which vanilla traits were expanded
[/list]
[h3]Somewhat Traits Core[/h3]
[list]
[*]Handles the traits mechanics
[/list]
[h3]Somewhat Traits Skills[/h3]
[list]
[*]Adds +1 to skill traits
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h3]Notes[/h3]
[list]
[*]Does not add new professions or change the existing ones (long term compatibility reasons)
[*]Does not add traits which affect interactions/communication between players or the experience gain (apart from "bonus to skill" traits module)
[*]Big thanks to [b]@clementine[/b] for creating pixel art for traits
[*]A group of experienced players was helping with the ideas for the traits
[*]Compatible with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3427091746]Combat Traits[/url] and [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3685392864]Keen Perception[/url]
[*]Made for [url=https://discord.gg/sunday-drivers]Sunday Drivers[/url] server
[*]Should be compatible with other small trait mods but there might be issues when used with other big trait mods, please do not report bugs related to incompatibility with other trait mods
[*]Can be added to existing save/server
[*]The initial release of this mod took about 80 hours to develop
[/list]

[h3]To Server Owners[/h3]
[list]
[*]This mod avoids [i]sendClientCommand[/i] and doesn't use it for anything meaningful
[*]All stats management is handled entirely by the server
[*]It should have little to no performance impact for both client and server, the load is equally distributed over time instead of running for all players at once
[/list]

A thumbs up helps with the mod's discoverability, thank you if you left one
Check [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/somewhatfrog/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]my other mods[/url], maybe you'd like some of them

[code]Modpacking or reuploading is not allowed as long as the original version is present.[/code]

[url=https://discord.gg/SReMnbV4V7]PZ Modding Community Discord[/url]

Workshop ID: 3498347699
Mod ID: SomewhatTraitsSkills
Mod ID: SomewhatTraitsCore
Mod ID: SomewhatTraits

publishedfiledetails.title: More Variety Loot [B41/B42]
publishedfiledetails.description:
More variety in zombie loot, almost 150 unique objects, all within reason, some food or condiments, materials, small tools, first aid, ammo, toiletries, or entertainment, including B42 items.

In the base mod, small items like a nail or gum have 2%, then the vast majority of items have 1 or 0.5% "materials or edibles", rarer things 0.2 or 0.1%, and the rarest and most valuable 0.05%, so the mod is very balanced, the idea is to make it more interesting to look at zombies, and not turn them into warehouses with legs.

With 4 versions, the first one seeks balance, approximately 1 in 5 zombies gives an item from the list, X2, where it is much more likely that items will come out, and 2 more, with 50% and 25% less probability, if you want less loot.

[h3]Help the mod grow by liking and favoriteing it, and the Z won't bite you![/h3]

[img]https://steamuserimages-a.akamaihd.net/ugc/43449874936701398/646C8E54667043460C0B61138A90DB3E6E61F22C/?imw=5000&imh=5000&ima=fit&impolicy=Letterbox&imcolor=%23000000&letterbox=false[/img]

publishedfiledetails.title: TchernoLib
publishedfiledetails.description:
Features and tools with high reuse potential.


[h1]Global Object[/h1]
[list]
[*]Simple interface to add your own Global Objects to the game.
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2986578314/3812906855246163752/]Details here[/url].
[/list]
see [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2986715593]Portal Gun[/url] mod for B41 example.
see [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3550032314]Moats[/url] mod for B42 example.


[h1]Spawn[/h1]
[list]
[*]Spawn a specific item in a specific container.
[*]Spawn a specific item from a right click + action on a specific square.
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2986578314/3812906855249791467/]Details here[/url].
[/list]
see [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2986715593]Portal Gun[/url] mod for example.


[h1]Sandbox Options[/h1]
[list]
[*]Handle sandbox color selection as enum.
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2986578314/3812906855246425780/]Details here[/url].
[/list]
see [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2986715593]Portal Gun[/url] and [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2970134188]Battle Royale[/url] mods for examples.


[h1]Player Variables[/h1]
[list]
[*]Simple interface for spreading player variables in multiplayer.
[*]Adds IsoPlayer Flags to player variables.
[*]Player variables are (mainly) used for animations.
[*]Adds isCmdReady(). A function to know when sendClientCommand can be safely used in MP.
[/list]
see [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2991010328]Infected Player[/url] mod for B41 example.
see [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3652012357]Lean & Lie[/url] mod for B42 example.


[h1]Movement control[/h1]
[list]
[*]Simple interface for movement control.
[*]Simple interface for teleport.
[*]Vanilla movement speed recomputed with lua.

publishedfiledetails.title: Auto Reload
publishedfiledetails.description:
Optimize reload training.

So you are here ?
Ah.. You don't know what to do, stuck in your base with that broken leg.
You've come to the right place.


[h1]How it works[/h1]
Get that shotgun you just plundered, one or more shells, and train your Reload speed.
Yes, you'll still be able to train while walking after getting back on your feet.
Once you're bored, just think on something else (run, activate combat mode or escape to stop).


[h1]Also[/h1]
The training is functional on magazines.

That's it.

Why are you still here ?
There is nothing more for you here. Get lost !
.. and maybe reduce your sound volume if you increase the game speed..
