publishedfiledetails.title: CleanUI [B42]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]Build Support: B42.12 to B42.17 (Build 42 only)[/h2]

[b]CleanUI[/b] is a streamlined inventory and loot interface overhaul for Project Zomboid Build 42. It redesigns the player inventory and external container panels to make item management cleaner, faster, and easier to read, while keeping the vanilla gameplay flow familiar.

It focuses on a more compact layout, clearer controls, integrated utility actions, and better organization for both personal inventory and loot windows. It also includes an optional in-game toggle to switch between [b]CleanUI[/b] and vanilla inventory panels whenever you prefer.

[b]Requirements:[/b]
[list]
[*]Requires [b]NeatUI Framework[/b] placed BEFORE in the load order.
[*]Build 42 only
[/list]

[b]Support / FAQ / Bug Reports:[/b]
For troubleshooting, compatibility notes, server setup, and bug reports, please check the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3437629766/800092645914367037/]CleanUI Support Hub[/url].

[b]Compatibility note:[/b] Most gameplay and content mods should work normally. Conflicts are more likely with mods that override the inventory UI, loot UI, or inventory context menu functions.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/TFFM7ZZ.png[/img]

[h2]Streamlined Inventory and Loot UI[/h2]
[list]
[*]Moves container buttons to the left for a more natural layout and less wasted space when panels are collapsed
[*]Places utility actions inside a collapsible side panel for a cleaner interface
[*]Integrates [b]Transfer All[/b] and [b]Take All[/b] into the capacity display area
[*]Uses cleaner icons and more consistent panel controls
[*]Redesigns UI textures with minimalist borders and high transparency
[*]Adds automatic text truncation to help prevent overlapping names and categories
[*]Improves text contrast for better readability
[*]Merges hotbar-equipped items into the [b]Equipped Items[/b] section
[*]Includes an optional toggle to switch between [b]CleanUI[/b] and vanilla inventory / loot panels
[/list]

[h2]Clean Usage Guide[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/3qBGq7M.png[/img]
(1) [b]Transfer Button[/b]: Opens a menu to transfer items by category or name. Depending on your settings, it can target one container or nearby containers.
(2) [b]Transfer All / Take All[/b]: Transfers or takes all items. By default, this area also shows inventory capacity.
(3) [b]Lock Button[/b]: Locks the panel so it can no longer be dragged or resized.
(4) [b]Search[/b]: Searches items in the current container. Matching entries move to the top and are highlighted.
(5) [b]Divider Drag Button[/b]: Adjusts the width balance between the name and category columns.
(6) [b]Sort Button[/b]: Opens sorting options such as name, weight, or type.
(7) [b]Collapse / Expand Items[/b]: Expands or collapses all items in the current inventory view.
(8) [b]Equipped Items[/b] (not like in the image): Click the icon next to [i][b]Equipped Items[/b][/i] at the bottom of the main inventory panel to hide or show equipped items.
(*) [b]Inventory UI Toggle[/b]: Right-click the [b]open box icon[/b] in the left-side button bar of the game window (below the primary and secondary hand icons and above the heart button) to open context menu with the [b]Use CleanUI Inventory[/b] toggle.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/0goWZXm.png[/img]
(1) Set the transfer method used by the transfer button
(2) Choose the sidebar position for the personal inventory panel
(3) Choose the sidebar position for the loot panel
(4) Adjust the background transparency of the content area
(5) Adjust the background transparency of the sidebar
(6) Set the text color for item names
(7) Set the text color for item categories
(8) Choose the category text alignment
(9) Enable or disable [b]Use CleanUI Inventory[/b]

[h2]Built-in Container Reordering[/h2]
Integrated container reordering functionality lets you drag container icons to rearrange backpack and carried container slots.

[h2]Controller Support[/h2]
Controller compatibility has not been fully tested. The mod keeps the game's original controller support where possible, but some limitations may still remain.

[h2]Known Compatibility[/h2]
Tested on B42.16.3
[list]
[*]Compatible with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3662387304][b]Item Rarity UI[/b][/url], including support for colored item names and improved CleanUI integration on recent B42.16 builds
[*]Compatible with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3669550831][b]Proximity Inventory B42.16[/b][/url]
[*]Compatible with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3586216562][b]Better Containers[/b][/url], although a few smaller UI issues may still remain and are already being looked at for future updates
[list]
[*][b]Better Containers[/b] and [b]Proximity Inventory[/b] are not compatible with each other[/list][/list]

[h2]Mods you may need to unsubscribe from[/h2]
[list]
[*]Reorder Containers (already integrated)
[*]Search Container (already integrated)
[*]Smart Stack (similar functionality already exists here)
[*]Auto Drop n' Loot (similar functionality already exists here)
[*]Bigger Inventory Container Icon (may cause UI display issues)
[*]JB Max Capacity Override
[*]Other inventory UI overhaul mods that replace the same interface files may conflict
[/list]

[b]Load order:[/b] place [b]NeatUI Framework[/b] before [b]CleanUI[/b].

If you enjoy this mod and your circumstances allow, it would be amazing if you could buy me a cup of coffee, but first and foremost, enjoy your game!
[url=https://afdian.com/a/23754956rocco][img]https://i.imgur.com/WLIHcQs.jpeg[/img][/url]

You can join my discord channel for more information [url=https://discord.gg/QCznu6naGw]Rocco's Mod[/url]

[quote]About permission
You are free to extract and use any content from this mod without asking for permission.
If you want to republish this mod, you need to follow these rules:
1. There must be substantial and important improvements.
2. If this mod has not been updated for a long time and has caused many issues, you may modify and republish it.[/quote]

[h2]Credits:[/h2]
[list]
[*] Original mod by [b]Rocco[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199485575276/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]PZ Workshop Items[/url]
[*] Maintenance / compatibility updates for newer B42 builds by [b]Afyrmo[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199133259679/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]PZ Workshop Items[/url]
[/list]

Workshop ID: 3437629766
Mod ID: CleanUI

publishedfiledetails.title: [B41/B42] More Immersive Vehicles
publishedfiledetails.description:
B41/B42

Project Zomboid is quite a realistic game, but the developers missed one point. Vehicles in the world appear only with closed doors, raised windows and closed trunks. This mod adds more immersiveness and atmosphere to the game! Vehicles can now have doors open, windows down, or trunks open.

I highly recommend using this mod along with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2807356025]Shark and Cytt's Kentucky Car Overhaul[/url] and [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2490220997]vehicles by KI5[/url].
Don't forget to adjust the probabilities in the sandbox settings!

Update from 06.08.2024
You can now separately configure probabilities for vehicles in a parking stall, as well as on the road or in traffic jams!

[b]This mod can be added and removed from your saves without any problems.[/b]

[b]WORKS IN SINLEPLAYER AND MULTIPLAYER![/b]

[b]COMPATIBILITY[/b]
Should be with all vehicles. If it is not, please let me know.

[b]INCOMPATIBILITY[/b]
Not sure, but possible with mods that add a new part that can be opened.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/4HonJNk.png[/img]

[url=http://theindiestone.com/forums/index.php/topic/2530-mod-permissions/?p=36478][img]https://steamuserimages-a.akamaihd.net/ugc/1822269007325041509/9A7DF10F3A778CF98E9CE7423417E8B6EA687334/?imw=5000&imh=5000[/img][/url]

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42MP] FlipVehiclePlusTrailer
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]WHAT THE FLIP!?[/h1]

Ever found yourself flipping your vehicle by accident? Need a way to unflip it? This mod lets you flip any vehicle or trailer back on its wheels!

[h2]How to use?[/h2]
> Car must be stopped.
> Must be outside the vehicle. [b](Can be disabled in Sandbox)[/b]
> Must detach the trailer from vehicle if attached. [b](Enabled by default)[/b]
> Must have a [b]Jack[/b]. [b](Can be enabled in Sandbox)[/b]
> Must have at least [b]level 5[/b] strength. [b](Can be enabled in Sandbox)[/b]
> Bring up the [b]Vehicle Radial Menu[/b], the default key is '[b]V[/b]' then click the flip icon.

All features can be configured through the [b]Sandbox Options[/b], giving you full control over how the mod works!

[h2]What's New?[/h2]
[list]
[*][b]Random Dialogues for Immersion[/b] – Your character now reacts when attempting to flip a vehicle depending on the condition.
[*][b]Jack Required (Optional)[/b] – Enable in Sandbox to require a [url=https://pzwiki.net/wiki/Jack]car jack[/url] to flip vehicles.
[*][b]Strength Check (Optional, Min Level 5)[/b] – Enable in Sandbox to restrict flipping to strong survivors (Strength 5+).
[*][b]Detach Trailer First (Enabled by Default)[/b] – Enable in Sandbox to require unhooking trailers before flipping.
[*][b]Flip Inside Vehicle (Disabled by Default)[/b] – Enable in Sandbox to allow flipping vehicles while inside them.
[*][b]Flip Time Based on Strength[/b] – The time it takes to flip a vehicle depends on your strength level. Stronger characters flip faster. (Level 5-10 strength)
[*][b]Fatigue from Flipping (Disabled by Default)[/b] - Enable in Sandbox to have flipping tire your character. Characters with higher Strength experience less fatigue, and those with high Fitness experience even less.
[*][b]Muscle Strain (Disabled by Default)[/b] - Enable in Sandbox to add strain to your body when flipping. Survivors with lower Strength suffer more muscle strain, temporarily reducing movement speed and combat effectiveness until they recover through rest.
[*][b]Adjustable Flip Speed[/b] - Configure how quickly vehicles rotate back upright (5 speed levels available).
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]Special Thanks[/h2]
Thanks to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/diwnstn2][MLT]뉴비 까마귀[/url] for the ideas! You carried this one.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/tTMflUD.gif[/img]

[h2]Translations[/h2]
Spanish - [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/taylor_mh]Taylor MH[/url]
Russian - [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/norank_scum]norank_scum[/url]
French - [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198027815009]Bobitec[/url]
Korean - [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198062695591]Hx[/url]

[h2]Credits[/h2]
Based on the initial code from the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3030105355]Flip 'n' Attach In Vehicle[/url] mod by [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199079709559]SportXAI[/url], but extensively modified and expanded.

[hr][/hr]
[url=https://discord.gg/SReMnbV4V7][img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Chuckleberry-Finn/chuckImages/main/PZmodCommBanner3.png[/img][/url]

Mod ID: flipvehicleplustrailer

publishedfiledetails.title: Mod Update and Alert System
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]This mod is optional on B41, and [b]required on B42[/b].[/h2]

[h2]Utility mod used for news and updates to mods I authored.[/h2]
[i]Or any other mod utilizing my ChangeLog.txt format. More information below.[/i]

[h3]Information can be found in a small (collapsible) window found in the main-menu, and the escape-menu.[/h3]
[h3]Further more, right-clicking the collapse button will hide the system for the rest of the session. (per Lua load)[/h3]

If you're interested in utilizing this system to display change-logs all you have to do is create a text file named 'ChangeLog.txt' within your mod's common folder. This mod includes a working example. Note the footer and header must be within spaced square brackets, and the footer must have six hyphens. Also, uploading your mod to the workshop will automatically populate the Steam changelog with the last entry - if the mod is enabled on the main menu.

publishedfiledetails.title: Remove Bag Slowdown
publishedfiledetails.description:
Clothing displays a run speed modifier, but it doesn't do anything.

Bags don;t display a run speed modifier, but they do slow you down... and they slow you down even more when full.  To make this worse, the speed penelty for all bags gets added together and if you use mods to let you shove more stuff into a bag the penelty just keeps on getting higher until you can barely move.

This mod removes the speed penel;ty from all bags. This is safe to add/remove midgame.Clothing displays a run speed modifier, but it doesn't do anything.

Bags don't display a run speed modifier, but they do slow you down... and they slow you down even more when full.  To make this worse, the speed penalty for all bags gets added together and if you use mods to let you shove more stuff into a bag the penalty just keeps on getting higher until you can barely move.

This mod removes the speed penalty from all bags. 



[h3]FAQ[/h3]

[b]Is this mod safe to add/remove mid-game?[/b]

Yes.

[b]Is the guy in the preview image naked?[/b]

No, he's wearing underpants and hiking boots as well as half a dozen bags.








Workshop ID: 3457038023
Mod ID: NepTemplate

Workshop ID: 3457038023
Mod ID: NepTemplate

publishedfiledetails.title: Clean HotBar [B42]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]Compatible with B42 up to Build 42.17 (SP/MP)[/h2]
Clean Hot Bar redesigns Project Zomboid’s hotbar and hand-equipped UI to make item status easier to read, less intrusive, and more visually intuitive. It improves visibility during gameplay while keeping the interface compact, clear, and customizable.

[h2]B42 Status Display Improvements[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/vSq7e8H.png[/img]
This mod visually integrates the new weapon and item status systems introduced in B42:
[list]
[*][b]Weapon Head Condition:[/b] Shows a status bar above the slot when a weapon has head condition data.
[*][b]Weapon Sharpness:[/b] Displays both current and maximum sharpness values.
[list]
[*]White: Current sharpness
[*]Brown: Maximum sharpness (see [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3461263912/600775381612528270]here[/url])
[/list]
[*][b]Toggleable Items:[/b] Clear ON/OFF indicators for items such as flashlights.
[*][b]Ammo Counter:[/b] Displays the current ammo count above firearm slots.
[/list]

[h2]Hand-Equipped UI Improvements[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/qdn3lSz.png[/img]
The hand-equipped UI has also been improved to show more detailed item information in a cleaner way.  
Original textures have been refined as well, with added transparency for a smoother and less obstructive appearance.

[h2]B42 Liquid System Integration[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/Hx8z1gT.png[/img]
The new B42 liquid system is represented with color-coded bars, making it easier to identify liquid contents at a glance.  
Some colors were initially generated with AI assistance, so feedback is welcome if you notice anything that looks inaccurate.

[h2]Animation Effects[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/KMJ1EZb.gif[/img]
The visual effects are designed to be noticeable without becoming distracting:
[list]
[*][b]Durability Shake:[/b] Items shake when durability decreases, such as battery drain.
[*][b]Flashing Effect:[/b] Durability values flash when going down.
[*][b]Liquid Drain Animation:[/b] Smooth visual feedback when liquids are consumed.
[*][b]Weapon Warning:[/b] A warning appears above the character’s head when the equipped weapon loses durability. This can be disabled in the settings panel.
[/list]

[h2]Customizable Settings[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/tr3AbW6.png[/img]
[list]
[*]Disable status displays for a more hardcore experience.
[*]Adjust the height of status bars.
[*]Enable [b]tooltips[/b] (default: off) to show item details on hover.
[*]Scale the size of the hotbar.
[*]Toggle the weapon durability warning animation above the character.
[/list]

[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[list]
[*]Compatible with [b]Reorder the Hotbar[/b]. For better integration, its hotbar button is moved into the settings panel.
[*]For best results, place Clean Hot Bar near the bottom of your mod load order so other mods load first.
[*]There may be conflicts with other mods that also add status indicators to the hotbar.
[*]If the mod does not work as expected, try moving it to the bottom of the load order.
[/list]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/pEPGwZV.png[/img]

[h2]Handheld Equipment Slot[/h2]
Basic status support is currently implemented for handheld equipment. More improvements may be added in future updates.

[hr]

publishedfiledetails.title: Neat Crafting [B42]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]Build Support: B42.9 to B42.17
(Build 42 only, SP / MP Host tested on recent builds)[/h2]
[b]Neat Crafting[/b] replaces the default Crafting menu with a faster, cleaner, and more compact UI for Build 42.

It improves recipe browsing with clearer icons, smarter categories, cleaner input/output panels, and more performance-friendly list handling, so you can find what you want and craft it with less clutter.

[b]Dependency:[/b] [b]NeatUI Framework[/b] must be enabled and placed before [b]Neat Crafting[/b] in your mod load order.

[h2]Optional addon: XP & Mod Display[/h2]
If you want recipe XP gain and source mod information to be displayed, try this addon:
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3540503606][b]Neat Crafting & Neat Building – XP & Mod Display[/b][/url]

[h2]Build 42 only[/h2]
If the mod does not appear in your mod list, you are probably running Build 41.
This mod requires [b]Build 42[/b].

[b]Tested on:[/b] multiple Build 42 versions including [b]42.17.0 SP / MP Host[/b].

[h2]⚠️ Multiplayer / Saves Note[/h2]
In Build 42 multiplayer, some mods may register [b]WorldDictionary[/b] keys. If a world was saved with certain mods enabled and they are later removed, that save may fail to load.

[b]Rule of thumb:[/b]
[list]
[*] If a world has used [b]Neat Crafting[/b] or [b]Neat Building[/b], avoid removing them from that save or server
[*] Always make a full backup before changing mod lists on MP
[/list]

[hr]
[h2]Better Layout[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/wLbT9k9.png[/img]
Neat Crafting redesigns the default crafting panel to make it easier to read and faster to use.

The goal is not to overload the screen, but to make the important information easier to spot.

Main improvements:
[list]
[*] More visual and recognizable icons
[*] Less unnecessary clutter
[*] Better use of tooltips instead of overloading the layout with text
[/list]

[h2]Better Categorization[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/7cwgO6O.png[/img]
The category list stays compact to save screen space, while detailed category names appear on hover.

This keeps navigation fast without losing clarity.

If you are a modder, you can also add your own category icons in [b]media/ui/CategoryIcon[/b].

A base PSD file is included there for convenience.

[h2]Better Input Panel[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/ViQqEoO.png[/img]
The input panel is simplified so the important information stands out more clearly:
[list]
[*] Only two key icons remain: [b]kept[/b] and [b]partially consumed[/b]
[*] Fully consumed items do not add extra visual noise
[*] Kept items are always shown first
[/list]

[h2]Performance Improvements[/h2]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/ieAnMMg.png[/img]
The crafting panel logic has been reworked so it updates only when needed, and long recipe lists are handled more efficiently.

This helps reduce unnecessary UI work and can improve FPS, especially with large modded recipe lists.

Feedback is especially welcome from players on lower-end PCs.

[h2]Gamepad Support[/h2]
Gamepad support is not planned in the short term.

The current focus is finishing the UI redesign work first.

Controller support may be explored later, but for now this mod is mainly designed for mouse / keyboard play.

[h2]Mod Compatibility[/h2]
[b]Compatible:[/b]
[list]
[*] Craft and Build Category Translate [B42]
[*] The Shortcut
[/list]

[b]Known incompatible:[/b]
[list]
[*] what can i craft
[*] what can i craft addon
[/list]

Other mods that directly modify the crafting panel usually will not affect the Neat Crafting panel.

[h2]Load Order Notes[/h2]
[list]
[*] Place [b]NeatUI Framework[/b] before [b]Neat Crafting[/b]
[*] If another mod also changes the crafting UI, load order may affect which panel is shown
[/list]

[h2]Credits:[/h2]
[list]
[*] Original mod by [b]Rocco[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199485575276/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]PZ Workshop Items[/url]
[*] Maintenance / compatibility updates for newer B42 builds by [b]Afyrmo[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199133259679/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]PZ Workshop Items[/url]
[/list]

You are free to extract and use any content from this mod without asking for permission.
If you want to republish this mod, you need to follow these rules:
1. There must be substantial and important improvements.
2. If this mod has not been updated for a long time and has caused many issues, you may modify and republish it.[/quote]

Mod ID: Neat_Crafting

publishedfiledetails.title: Mod Load Order Sorter [b42]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3180893708]Mod version for build 41 of Project Zomboid[/url]

This mod changes the logic of the automatic sorting of mods added in build 42 of ProjectZomboid.
Now the automatic sorting supports sorting rules, as well as highlights all found violations of the mod loading order.

[h3]How to use:[/h3]
All you need to do is enable this mod and apply the changes. Then activate all the mods you want, go to the mod load order and click the "Sort" button at the bottom of the screen. Apply the changes.
Done!
If necessary, you can manually change the mod load order or set your own rules using the sorting rules editor.

[h3]Warning:[/h3]
Any change in the order of loading mods in the middle of the game can lead to loss of the current save. Do so at your own risk.

[h3]Features:[/h3]
[list]
[*]Automatic sorting:
 The list of Active mods is pre-sorted alphabetically and also grouped in the following order:
[olist]
[*]Framework/API/Tweak mods,
[*]Resources/Textures,
[*]Maps/Locations,
[*]Vehicles,
[*]Code-only mods,
[*]Clothing,
[*]Interface,
[*]Other mods,
[*]Localization
[/olist]
 Subsequent application of the [b]topological sorting algorithm[/b] can detect and correct incorrect order of mods according to the requirements of each mod.
[*]Support for [b]Sorting Rules[/b]. Sorting Rules Editor
[*]Highlighting mods with detected violations
[*][b]Search[/b] by mod name or mod ID
[*]Support for [b]multiple file selection[/b] to manually edit the order of loading mods.
[*]When running the game in debug mode, additional information about the mod on which the mouse cursor is hovered will be displayed.
[/list]

[h3]Disclamer[/h3]
This mod is not a cure for all conflicts in the order of loading mods. Some conflicts can only be resolved manually.

[h3]Questions and Answers:[/h3]
[b]Q:[/b] What should I do if a mod doesn't appear in the mod list?
[b]A:[/b]
1) Please make sure you are subscribing to the correct version of the mod: In the main menu of the game at the bottom is written the version of the game. This version of the mod is only for version b42. For version 41 of the game use the mod of the corresponding version https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3180893708
2) If you already have some mods enabled and this mod doesn't appear in the list, try disabling all mods and rechecking the list (there are mods that change how mods appear in the mod list).
3) If this mod still doesn't appear, try deleting the Steam\steamapps\workshop\content\108600 folder , and then verify the integrity of the game files. WARNING: All mods you subscribed to will be redownloaded.

Workshop ID: 3423660713
Mod ID: ModLoadOrderSorter_b42

publishedfiledetails.title: Nepenthe's High Beams
publishedfiledetails.description:
[b]B42.13.0 update:[/b]  after testing this mod is still working (NOTE: tested in single player only)



[h1]Updated for 42.9.0![/h1]

>  Made vehicle headlights extend much farther.  

The latest update made headlights a lot better... but they could be even brighter!  This mod has been updated using the new functionality in B42.9.0 for the best headlights ever, without any manual java file installation.

Anyone using the main mod will have it automatically updated to version 3 if they are using Zomboid 42.9 or later (same modID, new code & name).  The 2.0 WIP version has been removed, and the Java mod is now depreciated.

[b]Bonus update:[/b] you can now toggle high beams using the radial menu (this can be disabled in mod options)

---


High Beams!  Because driving at night needs more than 8 feet of visibility.  

This mod adds high-beams you can toggle on/off that give your headlights extra distance and (optionally) light up the immediate area around the car.


[h3]Credits[/h3]
十叁 for their mod [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3425507560&searchtext=headlights]BrighterHeadlights[/url] which showed me the final missing piece to make the first version of this mod

Indie Stone for updating the headlight code to make more available via lua.

Fkekexue for the [url=https://www.veryicon.com/icons/transport/car-indicator/high-beam-2.html]radial menu icon[/url]

[h2]FAQ[/h2]

[b]How do I use this mod?[/b]

First, you need to be playing [b]B42[/b] and you need a vehicle with working headlights.  Then get in your car and toggle high-beam mode with the hotkey (set to '.' by default.) The headlight indicator will be blue when headlights are on and in high-beam mode. 

[b]What do the options do?[/b]

In Options -> Mods -> High Beams you can change a few settings:

[list]
[*]Keybind: The key to toggle high beams
[*]Always On: high beams are always on automatically
[*]Length multiplier: default length is 36 tiles, this lets you increase that distance
[*]Intensity Multiplier: allows you to blind other drivers with your aftermarket xenon bulbs
[*]Radial Menu: Adds a high beam toggle to the vehicle radial menu.
[*]Debug Mode: Prints extra info to console.
[/list]

After changing settings turn high beams off and on again for them to take effect.


[b]Does this mod work in B41?[/b]

This mod is B42 only.  It relies on new functionality added in B42.9.0 and can't be back-ported to B41.


[b]Can this mod be safely added mid-save?[/b]

Yes.


[b]Can this mod be safely removed mid-save?[/b]

Yes.


[b]Does this work with modded vehicles?[/b]

It works with every modded vehicle I've tested, and there should be no issues.  It will print a debug message if you try to use it in a vehicle without headlights.


[b]Does using high beams while the engine is off consume more battery power than normal lights?[/b]

No, because when I tried the game crashed so for now power usage is unchanged.


[b]Can I make the lighting smoother as I drive?[/b]

In Options -> Display & Performance -> Lighting Updates  set the number of lighting updates to a value higher than 15 per second.  (this technically has nothing to do with this mod, but is worth knowing since you're probably paying more attention to the lighting while looking at the effects of this mod)





Workshop ID: 3438126404
Mod ID: NepHighBeams

publishedfiledetails.title: More on the Floor
publishedfiledetails.description:
Zomboid onbly allows you to place 50 weight units of stuff on a single tile.  If you attempt to place an item that would go beyond that limit the highlight on the tile will be red instead of white, and you will ot be able to place the item.

This mod removes that restriction, allowing you to place more stuff on a single tile.  Handy for storing your overfull bags, water dispensor bottles and spare anvils as well as creating nice displays with the new 3D placement tool without worrying about weight limits.


[h3]FAQ[/h3]

[b]Which features work in multiplayer?[/b]

[table]
[tr]
[th] [/th]
[th]Drop[/th]
[th]Place[/th]
[/tr]
[tr]
[td]Single Player[/td]
[td]✅[/td]
[td]✅[/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
[td]Multi Player[/td]
[td]❌[/td]
[td]✅[/td]
[/tr]
[/table]

In SP you can [b]drop[/b] an unlimited amount of weight on one tile, and the weight limit is ignored when [b]placing[/b] items.

In MP the weight limit is ignored when [b]placing[/b]  items, but [b]dropping[/b] items uses vanilla behavior because a random bit of the dropping-stuff-in-multiplayer code is in Java for some reason.


[b]Can this mod be safely added/removed from existing games?[/b]

Yes.


[b]Is the weight of objects in Zomboid in kilograms, pounds, or arbitary units representing how awkward an item is to carry?[/b]

Yes.

Workshop ID: 3457064550
Mod ID: NepMoreOnFloor

[img]https://i.imgur.com/tZ9ZNM4.png[/img]

[h1][b]Incompatibilities[/b][/h1]
[list]
[*]Gamepad & Split Screen modes.
[*]Any mods that modify the hotbar (exception: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3461263912]Clean Hotbar[/url]).
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3397396843]Knox Event Expanded NPC[/url] (partly) — The icons overlap some of the group control buttons, but there is no direct incompatibility.
[*]And other mods that use the same areas.
[/list]

[h1][b]F.A.Q[/b][/h1]
[h3]1. The mod doesn't show up in the mod list, is this a bug?[/h3]
No, this means you have Build 41, and the mod is intended for Build 42.
[h3]2. Will Build 41 support be added?[/h3]
No.
[h3]3. Is this compatible with [...]?[/h3]
I don't know, check it yourself. And if not, report it.
[h3]4. Will [...] feature be added?[/h3]
While I consider suggestions for improvements, I prefer to work on developing and implementing ideas independently.
[h3]5. I found a bug! Read the comments![/h3]
Bug reports in the comments are ignored and/or deleted. There is a dedicated discussion for that:

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3495906499/595151277615296006/][img]https://i.postimg.cc/GhgNbmPy/bugreports.png[/img][/url]

[h1][b]Updates[/b][/h1]
If a mod isn't getting updates and you've already reported any bugs you found in the relevant discussion thread, please [b]DON'T[/b] bother me about it anywhere — Steam, Discord, wherever.

I do this for free in my spare time, and if I lose interest in the game or have stuff going on in my personal life, don't expect updates during that period — that includes replies to comments too.

[quote]
[url=http://theindiestone.com/forums/index.php/topic/2530-mod-permissions/?p=36479]On Lockdown[/url]
[img]https://i.postimg.cc/hPw6wD6Y/ezgif-5-a8c6fa520722.gif[/img]
This mod cannot be modified by anyone else, for anything else, at all, ever. Even if the user disappears, he or she would much rather you didn't touch the fruits of their hard labour.
[/quote]
[quote]
The mod is provided as-is. If something about it doesn't suit you (outside of actual bugs) and/or you disagree with the author's vision, you are free to simply not use it.
[/quote]

Pursuit Special is joined by the XB GT Coupe it was based on, they share most of the parts but also have some unique features. They are both fast and fun and will be dangerous companions in Knox. [b]Tools:[/b] Condition, Head and Sharpness
[b]Clothing:[/b] Condition and number of holes
[b]Vehicle Parts:[/b] Condition
[b]Light Bulbs:[/b] Condition
[b]Food:[/b] Hunger reduction (Calories, Carbs, Proteins and Fat with Nutritionist trait)
[b]Fluids containers:[/b] Fluid type and amount out of total capacity
[b]Usable items (Duck tape, Wire, Epoxy etc):[/b] Number of uses left out of total number of uses

[b]Note about the sort[/b]
By default it only sorts the first 50 items in a stack, but you can set the maximum number of items to sort in Mod Settings.
It is not recommended setting this to a high number if you have inventories with many stacks of hundreds of items, as the performance will take a hit when opening containers.
A value of 100 should be okay and above that, you have to try for yourself.
It depends on how big a hoarder you are :)

publishedfiledetails.title: Moodle Framework
publishedfiledetails.description:
Makes it easy to add moodles for modders.
This version may have frequent updates that we do not wanna bother B41 MP players with.


[h1]Moodle Framework Configuration[/h1]
If you want colors to be easier to distinguish, you can set to white the reference color for moodles (default on).
This applies to both vanilla and modded moodles.
You can also deactivate various displays to enhance mod compatibility.
Modded Backgrounds with small extensions right or down are now handled too.


[h1]How to create a new moodle[/h1]
If you wanna create a Moodle * then follow the next steps, replacing * by your moodle name.

[h2]1/ Moodle Texture[/h2]
Add Moodle *.png under media\ui.
Should be 30 by 30 pixels png with alpha enabled.

[h2]2/ Texture[/h2]
Add the following text under media\lua\shared\Translate\EN
[code]Moodles_EN {
    Moodles_*_Good_lvl1 = "text_1",
    Moodles_*_Good_desc_lvl1 = "text_desc_1",
    Moodles_*_Good_lvl2 = "text_2",
    Moodles_*_Good_desc_lvl2 = "text_desc_2",
    Moodles_*_Good_lvl3 = "text_3",
    Moodles_*_Good_desc_lvl3 = "text_desc_3",
    Moodles_*_Good_lvl4 = "text_4",
    Moodles_*_Good_desc_lvl4 = "text_desc_4",
    Moodles_*_Bad_lvl1 = "text_1",
    Moodles_*_Bad_desc_lvl1 = "text_desc_1",
    Moodles_*_Bad_lvl2 = "text_2",
    Moodles_*_Bad_desc_lvl2 = "text_desc_2",
    Moodles_*_Bad_lvl3 = "text_3",
    Moodles_*_Bad_desc_lvl3 = "text_desc_3",
    Moodles_*_Bad_lvl4 = "text_4",
    Moodles_*_Bad_desc_lvl4 = "text_desc_4",
}[/code]
You can implement only a subset of this as long as you call only the implemented part.

[h2]3/ Moodle object creation[/h2]
Add these lines in your mod:
[code]require "MF_ISMoodle"
MF.createMoodle(*);[/code]

[h2]4/ Moodle value updates[/h2]
Access your moodle from your code with
[code]require "MF_ISMoodle"
MF.getMoodle(*,playerNum):setValue(myNewMoodleValue)--float[/code]
0.0 <= myNewMoodleValue <=1.0
Missing playerNum will make the mod incompatible with SplitScreen.

[h2]For tuning options [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2859296947/3380536561724844838/]see dedicated discussion[/url][/h2]


[h1]Limitations[/h1]
[list]
[*]Not tested with multiplayer. (it is expected to work)
[*]Not tested with splitscreen. (it is expected to work since last version)
[*]Oscillations are not based on vanilla value and the behaviour may depend on performances.
[/list]


[h1]Credits where it is due[/h1]
[list]
[*]This is inspired from the (removed) mod [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2665369300]Zombie Camouflage[/url] moodle management by the awesome [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/Dislaik/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]Dislaik[/url].
[/list]


[h1]Known mods using this[/h1]
[list]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1299328280]More Traits[/url]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2459400130]Dynamic Traits and Expanded Moodles[/url]

publishedfiledetails.title: [B41 & B42] Rope Towing
publishedfiledetails.description:
To tow a car, you need equipment, right?
You can't just have a car and instantly have the ability to tow another one.
So, with this mod installed, you'll need to have a rope or a chain (both vaniilla and from The Workshop) in your inventory.

[b]Some players have reported having issues regarding the rope disapearing after detaching it from a vehicle. I'm aware of his issue, I've spend a lot of time on it but I can't replicate it nor understand why it happens. Quitting and rejoining seems to have been working for some, if it doesn't, I unfortunately don't have any other workaround.
Although, if you encounter this issue and want to help out, you can comment in the dedicated discussion and provide the build you are using, any vehicle or inventory mods you're using, the vehicle the rope was attached to and whether or not you logged off between the moment you attached the vehicle and the moment you dettached it.[/b]

[h2] FAQ [/h2]

[h3]Does this mod affect towing behavior?[/h3]
[i]No, it just prevents you from attaching vehicles without a rope.[/i]

[h3]Is there a visible rope attached to vehicles?[/h3]
[i]No[/i]

[h3]Do I need The Workshop mod for this mod to work?[/h3]
[i]No, it's compatible but not required[/i]

Please upvote the mod if you like it and check out my other mods!

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3389093252][B42] - Hand Bag Window Climb[/url] - Keep you bag in your hands when climbing through windows instead of dropping them.
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3391902125][B41 & B42] - Throw your bag across[/url] - Adds an option to throw your bag across windows for efficient looting.

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3262467691][B41 & B42] -  Close The Trunk[/url] - Close your trunk or stuff might fall from it.
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3321118178][B41] - Give Item[/url] - A quick way to give items to players around you without dropping them on the ground.
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3266074882][B41] - Cignition[/url] - Handling gas and smoking might not be the best idea after all.

publishedfiledetails.title: Yes' Improved Stealth v2 [B42/B41]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Now for B42[/h1]
If you don't see the light moodle in your game and you had enabled the mod before then It needs to be enabled again.

This is a complete rework of the stealth in this game. Making it more consistent [u]under the right conditions[/u].
I got tired of waiting for b42 so I came back to this game.

[h1]Recommended to be played with the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3102364952]XP on Backstab Mod[/url][/h1]
[h1]NOW COMPATIBLE WITH [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3008448748]THE CRAWL MOD[/url][/h1]
[h1]NOW COMPATIBLE WITH [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3268487204]BANDITS NPC MOD[/url][/h1] I think.

[h1]Darkness Matters.[/h1]

For this mod to start doing things it requires two things from you. First, you have to be sneaking. Second, you need to be in a dark enough tile, standing in the fog will work too. This means that the mod does not depend for it to be night at all, you can turn off the lights of a warehouse and you'll be covered by the shadows. But it also works the other way around, if you stand under a streetlamp long enough at night the zombies will have an easier time spotting you.

[h1]There is now a moodle displaying this light info[/h1]

This moodle will display the lightning information for you. You can turn it off in the sandbox settings. Thanks to Moodle Framework!

[h1]Your sneaking level matters.[/h1]

Each level you have in sneak will decrease the radius at which you can be spotted. This makes it so characters that spend trait points in sneaking will have a head start in stealth that will be noticeable. Remember, you NEED to be in the darkness (or fog) for this mod to do anything at all. If you stand in the middle of the street in broad day light, zombies will be able to see you and the game's default detection system will apply.

[h1]Your traits matter.[/h1]

Inconspicuous will decrease said radius by a small amount, while Conspicuous will increase it.

[h1]Your choice of clothing matters.[/h1]

Wearing dark clothing will give you a small bonus in your stealth radius. However, I made it so it is less intrusive. If you want this bonus, wearing a black T-Shirt underneath or even a black bandana will be enough, you don't need to change your entire fashion at all. Although, wearing a High vis vest will give you a huge penalty 100% of the time. If this mechanic isn't to your liking, you can always disable it at the sandbox options. I made this for the people like me who like to look sneaky while playing sneaky characters.

[h1]There is now a Camouflage system.[/h1]

Wearing clothes with similar colors to your environment will give you another bonus. Wearing grey or black in urban areas or wearing green in every other area with vegetation like a forest will active this bonus. Ghillie Tops, Military Green Camo Shirts, Military Urban Camo shirts and Green Camo jackets will also have this behavior. Please be aware that only clothing with uniform patterns will have this effect, any piece of clothing that has something (like a picture, badge, literally anything) on it will not be able to carry this Camo bonus.

[h1]You can set the backstab to be completely silent.[/h1]

This feature is turned off by default. You can enable it in the sandbox options.

[h1]This mod is CUSTOMIZABLE.[/h1]

You can edit the value of all this bonuses in the sandbox options. You also have the option if you want the [b]backstab[/b] to be completely silent and you can disable the clothing bonus.

[h1]Credit to TChernobill for Moodle Framework![/h1]
[h1]And remember, stick to the shadows survivor.[/h1]
[h1]This mod is 100% compatible with The Long Night mod.[/h1]
[h1]This mod should work with Night Vision Goggles now[/h1]
[h1]This mod should work with Throwables now[/h1]
This was tested with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2842179206&searchtext=night+vision]Night Vision Chucked[/url] and [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2460154811&searchtext=brita]Brita's Armor Pack[/url]. Please let me know if there is an issue or if you would like to see another mod being compatible.

For build 42 it was tested with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3303590197&searchtext=fred+night+vision]Fred's Night Vision Goggles[/url]

publishedfiledetails.title: More Damaged Objects [42MP]
publishedfiledetails.description:
More damaged objects added.

[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/carlesturo/Images/refs/heads/main/poster4.png[/img]

[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/carlesturo/Images/refs/heads/main/list_more_damaged_objects_pack6.png[/img]
[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/carlesturo/Images/refs/heads/main/Standpipe.gif[/img]

[b]It is safe to add or remove items mid-game.[/b] However, if you delete the mod, newly damaged objects will be replaced by their original versions but will not be visible. For example, a cardboard box that has been crushed will still exist in its original form, but it will be invisible.

[h3]Future plans:[/h3][list][*]I plan to add chairs as damaged objects, along with other tiles. If you want a specific sprite to become a damaged object, feel free to request it.[/list]

[h3]Fixed:[/h3][list][*]Floor lamps are now collision free in vanilla, but they don't have depthmap, my mod adds it.[/list]
[list][*]Water dispensers are now collision-free for players but still have collision for vehicles and include a depthmap.[/list]
[list][*]Vehicle lifts are now collision-free for players but still have collision for vehicles; the collision is only on the column rather than the entire tile, and the depthmap is vanilla.[/list]
[list][*]You can now place objects inside the bathtub and on top of the shower tray and the toilet.[/list]
[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/carlesturo/Images/refs/heads/main/Fixes_List.png[/img]
[list][*]All missing depthmap have been added to the vanilla tiles of "damaged_objects_01", "street_decoration_01", and "street_roadsigns_01". This is an example of three tiles with and without depthmap.[/list]
[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/carlesturo/Images/refs/heads/main/Fixes_List2.png[/img]

[h3]Thanks to [b]albion[/b]’s mod, [b]Starlit Library[/b], I was able to achieve the desired effect with the streetlights. Also, thanks to [b]albion[/b], I managed to make the water jet stop when the water gets shut off.[/h3]

[h3]Thanks to [b]PePePePePeil[/b], my code is now much better, and thanks to [b]Sir Doggy Jvla[/b] for sharing his delay function.[/h3]

Workshop ID: 3413150945
Mod ID: MoreDamagedObjects

publishedfiledetails.title: Rebalanced Prop Moving
publishedfiledetails.description:
Do I really need a hammer to move a couch?
I wish I could move this desk, but I haven't taken any carpentry classes!

This mod removes the nails holding your furniture and appliances to the floor!

Details:
- Furniture and appliances require no skill/tool to move.
- Fixtures like cabinets and shelves still require skills/tools.
- Break chance for props requiring skills/tools has been reduced to 30% at skill level 0, 20% at 1, 10% at 2, and 0% for >=3.
- Break chance for props not requiring skills/tools is now 0%.
- Sinks in vanilla have no associated skill and receive a 25% break chance default. Changed to 0% since apparently no skill is required!
- All the above only applies to "pickup" and "place" actions. Disassembly should not be affected by this mod.

The mod works by "whitelisting" props if their name matches certain patterns. This implies that the patterns may catch too much or too little, so feel free to provide feedback if you think some props should or should not require a skill/tool.

Note: Not compatible with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2685057281]Safer Prop Moving[/url]

Disclaimer: There could be big bugs in this mod! The vanilla lua code has numerous edge cases, please comment if you discover issues.

Should be compatible with 42.0 - 42.16


Workshop ID: 2699828474
Mod ID: RebalancedPropMoving

publishedfiledetails.title: Mini Health Panel [B41/B42.13+]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h2]✅ The mod is working fine on [u]B42.15.1[/u] (unstable multiplayer) ✅[/h2]
[h1]🔄️ [u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3622692490]B42.12 version here[/url][/u] (before unstable multiplayer update) 🔄️[/h1]

[hr]

A minimalistic wounds display without the need to open the health panel. Can be disappeared when no wound need to be treated.

[img]https://i.ibb.co/vZ2Lghv/gif-mod.gif[/img]

[h1]Updated for Build 42 [/h1]

The add-on has been [b][u]updated for Build 42[/u][/b] while a separate version for [b][u]Build 41 remains untouched and fully compatible[/u][/b], thanks to a new files structure system where each version of the mod is only visible by its respective game build, ensuring seamless functionality for players on both versions.

[b][u]Only for B42[/u][/b], It now includes a new muscle strain indicator for each body part. The indicator evolves by becoming less transparent and changes color from orange to red as muscle strain worsens.

If auto-hide is enabled, the silhouette is revealed with a brief blinking effect when a strain threshold is reached.

This feature can be toggled off if not needed.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/BnjPXPr.gif[/img]

[h1]What does it do?[/h1]
It give a permanent overview of your character's wounds on a minimalistic display. The body silhouette disappear when there are nothing to treat and appear when there is a wound.

When a body part is flashing red, there is a wound that needs to be treated.
When it's white, the wound is either bandaged or splinted.
When it's orange, the bandage is dirty and needs to be replaced.

If the wound is bandaged but it's still flashing that means you didn't fully treated the wound (glass still lodged, deep wound needs to be stitched, etc...)

[h1]How to use it?[/h1]
Right clicking on the component will bring a contextual menu with all wounds and the settings of the addon.

Left clicking just brings up the classic health panel of the base game.

You also can drag it around and place it where you desire.

[h1]Optional mod[/h1]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2169435993]Mod Options[/url] is optional and adds an option to disable my mod (mainly for public servers)

[h1]Compatibility[/h1]
This mod should work with any other mod (no incompatibility has been reported so far)
This mod works in single and multi-player.
This mod doesn't work in split-screen and won't be.

[h1]Known issue[/h1]
[list]
[*]The panel may look small or big depending of your game resolution, this is unfortunately a common problem for most of interface add-ons.
[list]
[*] This may be looked on when I will have the time.
[/list]
[/list]

[h1]Support me[/h1]
If you really like my mod you can support me through [b][u][url=https://ko-fi.com/speedy_von_gofast]Ko-fi[/url][/u][/b]

Workshop ID: 2866258937
Mod ID: MiniHealthPanel

publishedfiledetails.title: Map Legend UI [B42\41]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1]Works with Build 42 (including 42.16 MP build)[/h1]

Playing build 41, but [i]still[/i] don't remember what all the building colors stand for?

Or, perhaps, like me, you think that the vanilla build 42 map key looks a bit... [i]off[/i]?

Suffer no longer.
This mod adds a legend from old town maps to in-game world map.


[h1]Compatibility[/h1]
[list]
[*]Works with all stable builds (41.65 or newer), [b]including build 42![/b]
[*]Might work with newer experimental builds, but no guarantee
[*]Compatible with Multiplayer

[list]

	[*][url=steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2815560151]Share Annotations[/url]
	[*]Any other mod, theoretically.
[/list]
[/list]

[h1]NEW IN VERSION 2.0:[/h1]
[list]
[*]The legend window is now an actual UI element
[*]Created a standalone button to close and open the legend window.
[*]Added support for translations
[list]
	[*]Currently supported languages are listed in the pinned discussion
	[*]Legend window size adjusts automatically depending on your language setting
	[*]Adjusted text alignment (padding) when using languages with non-standard fonts
	[*]Want to submit a translation? Post it in the pinned discussion!
[/list]
[/list]

[h1]FAQ[/h1]
[b]Q: "Can I use this mod in my modpack?"[/b]
A: Yes, as long as you credit the original owner of the mod (me)
[b]Q: "Can this mod be used in an existing world\save?"[/b]
A: It should, but back up your save file before enabling the mod just in case. No guarantees here.

publishedfiledetails.title: '82 Jeep J10

Time to chase some tornados or not! J10 is considered a fantastic workhorse and it can take a punch, or 79, depends on how much armor you slap on it. It comes with many features to expand it, some will require you to choose, some will be available at the same time. It also comes with optional Mellow Yellow variant you can enable the spawns for or keep them off and use it as a reward/special vehicle. It's part of my 80s Pickups set that has compatible tires, armor, seats, roofracks and some other parts. perfect for traveling in groups small or big.

[*]Many texture variations

publishedfiledetails.title: that DAMN Library
publishedfiledetails.description:
[b]What is this?[/b]
This is a framework that fuels all KI5 mods, one centralized place to fine tune and expand all mods and make them ready for game changes and new features. 

You can find more about it here for B41: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3262959206]that DAMN Library - Crafting, Fixing and Recycling explained[/url], B42 guide is coming soon.

[b]Why do i need this?[/b]
It is a required mod for running KI5 mods, expanding them with crafting and recycling features for almost all parts, handy tire repair kit, plastic welder and many other things. All of those features are already available to most mods already released, try them out.

[b]Why are the comments closed?[/b]
There is no need for them here, all mod related stuff can be discussed on mod pages of those mods. That way i know what mod could be impacted.

[b]Can I use this for my mods?[/b]
Not yet, the contents of this library will be changing in the next weeks without any warnings so it's better to wait while we fine tune the things and give a green light for wide use.

[b]I found a bug, what now?[/b]
Hit the discussions of affected mods and give us detailed report of what happened.



[b]If you like the mod, you can buy us a coffee[/b],

publishedfiledetails.title: Named skill VHS tapes
publishedfiledetails.description:
Renames all VHS tapes to include the skill they level up.

This removes the need to consult the wiki or Steam guides when searching for relevant VHS tapes.

The mod only modifies the translation for the specific VHS items for the specified languages. Because of this, it is [b]completely safe[/b] to add and remove this mod during a playthrough.

I am the only person maintaining this mod and, although I do not speak all the supported languages, I have made every effort to translate the relevant VHS tapes accurately, in line with Project Zomboids localization.

Compatible with B41 and B42.15

Enjoy!

[h2]If you enjoy my mod and would like to support future development and maintenance, please consider supporting me on Ko-fi! Thank you[/h2]

publishedfiledetails.title: [B42/41] Reorder Containers - Backpack Orders
publishedfiledetails.description:
[b]Reorder Containers[/b] is an inventory mod that allows you to customize the order in which containers and backpacks appear in the inventory.

Simply drag and drop the container icons to rearrange them to your liking.
They'll even remember their preferred order if you drop the backpack and re-equip it later.


You also have the option to rearrange containers in the loot window, [b]but you need to turn it on using the options at the bottom of the loot window (next to the lock)[/b]

Lastly, you can manually set the sorting priority for containers if you need to. Just select the container and click the options icon at the bottom right of the window (next to the lock)

The drag and drop system will automatically handle the sorting priority by default.

publishedfiledetails.title: [B41/B42] Doorway Curtains
publishedfiledetails.description:
[h1] Doorway Curtains [/h1]

This mod allows you to place walk-through curtains on doorways, making it easy to pass through without needing to open them. Adds a practical, immersive detail to your base.

To craft curtains, you'll need the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3067798182]Building Menu[/url] mod, which enables curtain crafting. Otherwise, curtains must be collected from the game world.

[h1] Important Note: [/h1]
If using the Building Menu, load it [b]before[/b] this mod to be sure curtains are placed correctly on doorframes.

[b] Key Features: [/b]
[list]
[*] Walk-Through Curtains: Curtains you can walk through without opening.
[*] Dynamic Placement Validity: Curtains snap to doorframes and window frames and can only be placed in valid positions, avoiding interference with other objects. Also curtains are snapping to the doorframes, just like to the window frames.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h1] Frequently Asked Question: [/h1]

[h2] Is it compatible with Multiplayer? [/h2]
 Absolutely! Extensively tested and fully functional on MP servers.

[h2] Does it work on existing saves? [/h2]
 Absolutely! Enable it mid-save without issues.

[h2] Does it work for all game versions? [/h2]
 Only tested and working on the latest game version (v41.78.16).

publishedfiledetails.title: Vehicle Repair Overhaul
publishedfiledetails.description:
[img]https://i.imgur.com/8CRHL5v.png[/img]

[b]B41 and B42 COMPATIBLE[/b]

	[hr]						[/hr]

Find a clunker you love but its one zombie tap from disintegration? Fix it up!

[b]Repair and Salvage - Vanilla Vehicles and all the great Workshop Vehicle Creations!![/b]

Adds many more repair options for Hoods, Trunks, Seats, Doors, Gas Tanks, Lights, Windshields, Windows, Tires, Brakes, Suspension, Glove box, Roof Racks, Trailers, Cargo Containers, Armoured Parts & other Vehicle Accessories or Salvage the whole vehicle too removing it from your path!

[b]New Items included[/b]

Fix A Flat - Tire Repair - This will get you by until you switch out the tires - Can be found in Vehicle Trunks.**You can combine cans that you find**

	[hr]						[/hr]
[h2]This mod is safe to add or remove from your existing saves[/h2]

	[hr]						[/hr]
[h1] Repair & Salvage [/h1]

The aim of this mod is to let you repair and fix up your vehicle(s)
[b]and[/b] will bring more focus and choices for the mechanics and metal welders amongst us

 [b]-works in MP and SP (and can be installed or removed anytime)[/b]
 -with a shortage of working cars on some servers you can now fix up what you find
 -have a mechanic in your group? put their skills to use
 -collect cars and restore them to their former glory
 -learn your craft and progress, become that master mechanic you always wanted to be
 -grab your blowtorch and break down many vehicle parts into useable items
 -salvage the complete vehicle for many useful items depending on your skill
 -[b]compatible with all vehicle additions on the workshop [/b]
 -built-in support for Valhalla Vehicle Ownership module

	[hr]						[/hr]
[h1]Repair your ride[/h1]

Use varying parts to restore your ride
 -start easy changing tires then with seats, have some ductape, sheets, glue, a spring? you can repair it!
 -Parts have a lot more uses now, from metal bars and springs to rebuild the suspension, light bulbs for light, fabrics for seats and good old ductape for many minor fixes
 -Screws and metal for moderate fixes but progress to using a blow torch and your repairs will be solid!

	[hr]						[/hr]
[h1]Salvage of Vehicle and Parts[/h1]

Salvage parts from one vehicle and use the pieces to repair other parts on other vehicles

Grab your blow torch and mask to break down vehicle parts into useable items!

Salvaging Vehicle parts could provide you with several items in return!
Tear up those old stinky seats and you'll have a chance of gaining those dirty Rags, Leather, Denim or Thread!
Grab your blowtorch and mask, uninstall those beat-up doors and salvage them for a chance of Metal Sheets, Screws, Small Metal Sheets or even Electronical Scrap!
Enjoying those mods that introduce Roof Racks? Well now you can salvage those too!
Each Vehicle part can be salvaged with a chance of gaining up to three salvaged items depending on your Mechanic and Welding skills.

[b]Salvage Vehicle[/b] is now included

Right click on a vehicle and choose "Salvage Vehicle"

This option is available once you have Metal Working skill 4 or above, Mechanics skill 3 or above and requires 10 propane units and a Welders Mask. You can salvage all vehicles except Wrecks*.
This option will completely salvage and remove the vehicle clearing the path.
Depending on your Metal Working skill, you will salvage the vehicle faster and receive many useful items the higher your skill.
I do recommend removing as many parts of the vehicle as you can and empty the vehicle before salvage of the vehicle body. This will give you vehicle parts to use, further salvage options for those parts and your character will have gained more knowledge about vehicles and gained more skill XP in Mechanics and Metal Working.

The Vanilla option for *dismantle wreck option is also still available (only for wrecked vehicles), takes less time but your returns are smaller and less varied with less XP too.

The better you are at Mechanics or Metal Welding will mean your talents will produce better results from the Salvage!
A Mechanic knows the parts he wants, gaining skills in Metal Welding means he can better acquire those parts.

	[hr]						[/hr]
[h1]Suggested workshop items that add to the experience[/h1]

[b]Many Vehicle mods on the workshop are already supported with this mod ! [/b]
The list below are some of my favs

[URL=https://steamcommunity.com/id/KI5/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]KI5's Vehicles[/URL]
[URL=https://steamcommunity.com/id/bls13/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]Cyt Vehicles[/URL]
[URL=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198004947199/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]Sharks Vehicles[/URL]
[URL=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2719592131]Tread's Water Tank Trucks[/URL]
[URL=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1539281445]Crashed Cars[/URL]
[URL=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2787291513]Craft Helper Continued[/URL]
[URL=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2712632417]Viperels Vehicle Spare Parts[/URL]

	[hr]						[/hr]
[h1]Compatibility[/h1]

Good with everything!! I do not overwrite vanilla code, I create additions so it plays nice with any other mod you want to use.
**Place this mod after any vehicle mods or towards the bottom of your mod order for best results**

	[hr]						[/hr]
[h1]NOTES[/h1]

[strike]Heater repair and Lightbar Repair and Salvage will come eventually to this mod. It requires a different method of coding compared to the regular vehicle repairs and unfortunately I just haven't had the time to incorporate it.[/strike]

For now I suggest the "Easy Heater Repair" and "Easy Lightbar Repair" mods. (B41)

The B42 version now has updated versions of [url=steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2602450182]Easy Heater Repair[/url] and [url=steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2582648054]Easy Lightbar Repair[/url] integrated into it. Credit to [url=steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198037641428/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]PipMagnet[/url] for the original mods. If they return and want them removed, I will do so. - SP4RTAN

[h2]Note for server admins[/h2]

Due to the (sometimes) high frequency of updates to this mod, modpacking is allowed so that server admins can apply updates as needed and reduce server downtime.

All that we ask is that you credit us somewhere.

	[hr]						[/hr]
[h1]Contributing on GitHub[/h1]

If you have a translation or want to help add repair options to new vehicles and you know how Project Zomboid scripts work, you can make a pull request on [url=https://github.com/ThisIsSP4RTAN/PZ-VehicleRepairOverhaul]GitHub[/url]

publishedfiledetails.title: Antibodies (v1.97) [B41 + B42]
publishedfiledetails.description:
The Antibodies mod enhances the vanilla zombification mechanic by introducing an antibodies curve inspired by real-life immune system responses. If the antibodies successfully counteract the infection, the character will experience a gradual recovery over time.

[hr][/hr]
[h2]How it Works[/h2]

As you get infected, the Knox Virus in your body spreads. Alongside, your body slowly mounts an immunological response.

The antibodies output rate is tied to infection progression (peaks at 50% infection), your condition, wounds, infections and hygiene.

Your job is to sustain all positive effects, and reduce all negative effects (see diagnose panel) - so that antibodies can overtake the infection and eradicate the virus.

Playing as character with medical background actually makes sense now, since it allows you to diagnose yourself and others, and also provide significant bonuses while treating wounds.

By default, sandbox settings are rather forgiving. The easiest way to tweak that is by changing "Base Antibodies Growth" property in General section.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]FAQ:[/h1]

[b]Will this mod work if I install it after my character got infected?[/b]
Yes, unless you are past certain point. Infection length is randomized, but it takes between 2-3 days to resolve (either way).

[b]I've been nursing my character back to health for several days now, and he hasn't died, but it still says he's bitten. Should it be taking this long?[/b]

Most likely you are confusing a wound and the infection. Wound will stay with you for a while, even if you survive the virus - but it will eventually heal.

[b]Does this mod work in multiplayer mode?[/b]
Yes, including local co-op.

[b]Susceptible Mod compatibility?[/b]
Yes. Investigated by @The Animeville Horror. Thanks again.

[b]Does antibiotics pill found in the game help at all?[/b]
In real life antibiotics don't do anything against viruses. It will help by healing bacterial infections, however it won't do anything against Knox Virus.

[b]How can I make sure this mod works?[/b]
Create a character with medical background (with +2 First Aid) - this will add diagnose button in your health panel.
This is also the best way to tailor sandbox settings to your needs.

[b]How can I make this mod harder or adjust the difficulty?[/b]
The key setting is [i]Base Antibodies Growth[/i], which controls how quickly your character develops immunity. Here's what different values mean (assuming no other effects):
[list]
    [*][b]180 (default)[/b] - You overcome the infection at [b]40% progression[/b].
    [*][b]140[/b] - You overcome it at [b]66% progression[/b].
    [*][b]138[/b] - You overcome it at [b]70% progression[/b].
    [*][b]135[/b] - [b]You die[/b]. The infection progresses faster than your body can fight it.
[/list]

Every in-game effect modifies this baseline. You can adjust difficulty by:
[list]
    [*]Lowering [i]Base Antibodies Growth[/i] to shrink your margin for error.
    [*]Adding harsher penalties (e.g., from injuries, exhaustion, etc.).
    [*]Combining both for a tougher experience.
[/list]

[b]Personal note:[/b]
I think the vanilla Knox Infection kind of sidesteps some (even core) mechanics in the game. For example:
[list]
    [*][i]First Aid[/i] feels almost irrelevant without human enemies like bandits or raiders.
    [*][i]Tailoring[/i] also doesn't offer enough incentive to grind it to meaningful levels.
    [*][i]Hygiene/Washing[/i] completely meaningless, even though you can plumb your washing machine, after water gets shut off.
[/list]

Personally, I find [b]managing the infection[/b] more engaging than just hiding in a base for a few days - especially in multiplayer.
Don't worry, you will get cocky at some point, and you will get stranded without necessary supplies to survive the infection. It's just a matter of time.
But hey, don't let me tell you how to play your game.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Special Thanks:[/h1]

[b]MrTerreII[/b] for providing ES (Spanish) translation.
[b]VVentos[/b] for providing TR (Turkish) translation.
[b]llerafel[/b] for providing RU (Russian) translation.
[b]Rookavite[/b] for providing DE (German) translation.
[b]geuks[/b] for providing FR (French) translation.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Translations:[/h1]

I’m always happy to include more translations.
You can contribute here - and if you don’t know how to use Git, just drop me a message:
https://github.com/lonegamedev/pz_mod_antibodies

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Want to say thanks?[/h1]

If you enjoy the mod and want to support future updates, you can do so here:
[url=https://www.buymeacoffee.com/lonegamedev]Buy Me a Coffee[/url]

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Known conflicts:[/h1]

[b]zRe Vaccine ReMod by kERHUS[/b] - this mod packs older version of Antibodies, and will break the current version. If you want new features please ask author of that mod to update theirs. You shouldn't try to run both.

The same goes for any other mod that includes previous version of Antibodies.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Update Schedule:[/h1]

I have constrained myself to update only Tue-Thu. All potential problems with the update should be resolved in this time window.

I do however reserve a right to fix critical issues ASAP (for example when game update breaks the mod).


Workshop ID: 2392676812
Mod ID: lgd_antibodies

publishedfiledetails.title: KillCount
publishedfiledetails.description:
Details kills by weapon category and by weapon in a new dedicated tab of character window.
[Solo]Adds to the genuine character panel the number of Z that died of other things than player weapon.

[h1]B42.12 Limitations[/h1]
[list]
[*]Car kills are now partially counted by vanilla. This can create counters discrepencies.
[*]Ask your questions on [url=https://discord.gg/theindiestone]Discord mod_support channel[/url].
[/list]

[h1]Technical details[/h1]
[list]
[*]Total number of kills can be exported (if selected in sandbox options) to %username%/Zomboid/lua/killcount.txt (windows path)
[*][Solo only] Counts fire kills in addition to vanilla weapon kills on the genuine character sheet.
[*][Solo & Multiplayer] Details kills by weapon category and by weapon in a new dedicated tab of character window.
[*][Multiplayer] You can visualise the kills of other players, with a delay (configurable in sandbox options).
[*][Multiplayer] You can visualise the total number of kills on the server (same delay).
[*][Multiplayer] You can deactivate the sharing of dead characters from sandbox options (to reduce GlobalModData size on never-wiping servers).
[*][Multiplayer] You can deactivate the sharing of kill count from sandbox options (to remove GlobalModData use).
[*]Visible at death time in the PostDeathUI text (can be removed in sandbox options).
[*]Handles character specific counters (saved with mod data, so server must be stopped properly for the values to be saved).
[*]Works only for one local player. i.e. not for multiscreen.
[*]Ask your questions on [url=https://discord.gg/theindiestone]Discord mod_support channel[/url].
[/list]

Credits where it is due: this is inspired from [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2451245497]Additional Kill Counter[/url]

Workshop ID: 2553809727
Mod ID: KillCount

publishedfiledetails.title: Auto Tailoring
publishedfiledetails.description:
Automatic Tailoring Training.
Easy hole reparation and patch.
Hole detection and missing patch detection.


So you are here ?
Ah.. you probably wanna protect yourself but tailoring would destroy your fingers and brain.
You've come to the right place.


[h1]Automatic Tailoring Training[/h1]
Click the Train Tailoring button !
[list]
[*]You only have to click one button.(*)
[*]Inventory management is done automatically, tailoring ressources can be in equipped bags.
[*]Real time speed if optimized to the minimum (solo player only for speed increase).
[*]Once ressources are depleted, game is frozen (solo player only).
[*]You still have to spend the genuine in-game time (vanilla friendly).
[/list]


[h1]Easy hole detection, reparation and patch[/h1]
Click the red cross to repair !
[list]
[*]All is accessible from the Character Protection Page.
[*]Tooltip tells you where the hole is.
[*]Tooltip tells you where the patch is missing.
[*]Upper red cross allows to repair all in one click.(*)
[*]Red crosses make it easy to see where the holes are and allow selective repair.
[*]Yellow bubbles make it easy to see where the patches are missing are and allow selective patching (leather only).
[*]Adds bullet protection.
[*]Real time speed is optimized to the minimum (solo player only for speed increase).
[*]You still have to spend the genuine in-game time (vanilla friendly).
[*]If you use [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2859296947]MoodleFramework[/url], you have a temporary moodle (15s) that helps you track holes.
[/list]

(*)You still have to loot the tailoring ressources: a needle, lots of threads, infinite amounts or ripped sheets.


[h1]FAQ[/h1]
[list]
[*]With B42.13, vanilla tailoring actions on non-worn clothing will fail and spawn errors on the server. Train Tailoring also uses those action so it must only be used on worn clothes.
[*]Can be added to [/ remove from] any save, any time.
[*]Works for B42 and further versions.
[*]Does not work for B41 and prior versions.
[*]If you still have questions, or problems, discuss on [url=https://discord.gg/theindiestone]Official PZ Discord: mod_support channel[/url].
[/list]


[quote]
[url=http://theindiestone.com/forums/index.php/topic/2530-mod-permissions/?p=36479]On Lockdown[/url]
This mod cannot be modified by anyone else, for anything else, at all, ever. Even if the user disappears, he or she would much rather you didn't touch the fruits of their hard labour.
[/quote]

Workshop ID: 3388183573
Mod ID: AutoTailoring

publishedfiledetails.title: Equipment UI - Paper Doll Equipment Interface [B42/41]
publishedfiledetails.description:
[img]https://i.imgur.com/w5yeFNP.png[/img]

[h1]Equipment UI[/h1]
Equipment UI is an addition to Project Zomboids interface that adds a paper doll (STALKER / tArKov inspired) equipment panel for managing and viewing your character's equipment.

[i][b]Use mod options to hide equipped items from your inventory.[/b][/i]

[h3][i]Part 2 - NOW AVAILABLE ?[/i][/h3]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2982070344][img]https://i.imgur.com/agDCBaI.png[/img][/url]


[h3]Drag and Drop[/h3]
Drag and Drop items to and from the equipment ui to easily adjust your equipment!
Equipment slots will highlight and change color to visualize conflicting equipment.

[h3]Customize Your Layout[/h3]
The equipment window can be detached and positioned anywhere. It will follow and collapse with the inventory when it is attached. The layout is saved between sessions.

[h3]Hotkey[/h3]
Includes a customizable control for opening/closing the Equipment UI. The keyboard control can be adjusted in the game's regular control options. The controller binding can be changed via Mod Options.
The keyboard defaults to comma.
The controller defaults to SELECT (or LS/L3 if using [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2922618524&searchtext=wookie]Wookiee Gamepad Support[/url])


[h3]Mod Options[/h3]
Install mod options to adjust the mod to your liking!
Hide equipped items from your inventory and change the scale of the Equipment UI!

[img]https://i.imgur.com/31mCw6B.gif[/img]

[h1]FAQ[/h1]
[h3]Does this work in multiplayer?[/h3]
Yes.

[h3]Does this work with modded items?[/h3]
Yes. I've accounted for many non-vanilla body slots, anything not accounted for will neatly display at the bottom of the equipment ui before the hotbar slots.

[h3][i]Part 2 - Click ?[/i][/h3]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2982070344][img]https://i.imgur.com/agDCBaI.png[/img][/url]


[h3]If you enjoy my mods, consider supporting me on Ko-fi![/h3]

publishedfiledetails.title: Wake Them Up
publishedfiledetails.description:
Spawn building zombies fast optimizing performances costs.
Problem description in [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UOQaZ0PflZs]that video[/url].
Ducks' Zombie Building Spawn Fix correction explained in [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=foe78LkXOzU]this video[/url].
This mod is a replacement to Ducks' Zombie Building Spawn Fix.
It adds the same improvement but with less drawbacks, see details below.

[h1]Technical stuff[/h1]
[list]
[*]does one room per cycle and suspends once the cell is fully woken up.
[*]continues when the size increases (when new rooms are loaded). The trick is: the size never decreases.
[*]as a primary consequence: the peak cost is only one room's cost. (instead of many as it occures a lot that many pop in one cycle) => that's what reduces / removes the stutters.
[*]as a consequence: each room is only requested once. (instead of constantly)
[*]as a consequence: long time server runs bigroom list is not a problem as the room list is never parsed.
[*]Mimic Vanilla behaviour to avoid spawning Zombies in the player's spawning building.
[*]Mimic Vanilla behaviour to avoid spawning Zombies in specific 'empty' rooms.
[/list]

[h1]Remaining drawback[/h1]
[list]
[*]Spawning zombies faster than vanilla will increase the CPU load. So you have to choose between that CPU cost increase and the risk of last second zombie spawn as described in the video.
[/list]

[h1]Non technical stuff[/h1]
[list]
[*]Credits to [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/iwishforducks]Ducks[/url] for the initial solution: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2961467351]Ducks' Zombie Building Spawn Fix[/url].
[*]Picture comes from [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=foe78LkXOzU]this video[/url]'s miniature. Credits to the author for pointing out the problem.
[/list]


[quote]
[url=http://theindiestone.com/forums/index.php/topic/2530-mod-permissions/?p=36479]On Lockdown[/url]
This mod cannot be modified by anyone else, for anything else, at all, ever. Even if the user disappears, he or she would much rather you didn't touch the fruits of their hard labour.
[/quote]

If you wanna support, kind words are very welcome.
If you don't have any, you can still give constructive criticism.

Workshop ID: 2972287975
Mod ID: WakeThemUp
